function isWithinRoot(candidatePath, requireCanonical = false) {
  const canonicalRoot = fs.realpathSync.native(path.resolve(root));
  const normalizedCandidate = canonicalizeExistingPrefix(candidatePath);
  if (!isWithinBase(canonicalRoot, normalizedCandidate)) return false;
  if (!requireCanonical && !fs.existsSync(path.resolve(candidatePath))) return true;
  const candidate = canonicalizePath(candidatePath);
  return isWithinBase(canonicalRoot, candidate);
}

function assertPathWithinRoot(candidatePath, label, requireCanonical = false) {
  if (!isWithinRoot(candidatePath, requireCanonical)) {
    throw new Error(`${label} must stay within workspace root ${root}: ${candidatePath}`);
  }
}

function ensureCanonicalDir(dirPath) {
  assertPathWithinRoot(dirPath, 'code graph DB directory');
  fs.mkdirSync(path.resolve(dirPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
  assertPathWithinRoot(dirPath, 'code graph DB directory', true);
  return canonicalizePath(dirPath);
}

function writeState(payload) {
  fs.mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
  fs.writeFileSync(stateFile, `${JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)}\n`);
}

function resolvedDbDir() {
  const candidate = process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR ?? dbDir;
  assertPathWithinRoot(candidate, 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR');
  if (fs.existsSync(candidate)) assertPathWithinRoot(candidate, 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR', true);
  return canonicalizePath(candidate);
}

function leasePath() {
  return path.join(resolvedDbDir(), PID_FILE_NAME);
}

function ownerLeasePath() {
  return path.join(resolvedDbDir(), OWNER_LEASE_FILE_NAME);
}

function legacyLeasePaths() {
  // After the 2026-05-29 relocation, skill-local (mcp_server/database/) is the PRIMARY path.
  // The legacy probe now covers the former shared `.spec-kit` location plus the pre-rename
  // `skill/` (singular) typo dir, so a launcher still holding an old-location lease is detected.
  return [
    path.join(opencodeDir, '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database', PID_FILE_NAME),
    path.join(opencodeDir, 'skill', 'system-code-graph', 'mcp_server', 'database', PID_FILE_NAME),
  ].map(canonicalizePath);
}

function readLeaseFile(filePath = leasePath()) {
  try {
    const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
    if (typeof parsed.pid === 'number') return parsed;
  } catch {
    // Missing or corrupt lease files are treated as no active lease.
  }
  return null;
}

function readOwnerLeaseFile(filePath = ownerLeasePath()) {
  try {
    const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
    if (
      Number.isInteger(parsed.ownerPid) &&
      Number.isInteger(parsed.ppid) &&
      typeof parsed.executablePath === 'string' &&
      typeof parsed.startedAtIso === 'string' &&
      typeof parsed.lastHeartbeatIso === 'string' &&
      Number.isInteger(parsed.ttlMs) &&
      typeof parsed.canonicalDbDir === 'string'
    ) {
      return parsed;
    }
  } catch {
    // Missing or corrupt owner leases are treated as no active owner.
  }
  return null;
}

function writeOwnerLeaseFile(lease) {
  const currentLeasePath = path.join(ensureCanonicalDir(path.dirname(ownerLeasePath())), OWNER_LEASE_FILE_NAME);
  const tmp = `${currentLeasePath}.tmp.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
  fs.writeFileSync(tmp, `${JSON.stringify(lease, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600, flag: 'wx' });
  fs.renameSync(tmp, currentLeasePath);
}

function writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive(lease) {
  const currentLeasePath = path.join(ensureCanonicalDir(path.dirname(ownerLeasePath())), OWNER_LEASE_FILE_NAME);
  let fd;
  try {
    fd = fs.openSync(currentLeasePath, 'wx', 0o600);
    fs.writeFileSync(fd, `${JSON.stringify(lease, null, 2)}\n`, 'utf8');
    fs.fsyncSync(fd);
    return true;
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.code === 'EEXIST') return false;
    throw error;
  } finally {
    if (typeof fd === 'number') fs.closeSync(fd);
  }
}

// The socket path this owner's environment computes. Recording it in the
// leases lets a secondary under a divergent SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR bridge
// to the REAL socket instead of probing a recomputed wrong one and
// misreporting a healthy owner as no-bridge-socket.
function ownerSocketPath() {
  try {
    const { getIpcSocketPath } = loadBridgeModule();
    if (typeof getIpcSocketPath !== 'function') return null;
    return getIpcSocketPath('mk-code-index', { dbDir: resolvedDbDir() }) ?? null;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

let ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer = null;

function clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat() {
  if (ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer) {
    clearInterval(ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer);
    ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer = null;
  }
}

// Without a periodic refresh, every healthy long-running owner classifies
// as stale-heartbeat-reclaim about two minutes after spawn, so each later
// secondary start rewrites the owner lease before rediscovering the live
// PID lease — churn plus reclaim-race exposure for nothing.
function startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat(ownerPid) {
  clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat();
  const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
  const ttlMs = Number.isFinite(lease?.ttlMs) && lease.ttlMs > 0 ? lease.ttlMs : 60000;
  const intervalMs = Math.max(1000, Math.floor(ttlMs / 2));
  ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer = setInterval(() => {
    if (refreshOwnerLeaseFile(ownerPid)) return;
    // A failed refresh means a concurrent reclaim superseded this owner;
    // this launcher exits with its child rather than supervising relaunches,
    // so stop heartbeating instead of fighting the new owner.
    log('owner lease heartbeat refresh failed (superseded); stopping heartbeat');
    clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat();
  }, intervalMs);
  ownerLeaseHeartbeatTimer.unref?.();
}

function buildOwnerLease(ownerPid = process.pid) {
  return {
    ownerPid,
    ppid: process.ppid,
    executablePath: process.execPath,
    startedAtIso: new Date().toISOString(),
    lastHeartbeatIso: new Date().toISOString(),
    ttlMs: 60000,
    canonicalDbDir: resolvedDbDir(),
    socketPath: ownerSocketPath(),
  };
}

function processLiveness(pid) {
  if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) return 'dead';
  try {
    process.kill(pid, 0);
    return 'alive';
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.code === 'ESRCH') return 'dead';
    if (error.code === 'EPERM') return 'unknown-eperm';
    return 'alive';
  }
}

function readParentPid(pid) {
  if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) return null;
  if (process.platform === 'linux') {
    try {
      const status = fs.readFileSync(`/proc/${pid}/status`, 'utf8');
      const match = status.match(/^PPid:\s+(\d+)$/m);
      return match ? Number.parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;
    } catch {
      return null;
    }
  }
  const result = spawnSync('ps', ['-o', 'ppid=', '-p', String(pid)], {
    encoding: 'utf8',
    stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
  });
  if (result.status !== 0 || !result.stdout) return null;
  const parsed = Number.parseInt(result.stdout.trim(), 10);
  return Number.isInteger(parsed) ? parsed : null;
}

function classifyOwnerLease(lease) {
  const liveness = processLiveness(lease.ownerPid);
  if (liveness === 'dead') return 'stale-pid';
  if (liveness === 'unknown-eperm') return 'unknown-eperm';

  const actualPpid = readParentPid(lease.ownerPid);
  if (actualPpid !== null && actualPpid !== lease.ppid && actualPpid === 1) {
    return 'ppid-1-orphan';
  }

  const heartbeatMs = Date.parse(lease.lastHeartbeatIso);
  const ttlMs = Number.isFinite(lease.ttlMs) ? lease.ttlMs : 0;
  if (!Number.isFinite(heartbeatMs) || Date.now() - heartbeatMs > ttlMs * 2) {
    return 'stale-heartbeat-reclaim';
  }

  return 'live-owner';
}

function acquireOwnerLeaseFile() {
  const currentOwnerLeasePath = ownerLeasePath();
  const existing = readOwnerLeaseFile(currentOwnerLeasePath);

  if (existing) {
    const classification = classifyOwnerLease(existing);
    if (classification === 'live-owner' || classification === 'unknown-eperm') {
      return { acquired: false, holder: existing, classification };
    }
    log(`ownerLeaseReclaimed: ${classification} ownerPid=${existing.ownerPid}`);
  }

  const lease = buildOwnerLease(process.pid);
  // Reclaim a stale lease by removing it first, then claim exclusively (O_EXCL).
  // This collapses the fresh-acquire and stale-reclaim paths into a single CAS so
  // only one racer can win: the loser's exclusive create hits EEXIST and returns
  // acquired:false instead of last-writer-wins overwriting the winner's lease.
  if (existing) {
    try {
      fs.unlinkSync(currentOwnerLeasePath);
    } catch (error) {
      if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;
    }
  }
  if (!writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive(lease)) {
    const holder = readOwnerLeaseFile(currentOwnerLeasePath);
    return {
      acquired: false,
      holder: holder || lease,
      classification: holder ? classifyOwnerLease(holder) : 'live-owner',
    };
  }
  ownerLeasePid = process.pid;
  if (existing) {
    emitLeaseMetric(leaseMetricClassForTransition('staleReclaimed'), {
      ownerPid: existing.ownerPid,
      ownerClassification: classifyOwnerLease(existing),
      leaseKind: 'owner',
    });
  }
  return { acquired: true, lease, reclaimed: existing };
}

function refreshOwnerLeaseFile(ownerPid, patch = {}) {
  const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
  if (!lease || lease.ownerPid !== ownerPid) return false;
  const nextOwnerPid = patch.ownerPid ?? ownerPid;
  writeOwnerLeaseFile({
    ...lease,
    ...patch,
    lastHeartbeatIso: new Date().toISOString(),
  });
  // Re-read after the atomic write; if a concurrent reclaim superseded us between
  // the ownership check and the write, do not claim the refresh succeeded.
  const reread = readOwnerLeaseFile();
  if (!reread || reread.ownerPid !== nextOwnerPid) return false;
  ownerLeasePid = nextOwnerPid;
  return true;
}

function clearOwnerLeaseFile() {
  if (!Number.isInteger(ownerLeasePid)) return;
  try {
    // Re-confirm ownership immediately before unlink so a successor lease written
    // after the first read is not deleted by path. A residual sub-syscall window remains; fully
    // closing it would require the launcher to share owner-lease.ts's mutation lock.
    const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
    if (lease && lease.ownerPid === ownerLeasePid
        && readOwnerLeaseFile()?.ownerPid === ownerLeasePid) {
      fs.unlinkSync(ownerLeasePath());
    }
  } catch {
    // Idempotent cleanup.
  } finally {
    ownerLeasePid = null;
  }
}

function clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner(ownerPid) {
  try {
    // Re-confirm ownership immediately before unlink (see clearOwnerLeaseFile).
    const lease = readOwnerLeaseFile();
    if (lease && lease.ownerPid === ownerPid
        && readOwnerLeaseFile()?.ownerPid === ownerPid) {
      fs.unlinkSync(ownerLeasePath());
    }
  } catch {

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '860,1245p' .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
  }
  run(process.execPath, [localTscEntrypoint(), '-p', 'tsconfig.json'], { cwd: kitDir });

  const missing = requiredArtifacts().filter((artifact) => !exists(artifact));
  if (missing.length > 0) {
    throw new Error(`bootstrap finished but artifacts are still missing: ${missing.map(rel).join(', ')}`);
  }
}

const BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes — fallback for unstamped (legacy) lock dirs
const BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = 120000;
const BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_OWNER_FILE = 'owner.pid';

// Returns the pid recorded inside the lock dir, or null when no readable pid
// stamp exists (legacy lock dirs, or a holder that died before stamping).
function readBootstrapLockOwnerPid() {
  try {
    const raw = fs.readFileSync(path.join(lockDir, BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_OWNER_FILE), 'utf8').trim();
    const pid = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
    return Number.isInteger(pid) && pid > 0 ? pid : null;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

// Reclaim a stale bootstrap lockdir. A lock is reclaimable as soon as its
// recorded holder is provably dead; the mtime TTL is only a fallback for lock
// dirs with no readable pid stamp (legacy dirs) or a holder whose liveness
// cannot be determined. Without the dead-holder check, a holder killed less than
// staleMs ago wedges every requireLock respawn for the full wait deadline.
function removeStaleBootstrapLock(staleMs = BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS) {
  let ageMs;
  try {
    ageMs = Date.now() - fs.statSync(lockDir).mtimeMs;
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.code === 'ENOENT') return false;
    throw error;
  }
  const ownerPid = readBootstrapLockOwnerPid();
  const ownerDead = ownerPid !== null && processLiveness(ownerPid) === 'dead';
  if (!ownerDead && ageMs <= staleMs) {
    return false;
  }
  // Atomically CLAIM the stale lockdir via rename before deleting it. Only one
  // racer wins the rename; a successor that mkdir's a fresh lockDir after our stat
  // creates a NEW inode that our rename/rmSync cannot touch, so we never delete a
  // live successor lock. A losing racer's rename throws ENOENT and falls through to
  // the outer retry.
  const staleClaim = `${lockDir}.stale.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}`;
  try {
    fs.renameSync(lockDir, staleClaim);
    fs.rmSync(staleClaim, { recursive: true, force: true });
    const reason = ownerDead ? `dead holder pid=${ownerPid}` : `mtime ${Math.round(ageMs / 1000)}s old`;
    log(`reclaiming stale bootstrap lock ${rel(lockDir)} (${reason})`);
    return true;
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.code === 'ENOENT' || error.code === 'ENOTEMPTY') return false;
    throw error;
  }
}

async function acquireBootstrapLock(options = {}) {
  const requireLock = options.requireLock === true;
  fs.mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
  const deadline = Date.now() + (options.timeoutMs ?? BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS);
  const staleMs = options.staleMs ?? BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_STALE_MS;
  const retrySleepMs = options.retrySleepMs ?? 1000;
  while (true) {
    try {
      fs.mkdirSync(lockDir);
      // Stamp the holder pid so a later launcher can reclaim this lock the
      // instant we die, instead of waiting out the mtime TTL. Best-effort: a
      // failed stamp degrades to the TTL path, never blocks acquisition.
      try {
        fs.writeFileSync(path.join(lockDir, BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_OWNER_FILE), String(process.pid), { mode: 0o600 });
      } catch { /* TTL fallback covers an unstamped lock */ }
      return true;
    } catch (error) {
      if (error.code !== 'EEXIST') {
        throw error;
      }
      if (artifactsReady() && !requireLock) {
        return false;
      }
      if (removeStaleBootstrapLock(staleMs)) {
        continue;
      }
      if (Date.now() > deadline) {
        throw new Error(`bootstrap lock timed out at ${rel(lockDir)}`);
      }
      await sleep(retrySleepMs);
    }
  }
}

function launchServer() {
  if (launchStarted) {
    log('launchServer skipped: launch already started in this launcher process');
    return false;
  }
  launchStarted = true;
  // Set DB dir for the child process (operator-set env var wins).
  if (!process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR) {
    process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR = resolvedDbDir();
  }

  const server = path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'index.js');
  childProcess = spawn(process.execPath, [server], {
    cwd: root,
    env: buildChildEnv(),
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });

  if (typeof childProcess.pid === 'number') {
    const refreshed = refreshOwnerLeaseFile(process.pid, {
      ownerPid: childProcess.pid,
      ppid: process.pid,
      executablePath: process.execPath,
    });
    if (!refreshed) {
      log('owner lease refresh to child pid failed; launcher pid remains the recorded owner');
    }
    startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat(refreshed ? childProcess.pid : process.pid);
  }

  childProcess.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
    if (signal) {
      // Clear the lease before mirroring the signal; process.on('exit') does not fire on SIGKILL.
      clearAllLeaseFiles();
      // Remove our own handler for this signal first. Without this, the self-sent
      // catchable signal (SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP/SIGQUIT) re-enters installSignalHandlers'
      // handler, which — for an externally-killed child (childProcess.killed === false) —
      // exits 0, so a supervising runtime misreads abnormal daemon death as a clean
      // shutdown. Removing the handler lets the re-raised signal terminate this process
      // with the signaled status (128 + n). SIGKILL is uncatchable and already mirrors.
      process.removeAllListeners(signal);
      process.kill(process.pid, signal);
      return;
    }
    clearAllLeaseFiles();
    process.exit(code ?? 0);
  });

  childProcess.on('error', (error) => {
    log(error.stack || error.message);
    process.exit(1);
  });
  return true;
}

function installSignalHandlers() {
  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM', 'SIGHUP', 'SIGQUIT']) {
    process.on(signal, () => {
      if (childProcess && !childProcess.killed) {
        childProcess.once('exit', () => {
          clearLeaseFile();
          process.exit(128);
        });
        childProcess.kill(signal);
        setTimeout(() => {
          clearAllLeaseFiles();
          process.exit(128);
        }, 5000).unref();
        return;
      }
      clearAllLeaseFiles();
      process.exit(128);
    });
  }
  process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
    try {
      clearAllLeaseFiles();
    } catch {
      // Preserve default uncaughtException crash behavior.
    }
    throw err;
  });
}

async function launcherMain() {
  const started = now();
  const actions = [];
  let lockHeld = false;
  // Orphan daemon pid recorded by a reclaimed prior owner lease. A SIGKILL'd
  // launcher leaves its non-detached child reparented to init while still holding
  // the SQLite DB; reap it before spawning a successor so we never run two writers
  // against the same DB (mirrors the dead-socket respawn reap and the sibling
  // launchers' adopt-or-reap guards).
  let reclaimedOrphanPid = null;

  try {
    installSignalHandlers();
    // Lease cleanup runs unconditionally regardless of child termination path.
    process.on('exit', clearAllLeaseFiles);
    refreshPaths();
    ensureLayout(actions);
    refreshPaths();

    const strictSingleWriter = !isStrictModeDisabled(process.env.MK_CODE_INDEX_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER);
    if (strictSingleWriter) {
      const ownerLeaseResult = acquireOwnerLeaseFile();
      if (ownerLeaseResult.acquired
          && ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed
          && Number.isInteger(ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid)
          && ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid > 0
          && ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid !== process.pid) {
        reclaimedOrphanPid = ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid;
      }
      if (!ownerLeaseResult.acquired) {
        // A live owner already holds the single-writer lease. Bridge this
        // client's stdio to the owner's IPC socket so additional sessions and
        // MCP reconnects share the one daemon (matches mk-spec-memory-launcher).
        // maybeBridgeLeaseHolder falls back to a LEASE_HELD_BY diagnostic line
        // only when the socket is missing/refused or bridging is disabled.
        log(`liveOwnerDetected: ownerPid=${ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid} classification=${ownerLeaseResult.classification}`);
        await bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld({
          ownerPid: ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid,
          startedAt: ownerLeaseResult.holder.startedAtIso,
          socketPath: typeof ownerLeaseResult.holder.socketPath === 'string' ? ownerLeaseResult.holder.socketPath : null,
        }, {
          respawnChildPid: ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid,
        });
        return;
      }

      const leaseResult = isLeaseHeld();
      if (leaseResult.held && !leaseResult.staleReclaimable) {
        clearOwnerLeaseFile();
        await bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld(leaseResult);
        return;
      }
      if (leaseResult.staleReclaimable) {
        log(`staleReclaimed: true${leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : ''}`);
        emitLeaseMetric(leaseMetricClassForTransition('staleReclaimed'), {
          ownerPid: leaseResult.ownerPid,
          leaseKind: leaseResult.legacyPath ? 'legacy-pid' : 'pid',
        });
      }
    } else {
      log('MK_CODE_INDEX_STRICT_SINGLE_WRITER is disabled; skipping lease check');
    }

    lockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock();
    if (lockHeld) {
      // Auto-migrate DB from the former shared standalone location back to skill-local.
      // The former DB is preserved as a backup (copy, not move).
      // This block now runs ONLY for the single bootstrap-lock winner (lockHeld), AFTER the lock
      // is held and BEFORE the DB is opened by the spawned child (launchServer, below). Previously
      // it ran in the main IIFE before any lock, so two concurrent launchers could both pass the
      // !exists(target) guard and both copy — a lagging copier truncate-overwriting the now-LIVE
      // target DB out from under the winner's daemon (SQLite corruption). Gating on lockHeld makes
      // only one launcher migrate; the COPYFILE_EXCL + pre-copy re-check below is the belt-and-
      // suspenders guarantee that NO launcher ever overwrites an existing (possibly live) target DB.
      // Never auto-seed/migrate into an explicit SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR override
      //   (the operator chose that target); migrate only into the resolved default.
      // Never copy a former DB that a live legacy owner could still be
      //   writing — probe BOTH the former PID lease and the former owner lease for liveness first.
      //   The former owner lease (.code-graph-owner.json) is intentionally NOT copied forward.
      const formerSharedDbDir = path.join(opencodeDir, '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database');
      const migrationTarget = resolvedDbDir();
      if (!process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR
          && !exists(path.join(migrationTarget, 'code-graph.sqlite'))
          && exists(path.join(formerSharedDbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'))
          && !formerLocationOwnerLive(formerSharedDbDir)) {
        fs.mkdirSync(migrationTarget, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
        const dbFiles = [
          'code-graph.sqlite',
          'code-graph.sqlite-shm',
          'code-graph.sqlite-wal',
          '.code-graph-readiness.json',
          '.mk-code-index-launcher.json',
        ];
        // Re-check the sqlite target immediately before copying so a launcher that
        // raced past the outer guard (e.g. a concurrent migrator that just finished) does not
        // clobber the freshly-migrated DB. EEXIST from COPYFILE_EXCL means another launcher
        // already migrated that file -> treat the target as authoritative and stop migrating.
        let migrated = false;
        if (!exists(path.join(migrationTarget, 'code-graph.sqlite'))) {
          try {
            for (const file of dbFiles) {
              const src = path.join(formerSharedDbDir, file);
              const dst = path.join(migrationTarget, file);
              if (exists(src)) {
                // COPYFILE_EXCL makes the copy fail with EEXIST rather than truncate-
                // overwrite an existing (possibly live) target file.
                fs.copyFileSync(src, dst, fs.constants.COPYFILE_EXCL);
              }
            }
            migrated = true;
          } catch (copyError) {
            if (copyError.code === 'EEXIST') {
              // Another launcher already migrated into this target; the existing target
              // is authoritative. Do NOT overwrite it.
              log('migration skipped: target already present (EEXIST); treating existing target as authoritative');
            } else {
              throw copyError;
            }
          }
        }
        if (migrated) {
          process.stderr.write(
            `[mk-code-index-launcher] migrated DB from ${rel(formerSharedDbDir)} to ${rel(migrationTarget)} (former location preserved)\n`
          );
        }
      }

      buildIfNeeded(actions);
      log(`ready: ${JSON.stringify({ start: started, end: now(), actions, server: rel(path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'index.js')) })}`);
    }

    // Reap the orphan daemon recorded by the reclaimed prior owner lease before
    // spawning a successor. The bootstrap lock above serializes this so only the
    // winning launcher reaps + respawns; a still-live orphan would otherwise keep
    // writing the SQLite DB alongside the new daemon.
    if (Number.isInteger(reclaimedOrphanPid) && reclaimedOrphanPid > 0) {
      const reapResult = await reapOwnerBeforeRespawn(reclaimedOrphanPid);
      if (!reapResult.allowed) {
        log(`reclaimed orphan daemon pid ${reclaimedOrphanPid} could not be reaped (${reapResult.reason}); not spawning a second daemon`);
        process.exit(0);
      }
    }

    writeLeaseFile();
    const reprobe = readLeaseFile();
    if (!reprobe || reprobe.pid !== process.pid) {
      const startedAt = reprobe?.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
      process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${reprobe ? reprobe.pid : 'unknown'} startedAt=${startedAt}\n`);
      process.exit(0);
    }
    launchServer();
  } catch (error) {
    log(`failed: ${JSON.stringify({ start: started, end: now(), actions, error: error.message })}`);
    log(error.stack || error.message);
    process.exit(1);
  } finally {
    if (lockHeld) {
      fs.rmSync(lockDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    }
  }
}

// Run only when invoked directly as the launcher script; stay inert (just export) when required by a
// test, so the reconnecting-proxy wiring above can be unit-checked without spawning the daemon.
if (require.main === module) {
  bootstrapLauncherEnv();
  void launcherMain();
}

function configureLauncherPathsForTesting(nextPaths) {
  if (nextPaths.skillsDir) skillsDir = nextPaths.skillsDir;
  if (nextPaths.kitDir) kitDir = nextPaths.kitDir;
  if (nextPaths.dbDir) dbDir = nextPaths.dbDir;
  if (nextPaths.lockDir) lockDir = nextPaths.lockDir;
  if (nextPaths.stateFile) stateFile = nextPaths.stateFile;
}

module.exports = {
  CODE_INDEX_REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES,
  CODE_INDEX_UNSAFE_TOOL_NAMES,
  LEASE_METRIC_TRANSITION_CLASS,
  classifyCodeIndexFrame,
  bridgeStdioThroughSessionProxy,
  MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV,
  resolveMaintainerModeCategories,
  configureLeaseMetricSinkForTesting,
  emitLeaseMetric,
  leaseMetricClassForTransition,
  leaseMetricCounterName,
  acquireBootstrapLock,
  acquireOwnerLeaseFile,
  artifactsReady,
  clearOwnerLeaseFile,
  ownerLeasePath,
  readOwnerLeaseFile,
  reapOwnerBeforeRespawn,
  removeStaleBootstrapLock,
  readBootstrapLockOwnerPid,
  configureLauncherPathsForTesting,
};

   147	  return socket;
   148	}
   149	
   150	function probeDaemon(socketPath, options = {}) {
   151	  const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
   152	  const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
   153	  // deepProbe=true REQUIRES a JSON-RPC initialize reply to call the daemon alive (detects a hung daemon
   154	  // that accepts the connection but never responds). Without it, a bare socket 'connect' counts as alive
   155	  // (connect-ok) — only safe for callers that genuinely need connection-liveness, NOT for the reap/bridge
   156	  // decision (which always passes deepProbe:true). See maybeBridgeLeaseHolder.
   157	  const deepProbe = options.deepProbe === true;
   158	  const id = nextProbeId++;
   159	  const request = `${JSON.stringify({
   160	    jsonrpc: '2.0',
   161	    id,
   162	    method: 'initialize',
   163	    params: {
   164	      protocolVersion: JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
   165	      capabilities: {},
   166	      clientInfo: { name: 'liveness-probe', version: '0' },
   167	    },
   168	  })}\n`;
   169	
   170	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   171	    let socket;
   172	    let settled = false;
   173	    let buffer = '';
   174	    let timer;
   175	    // Hold a multibyte char split across chunk boundaries until complete, so a probe reply
   176	    // carrying CJK/emoji (e.g. serverInfo strings) is not corrupted to U+FFFD mid-parse.
   177	    const decoder = new StringDecoder('utf8');
   178	
   179	    const finish = (status, reason) => {
   180	      if (settled) return;
   181	      settled = true;
   182	      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
   183	      if (socket) socket.destroy();
   184	      resolve({ status, reason });
   185	    };
   186	
   187	    try {
   188	      socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
   189	    } catch (error) {
   190	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
   191	      return;
   192	    }
   193	
   194	    timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
   195	    timer.unref?.();
   196	
   197	    socket.once('connect', () => {
   198	      if (!deepProbe) {
   199	        finish('alive', 'connect-ok');
   200	        return;
   201	      }
   202	      socket.write(request);
   203	    });
   204	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   205	      buffer += Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? decoder.write(chunk) : String(chunk ?? '');
   206	      let newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
   207	      while (newlineIndex !== -1) {
   208	        const line = buffer.slice(0, newlineIndex).trim();
   209	        buffer = buffer.slice(newlineIndex + 1);
   210	        newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
   211	        if (!line) continue;
   212	        try {
   213	          const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
   214	          if (
   215	            parsed &&
   216	            parsed.jsonrpc === '2.0' &&
   217	            parsed.id === id &&
   218	            (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'result') ||
   219	              Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'error'))
   220	          ) {
   221	            finish('alive', 'json-rpc-reply');
   222	            return;
   223	          }
   224	        } catch {
   225	          // Ignore malformed or unrelated frames; the bounded probe decides liveness.
   226	        }
   227	      }
   228	    });
   229	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   230	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
   231	    });
   232	    socket.once('close', () => {
   233	      finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
   234	    });
   235	  });
   236	}
   237	
   238	function parseHttpJsonResponse(buffer) {
   239	  const raw = buffer.toString('utf8');
   240	  const headerEnd = raw.indexOf('\r\n\r\n');
   241	  if (headerEnd === -1) return null;
   242	  const statusLine = raw.slice(0, raw.indexOf('\r\n'));
   243	  const statusMatch = statusLine.match(/^HTTP\/\d(?:\.\d)?\s+(\d+)/);
   244	  if (!statusMatch) return null;
   245	  const statusCode = Number.parseInt(statusMatch[1], 10);
   246	  const bodyRaw = raw.slice(headerEnd + 4).trim();
   247	  let body = null;
   248	  try {
   249	    body = bodyRaw ? JSON.parse(bodyRaw) : null;
   250	  } catch {
   251	    return { statusCode, body: null };
   252	  }
   253	  return { statusCode, body };
   254	}
   255	
   256	function probeModelServer(socketPath, options = {}) {
   257	  const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
   258	  const loadingMaxMs = Number.isFinite(options.loadingMaxMs) && options.loadingMaxMs > 0
   259	    ? options.loadingMaxMs
   260	    : parsePositiveInteger(
   261	      (options.env ?? process.env).SPECKIT_HF_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
   262	      DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
   263	    );
   264	  const nowMs = typeof options.nowMs === 'function' ? options.nowMs : () => Date.now();
   265	  const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
   266	  // Mark this as the launcher's internal liveness probe so a cold-state demand listener can answer
   267	  // without spawning a model server. Genuine embed consumers do not send this header, so their
   268	  // wake-on-demand path is unchanged.
   269	  const request = `GET ${MODEL_SERVER_HEALTH_PATH} HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nAccept: application/json\r\nX-Speckit-Probe: liveness\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n`;
   270	
   271	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   272	    let socket;
   273	    let settled = false;
   274	    let chunks = [];
   275	    let timer;
   276	
   277	    // Carry the parsed health body so callers (the idle-eviction tick) can read
   278	    // lastSuccessfulEmbedAt / inFlight. The 'alive' branches below pass parsed.body as the 3rd arg.
   279	    const finish = (status, reason, health) => {
   280	      if (settled) return;
   281	      settled = true;
   282	      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
   283	      if (socket) socket.destroy();
   284	      resolve({ status, reason, health });
   285	    };
   286	
   287	    try {
   288	      socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
   289	    } catch (error) {
   290	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
   291	      return;
   292	    }
   293	
   294	    timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
   295	    timer.unref?.();
   296	
   297	    socket.once('connect', () => {
   298	      socket.write(request);
   299	    });
   300	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   301	      chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(String(chunk ?? '')));
   302	    });
   303	    socket.once('end', () => {
   304	      const parsed = parseHttpJsonResponse(Buffer.concat(chunks));
   305	      const state = parsed && parsed.body && typeof parsed.body === 'object'
   306	        ? parsed.body.state
   307	        : null;
   308	      if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'ready') {
   309	        finish('alive', `health-${state}`, parsed.body);
   310	        return;
   311	      }
   312	      if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'loading') {
   313	        const loadStartedAt = parsed.body.loadStartedAt;
   314	        const loadProgressAt = parsed.body.loadProgressAt;
   315	        // The loading budget is per model-load attempt; device fallback re-stamps loadProgressAt.
   316	        const loadingMarker = Number.isFinite(loadProgressAt) && loadProgressAt > 0
   317	          ? loadProgressAt
   318	          : loadStartedAt;
   319	        if (Number.isFinite(loadingMarker) && loadingMarker > 0 && nowMs() - loadingMarker > loadingMaxMs) {
   320	          finish('dead', 'loading-wedged');
   321	          return;
   322	        }
   323	        finish('alive', 'health-loading', parsed.body);
   324	        return;
   325	      }
   326	      finish('dead', state === 'error' ? 'health-error' : 'health-not-ready');
   327	    });
   328	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   329	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
   330	    });
   331	    socket.once('close', () => {
   332	      if (!settled && chunks.length === 0) finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
   333	    });
   334	  });
   335	}
   336	
   337	// Run the deep liveness probe up to `attempts` times; any 'alive' short-circuits to a bridge, and
   338	// only an all-failures run returns the final (dead) result so the caller respawns. The probe fn and
   339	// sleep are injectable so the retry decision is unit-testable without real sockets or timers.
   340	async function probeLeaseHolderWithRetries(socketPath, options = {}) {
   341	  const {
   342	    probe = probeDaemon,
   343	    firstTimeoutMs,
   344	    retryTimeoutMs = resolveLeaseProbeRetryTimeoutMs(),
   345	    retryBackoffMs = resolveLeaseProbeRetryBackoffMs(),
   346	    attempts = resolveLeaseProbeAttempts(),
   347	    connect,
   348	    sleepFn = (ms) => new Promise((resolve) => { setTimeout(resolve, ms); }),
   349	    onRetry,
   350	  } = options;
   351	  const totalAttempts = Math.max(1, attempts);
   352	  let result = { status: 'dead', reason: 'no-probe-attempted' };
   353	  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= totalAttempts; attempt += 1) {
   354	    const timeoutMs = attempt === 1 ? firstTimeoutMs : retryTimeoutMs;
   355	    result = await probe(socketPath, { timeoutMs, connect, deepProbe: true });
   356	    if (result.status === 'alive') return result;
   357	    if (attempt < totalAttempts) {
   358	      if (typeof onRetry === 'function') onRetry(attempt, totalAttempts, result);
   359	      await sleepFn(retryBackoffMs);
   360	    }
   361	  }
   362	  return result;
   363	}
   364	
   365	async function maybeBridgeLeaseHolder(options) {
   366	  const {
   367	    serviceName,
   368	    leaseResult,
   369	    loggerPrefix,
   370	    dbDir,
   371	    legacyReport,
   372	    probeTimeoutMs,
   373	    connect,
   374	    bridge,
   375	  } = options;
   376	  const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
   377	  const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
   378	  const ownerPid = leaseResult.ownerPid;
   379	  const writeLeaseHeld = (suffix = '') => {
   380	    process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);
   381	  };
   382	
   383	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED === '1') {
   384	    if (legacyReport) {
   385	      legacyReport(leaseResult);
   386	      return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
   387	    }
   388	    writeLeaseHeld();
   389	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
   390	  }
   391	
   392	  // Prefer the path the lease owner actually recorded over recomputing one from env. Under a
   393	  // divergent SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR (e.g. a secondary launcher in a different worktree env), the
   394	  // recomputed path can miss the live socket and false-report 'no-bridge-socket'. Only trust the
   395	  // stored path when it still exists on disk; tcp:// endpoints bypass the existence check. Fall back
   396	  // to recomputation for legacy leases that predate socketPath and for the other launchers whose
   397	  // leases never carry it.
   398	  const storedSocketPath = leaseResult.socketPath;
   399	  const usableStoredSocketPath = typeof storedSocketPath === 'string'
   400	    && storedSocketPath.length > 0
   401	    && (storedSocketPath.startsWith('tcp://') || fs.existsSync(storedSocketPath))
   402	    ? storedSocketPath
   403	    : null;
   404	  const socketPath = usableStoredSocketPath ?? getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, { dbDir });
   405	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://') && !fs.existsSync(socketPath)) {
   406	    writeLeaseHeld(' (no-bridge-socket)');
   407	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'no-bridge-socket', socketPath };
   408	  }
   409	
   410	  // deepProbe: REQUIRE a JSON-RPC reply, not just a socket accept. The reap/bridge decision must
   411	  // detect a HUNG daemon (event loop wedged / deadlocked — it still accepts the UDS connection but
   412	  // never services requests). A connect-ok-only probe would bridge a client to the wedged daemon and
   413	  // hang it forever, never respawning. The raised probe timeout (default 5000ms, < the 7000ms launcher
   414	  // grace) already prevents false-reaping a busy-but-responsive daemon mid-FTS-merge, so deep probing
   415	  // does not regress that.
   416	  // Require N consecutive failures (not one transient miss) before reaping a sibling's owner.
   417	  const probeAttempts = resolveLeaseProbeAttempts();
   418	  const probe = await probeLeaseHolderWithRetries(socketPath, {
   419	    firstTimeoutMs: probeTimeoutMs,
   420	    attempts: probeAttempts,
   421	    connect,
   422	    onRetry: (attempt, total, result) => process.stderr.write(
   423	      `[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} probe ${attempt}/${total} not alive (${result.reason}); retrying\n`,
   424	    ),
   425	  });
   426	  if (probe.status !== 'alive') {
   427	    process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath} failed ${probeAttempts} consecutive liveness probes: ${probe.reason}\n`);
   428	    return { action: 'respawn', reason: probe.reason, socketPath };
   429	  }
   430	
   431	  process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] bridging to lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath}\n`);
   432	  const bridgeToSocket = bridge ?? bridgeStdioToSocket;
   433	  // Await so an injected reconnecting bridge (whose start() resolves after the first
   434	  // attach) is fully wired before returning. The raw bridge returns a socket, not a
   435	  // promise, so Promise.resolve keeps the original fire-and-forget timing for it.
   436	  await Promise.resolve(bridgeToSocket(socketPath, {
   437	    onError: () => {
   438	      writeLeaseHeld(' (bridge-refused)');
   439	    },
   440	  }));
   441	  return { action: 'bridge', socketPath };
   442	}
   443	
   444	module.exports = {
   445	  DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
   446	  bridgeStdioToSocket,
   447	  getIpcSocketPath,
   448	  maybeBridgeLeaseHolder,
   449	  probeDaemon,
   450	  probeLeaseHolderWithRetries,
   451	  probeModelServer,
   452	  resolveLeaseProbeAttempts,
   453	  resolveLeaseProbeRetryBackoffMs,
   454	  resolveLeaseProbeRetryTimeoutMs,
   455	  toConnectionOptions,
   456	};

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:3:description: "Unix-socket IPC bridge and idle-timeout helpers for secondary mk-code-index clients and launcher-managed daemon access."
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:6:  - "mk-code-index socket"
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:20:`lib/ipc/` owns the local IPC socket server used by the launcher bridge. It creates a workspace-contained Unix socket or explicit TCP listener, accepts bounded secondary clients and connects each client to a fresh MCP `Server` over `StdioServerTransport`.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:24:- `socket-server.ts` owns secondary-client socket bridging.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:26:- Socket paths default to the code-graph database directory and can be overridden with `SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR`.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:38:  -> startIpcSocketServer({ createServer })
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:47:index.ts -> lib/ipc/socket-server.ts
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:48:socket-server.ts -> ../utils/workspace-path.ts
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:49:socket-server.ts -> @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:59:+-- socket-server.ts          # Multi-client socket bridge and stats
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:70:| `socket-server.ts` | Resolves socket paths, starts the socket server, tracks active secondary clients and exposes bridge stats. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:78:| Workspace safety | Non-TCP socket directories must stay within the workspace root. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:79:| Socket cleanup | Existing sockets are unlinked only when they are real sockets owned by the current user. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:82:| Transport | Each secondary socket gets its own `StdioServerTransport` and MCP server instance. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:83:| Database | This folder does not own SQLite state. It may use the DB directory only as a safe default socket location. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:91:| `resolveIpcSocketPath` | Function | Resolves the socket path from a DB directory and optional environment override. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:93:| `getIpcBridgeStats` | Function | Reports current socket path, secondary client count and message counters. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md:121:| [../../tests/lib/README.md](../../tests/lib/README.md) | Focused tests covering socket hardening. |

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     3	// MODULE: mk-code-index MCP Server Entrypoint
     4	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     5	/**
     6	 * mk-code-index MCP server entry point.
     7	 *
     8	 * ## Environment Variables
     9	 *
    10	 * - `MK_CODE_INDEX_ROOT_DIR` — Workspace root directory used for readiness
    11	 *   snapshot evaluation and the marker JSON body. The marker file path is
    12	 *   resolved from the configured code-graph database directory.
    13	 */
    14	import { Server } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js';
    15	import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
    16	import { CallToolRequestSchema, ListToolsRequestSchema } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js';
    17	import * as codeGraphTools from './tools/index.js';
    18	import { CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS } from './tool-schemas.js';
    19	import { writeCodeGraphReadinessMarker } from './lib/readiness-marker.js';
    20	import { closeDbWithAssertion, refreshOwnerLease } from './lib/index.js';
    21	import { DATABASE_DIR } from './core/config.js';
    22	import {
    23	  getIpcBridgeStats,
    24	  resolveIpcSocketPath,
    25	  startIpcSocketServer,
    26	  type IpcSocketServerHandle,
    27	} from './lib/ipc/socket-server.js';
    28	import {
    29	  createLauncherIdleMonitor,
    30	  type LauncherIdleMonitor,
    31	} from './lib/ipc/launcher-idle-timeout.js';
    32	
    33	const DEFAULT_OWNER_LEASE_TTL_MS = 60_000;
    34	const OWNER_LEASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS = DEFAULT_OWNER_LEASE_TTL_MS / 3;
    35	let ownerLeaseRefreshTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
    36	let ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted = false;
    37	
    38	function clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer(): void {
    39	  if (!ownerLeaseRefreshTimer) return;
    40	  clearInterval(ownerLeaseRefreshTimer);
    41	  ownerLeaseRefreshTimer = null;
    42	}
    43	
    44	function startOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer(): void {
    45	  if (ownerLeaseRefreshTimer) return;
    46	  ownerLeaseRefreshTimer = setInterval(() => {
    47	    try {
    48	      const refreshed = refreshOwnerLease(DATABASE_DIR, process.pid);
    49	      if (!refreshed && !ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted) {
    50	        ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted = true;
    51	        void shutdownCodeIndex('owner lease moved to another process').finally(() => process.exit(0));
    52	      }
    53	    } catch (error: unknown) {
    54	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    55	      console.error(`[owner-lease] heartbeat refresh failed: ${message}`);
    56	    }
    57	  }, OWNER_LEASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS);
    58	  ownerLeaseRefreshTimer.unref();
    59	}
    60	
    61	process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
    62	  console.error('[mk-code-index] uncaughtException:', err);
    63	  clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
    64	  closeDbWithAssertion();
    65	  process.exit(1);
    66	});
    67	process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason) => {
    68	  console.error('[mk-code-index] unhandledRejection:', reason);
    69	  clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
    70	  closeDbWithAssertion();
    71	  process.exit(1);
    72	});
    73	
    74	function createCodeIndexMcpServer(): Server {
    75	  const codeIndexServer = new Server(
    76	    { name: 'mk-code-index', version: '1.0.0' },
    77	    { capabilities: { tools: {} } }
    78	  );
    79	
    80	  codeIndexServer.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => ({
    81	    tools: CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS,
    82	  }));
    83	
    84	  codeIndexServer.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, async (request): Promise<any> => {
    85	    const args = (request.params.arguments ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
    86	    return await codeGraphTools.dispatch(request.params.name, args);
    87	  });
    88	
    89	  return codeIndexServer;
    90	}
    91	
    92	let ipcBridge: IpcSocketServerHandle | null = null;
    93	let launcherIdleMonitor: LauncherIdleMonitor | null = null;
    94	let shutdownPromise: Promise<void> | null = null;
    95	
    96	async function shutdownCodeIndex(reason: string): Promise<void> {
    97	  if (shutdownPromise) return shutdownPromise;
    98	
    99	  shutdownPromise = (async () => {
   100	    console.error(`[mk-code-index] ${reason}`);
   101	    clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
   102	    if (launcherIdleMonitor) {
   103	      launcherIdleMonitor.stop();
   104	      launcherIdleMonitor = null;
   105	    }
   106	    if (ipcBridge) {
   107	      const bridge = ipcBridge;
   108	      ipcBridge = null;
   109	      await bridge.close().catch((error: unknown) => {
   110	        const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   111	        console.error(`[mk-code-index] ipc-bridge close error: ${message}`);
   112	      });
   113	    }
   114	    closeDbWithAssertion();
   115	  })();
   116	
   117	  return shutdownPromise;
   118	}
   119	
   120	process.once('SIGINT', () => {
   121	  void shutdownCodeIndex('SIGINT').finally(() => process.exit(0));
   122	});
   123	process.once('SIGTERM', () => {
   124	  void shutdownCodeIndex('SIGTERM').finally(() => process.exit(0));
   125	});
   126	
   127	const server = createCodeIndexMcpServer();
   128	
   129	try {
   130	  writeCodeGraphReadinessMarker(process.env.MK_CODE_INDEX_ROOT_DIR || process.cwd());
   131	} catch (error: unknown) {
   132	  const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   133	  console.error(`[mk-code-index] readiness marker write failed: ${message}`);
   134	}
   135	
   136	const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
   137	try {
   138	  await server.connect(transport);
   139	  startOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
   140	  launcherIdleMonitor = createLauncherIdleMonitor({
   141	    serviceName: 'mk-code-index',
   142	    getActiveClientCount: () => getIpcBridgeStats().secondary_clients_count,
   143	    onIdle: async () => {
   144	      await shutdownCodeIndex('launcher idle timeout');
   145	      process.exit(0);
   146	    },
   147	    log: (message: string) => console.error(message),
   148	  });
   149	  ipcBridge = await startIpcSocketServer({
   150	    socketPath: resolveIpcSocketPath(DATABASE_DIR),
   151	    createServer: () => createCodeIndexMcpServer(),
   152	    log: (message: string) => console.error(message),
   153	    onActivity: () => launcherIdleMonitor?.markActivity(),
   154	  });
   155	} catch (error: unknown) {
   156	  clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
   157	  const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   158	  const stack = error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined;
   159	  console.error('[mk-code-index] connect failed:', message);
   160	  if (stack) console.error(stack);
   161	  process.exit(1);
   162	}

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/README.md:23:- `index.ts` bootstraps the MCP server with stdio transport and writes a readiness marker before serving requests.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/README.md:26:- Tool calls go through `tools/index.js` (the dispatcher) which validates arguments against the published input schemas and routes to handler modules in `handlers/`. Graph readiness is enforced per-handler via `ensureCodeGraphReady` (read-path handlers return `blocked` on a non-fresh graph), not at the dispatch boundary.

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:75:const codeGraphContext: ToolDefinition = {
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:77:  description: '[L6:Analysis] Get LLM-oriented compact graph neighborhoods from manual or graph seeds. Modes: neighborhood (1-hop calls+imports), outline (file symbols), impact (reverse callers). When readiness requires a full scan, returns an explicit blocked payload with requiredAction `code_graph_scan`, readiness metadata, and lastPersistedAt instead of degraded graph answers. Successful responses include metadata.partialOutput for deadline/budget truncation details (reasons, omittedSections, omittedAnchors, truncatedText). Token Budget: 1200.',

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:161:      dryRun: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Run pre/post batteries and classification, but skip operation dispatch.' },

Refuses to answer when graph readiness is anything other than "fresh" — returns status: "blocked" instead of a false-safe empty affectedSymbols[]. .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/core/README.md:2:title: "Core: Database Directory Configuration"
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/core/README.md:3:description: "Resolves and exports the DATABASE_DIR constant for the code-graph MCP server, creating the directory when it does not exist."
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/core/README.md:11:# Core: Database Directory Configuration
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/core/README.md:19:`mcp_server/core/` owns the `DATABASE_DIR` constant used by the code-graph MCP server. It resolves the database directory from the `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` environment variable, falling back to a default path. The module creates the target directory on load when it does not exist.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/core/README.md:44:| `DATABASE_DIR` | const | Absolute path to the code-graph database directory. Imported by `mcp_server/index.ts`, `lib/code-graph-db.ts`, `lib/apply-orchestrator.ts`, and `lib/recovery-procedures.ts`. |

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tests/README.md:3:description: "Vitest coverage for code graph indexing, readiness, query, context, recovery and change-detection behavior."
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tests/README.md:19:`mcp_server/tests/` contains Vitest coverage for the code graph package. The suite checks structural indexing, SQLite persistence behavior, readiness gates, query and context handlers, seed resolution, verification, apply-mode recovery and diff-to-symbol attribution.

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:3:description: "Core implementation modules for structural indexing, SQLite graph storage, readiness checks, recovery operations and compact graph context."
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:8:  - "readiness contract"
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:20:`lib/` owns the implementation behind the code graph handlers. It indexes source files, stores graph records in SQLite, resolves seeds, builds compact context payloads, runs recovery workflows and reports readiness metadata for callers that need false-safe graph reads.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:26:- The database layer stores files, nodes, edges, metadata, diagnostics, parser skip-list rows and verification records. Edge writes use a bitemporal close-and-insert path that stamps `invalid_at` on superseded edges instead of deleting them, so live readers filter on `invalid_at IS NULL` while as-of readers reconstruct a past edge snapshot.
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:36:  -> readiness and result adapters
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:60:+-- structural-indexer.ts       # Workspace walk, parse and persist pipeline
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:70:+-- config-defaults.ts          # Shared config default constants and env overrides
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:74:+-- readiness-contract.ts       # Readiness and trust vocabulary
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:75:+-- readiness-marker.ts         # File-based readiness snapshot for startup consumers
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:76:+-- exclude-rule-classifier.ts  # Tiered scan exclude-rule classification
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:77:+-- owner-lease.ts              # Single-owner lifecycle lease
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:92:+-- query-intent-classifier.ts  # Query intent routing
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:141:+-- exclude-rule-classifier.ts
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:148:+-- owner-lease.ts

.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:189:| `readiness-contract.ts` | Defines readiness, canonical readiness and trust-state terms. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:201:| `owner-lease.ts` | Acquires, refreshes, classifies and releases the single Code Graph owner lease. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:213:| Exports | `index.ts` exposes library modules to handlers and startup surfaces, including lifecycle helpers (`owner-lease.ts`, `canonical-db-dir.ts`, `close-db-assertion.ts`). |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:214:| Ownership | Core graph state, parser behavior, readiness, recovery and context assembly live here. MCP argument handling lives in `../handlers/`. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:245:| `initDb()` / `getDb()` / `closeDb()` | Functions | Manage the SQLite graph database lifecycle. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:251:| `ensureCodeGraphReady()` | Function | Checks readiness before graph reads. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:254:| `acquireOwnerLease()` / `refreshOwnerLease()` / `releaseOwnerLease()` | Functions | Manage the Code Graph single-owner lifecycle lease. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:255:| `resolveCanonicalDbDir()` | Function | Resolves DB directory identity for owner and DB lifecycle boundaries. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:258:| `getParserHealth()` / `classifyError()` | Functions | Parser-health getter and parser-error classifier. |
.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/README.md:271:Expected result: TypeScript exits `0`, and parser, indexer, context, readiness, recovery and database suites pass.

codex
Key direction so far: GLM’s core “PID alive is insufficient” premise is confirmed by the old-style lease checks, but the current bridge already has a TCP bypass for the socket-file fast path. The remaining interesting gaps are around first-start timing, corrupt state files, migration WAL handling, and kill/identity safety.

  1025	
  1026	/** Initialize (or get) the code graph database */
  1027	export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
  1028	  if (db) return db;
  1029	
  1030	  try {
  1031	    dbPath = join(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
  1032	    db = new Database(dbPath);
  1033	    // Explicit busy_timeout so concurrent writers wait up to 5s
  1034	    // for the writer lock instead of throwing SQLITE_BUSY immediately. Set
  1035	    // BEFORE journal_mode/foreign_keys so any incidental contention during
  1036	    // PRAGMA setup also benefits from the wait.
  1037	    db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
  1038	    db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); // WAL enables concurrent readers without locks
  1039	    db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
  1040	    db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
  1041	    ensureSchemaMigrations(db);
  1042	
  1043	    const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
  1044	    if (!versionRow) {
  1045	      db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
  1046	    } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
  1047	      db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
  1048	    }
  1049	
  1050	    return db;
  1051	  } catch (err) {
  1052	    if (db) {
  1053	      try { db.close(); } catch { /* best effort cleanup for failed init */ }
  1054	    }
  1055	    db = null;
  1056	    dbPath = null;
  1057	    throw err;
  1058	  }
  1059	}
  1060	
  1061	/** Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed) */
  1062	export function getDb(): Database.Database {
  1063	  if (!db) initDb(DATABASE_DIR);
  1064	  // initDb either assigns the singleton database or throws before this return.
  1065	  return db!;
  1066	}
  1067	
  1068	/** Close the database connection */
  1069	export function closeDb(): void {
  1070	  if (db) {
  1071	    db.close();
  1072	    db = null;
  1073	    dbPath = null;
  1074	  }
  1075	}
  1076	
  1077	export function closeDbWithAssertion(): void {
  1078	  const handle = db;
  1079	  closeDb();
  1080	  assertDbHandleClosed(handle);
  1081	}
  1082	
  1083	function getMetadata(key: string): string | null {
  1084	  const d = getDb();
  1085	  const row = d.prepare('SELECT value FROM code_graph_metadata WHERE key = ?').get(key) as { value: string } | undefined;

     2	// MODULE: IPC Socket Server (multi-client launcher bridge)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Canonical bridge logic shared by every daemon launcher (memory,
     5	// code-index, skill-advisor). Each service supplies its own socket/db
     6	// PATHS; only the bind/reclaim/serve LOGIC lives here so the security
     7	// and race-safety contract stays identical across services.
     8	
     9	import fs from 'node:fs';
    10	import net from 'node:net';
    11	import os from 'node:os';
    12	import path from 'node:path';
    13	
    14	import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
    15	
    16	const SOCKET_FILE_NAME = 'daemon-ipc.sock';
    17	// Every live session's launcher holds one persistent slot, and multi-seat
    18	// fan-outs run well past 8 concurrent sessions. A refused connection is
    19	// accepted then closed, which probes cannot distinguish from a dead daemon,
    20	// so the cap must exceed any realistic session fleet.
    21	const DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS = 64;
    22	const TCP_EADDRINUSE_RETRY_DELAYS_MS = [100, 250, 500, 1000, 1500] as const;
    23	
    24	interface IpcBridgeStats {
    25	  socket_path: string | null;
    26	  secondary_clients_count: number;
    27	  total_secondary_messages_in: number;
    28	  total_secondary_messages_out: number;
    29	}
    30	
    31	// Structural shape of the only MCP server capability this bridge uses. Typed structurally (a
    32	// method, so its transport param is bivariant) rather than against a concrete `Server` import so
    33	// every consuming package satisfies it with its own `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` copy — the three
    34	// daemons each install the SDK separately and its private members make the classes nominally
    35	// distinct, which a concrete import would reject across package boundaries.
    36	interface McpServerLike {
    37	  connect(transport: StdioServerTransport): Promise<void>;
    38	}
    39	
    40	interface IpcSocketServerOptions {
    41	  readonly socketPath: string;
    42	  readonly createServer: () => McpServerLike;
    43	  readonly maxClients?: number;
    44	  readonly log?: (message: string) => void;
    45	  readonly onActivity?: () => void;
    46	}
    47	
    48	interface IpcSocketServerHandle {
    49	  readonly socketPath: string;
    50	  readonly close: () => Promise<void>;
    51	}
    52	
    53	let activeServer: net.Server | null = null;
    54	let activeSocketPath: string | null = null;
    55	const activeSockets = new Set<net.Socket>();
    56	const activeTransports = new Map<net.Socket, StdioServerTransport>();
    57	let totalSecondaryMessagesIn = 0;
    58	let totalSecondaryMessagesOut = 0;
    59	
    60	function countJsonRpcFrames(chunk: unknown): number {
    61	  const text = Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk.toString('utf8') : String(chunk ?? '');
    62	  const newlineCount = text.split('\n').length - 1;
    63	  return Math.max(1, newlineCount);
    64	}
    65	
    66	function parseMaxClients(rawValue = process.env.SPECKIT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS): number {
    67	  if (rawValue === undefined || rawValue === null || String(rawValue).trim() === '') {
    68	    return DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS;
    69	  }
    70	  const parsed = Number.parseInt(String(rawValue), 10);
    71	  if (!Number.isFinite(parsed) || parsed < 1) {
    72	    return DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS;
    73	  }
    74	  return parsed;
    75	}
    76	
    77	function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
    78	  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
    79	}
    80	
    81	// Canonicalize a path via realpath, even when the leaf does not exist yet. realpath the nearest
    82	// existing ancestor (so symlinked roots like macOS `/tmp` -> `/private/tmp` normalize) and
    83	// re-append the missing tail. Without ancestor canonicalization, a socket dir that was cleared
    84	// (e.g. `/tmp/<service>` after a reboot) stays literal `/tmp/...` and fails the allowed-root
    85	// check below — which canonicalizes `/tmp` to `/private/tmp` — crashing the server.
    86	// Fail closed: an existing ancestor that cannot be canonicalized (permission/loop error) must
    87	// abort rather than return a non-canonical path, which would weaken the allowed-root containment
    88	// check that downstream binds rely on.
    89	function canonicalizePath(target: string): string {
    90	  const resolved = path.resolve(target);
    91	  const tail: string[] = [];
    92	  let current = resolved;
    93	  while (!fs.existsSync(current)) {
    94	    const parent = path.dirname(current);
    95	    if (parent === current) {
    96	      return resolved;
    97	    }
    98	    tail.unshift(path.basename(current));
    99	    current = parent;
   100	  }
   101	  return path.join(fs.realpathSync.native(current), ...tail);
   102	}
   103	
   104	// Prefix-match containment: true iff `candidate` is `root` itself or a descendant of it.
   105	// Both inputs MUST already be canonicalized. Inlined here so the shared bridge carries no
   106	// cross-module dependency for a single containment call.
   107	function isWithinRoot(root: string, candidate: string): boolean {
   108	  if (candidate === root) {
   109	    return true;
   110	  }
   111	  const prefix = root.endsWith(path.sep) ? root : `${root}${path.sep}`;
   112	  return candidate.startsWith(prefix);
   113	}
   114	
   115	// Allowed roots for the IPC socket directory: the workspace itself plus the system temp dirs.
   116	// The macOS `sun_path` limit (104 chars) forces deep in-workspace socket paths into a short
   117	// `/tmp` path, so a workspace-only constraint is incompatible with the documented config.
   118	// `os.tmpdir()` is portable (Linux/CI; macOS resolves to `/var/folders/...`), and `/tmp` covers
   119	// the project convention (`SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=/tmp/<service>`). The owner check in
   120	// `canUnlinkExistingSocket` preserves the unlink-hardening intent.
   121	function allowedSocketRoots(): string[] {
   122	  const roots = new Set<string>();
   123	  roots.add(canonicalizePath(process.cwd()));
   124	  roots.add(canonicalizePath(os.tmpdir()));
   125	  roots.add(canonicalizePath('/tmp'));
   126	  return [...roots];
   127	}
   128	
   129	function isWithinAllowedSocketRoot(candidate: string): boolean {
   130	  return allowedSocketRoots().some((root) => isWithinRoot(root, candidate));
   131	}
   132	
   133	function resolveIpcSocketPath(dbDir: string): string {
   134	  const rawSocketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR
   135	    ? path.resolve(process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR)
   136	    : path.resolve(dbDir);
   137	  const socketDir = canonicalizePath(rawSocketDir);
   138	  if (!isWithinAllowedSocketRoot(socketDir)) {
   139	    throw new Error(
   140	      `IPC socket directory must stay within the workspace root or a system temp dir: ${socketDir}`,
   141	    );
   142	  }
   143	  return path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
   144	}
   145	
   146	// Only remove a stale socket at `socketPath` when it is provably ours: the parent dir resolves
   147	// inside an allowed root, the path is an actual socket, and it is owned by the current uid.
   148	// Guards against socket-hijack on shared hosts where an attacker plants a non-socket file or a
   149	// socket they own at the bind path.
   150	function canUnlinkExistingSocket(socketPath: string): boolean {
   151	  let parent: string;
   152	  try {
   153	    parent = fs.realpathSync.native(path.dirname(socketPath));
   154	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   155	    // A racing peer can remove the socket directory between the EADDRINUSE failure and this
   156	    // resolve; a vanished node is safe to reclaim, so never abort the bind on a missing path.
   157	    if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'ENOENT') {
   158	      return true;
   159	    }
   160	    throw error;
   161	  }
   162	  if (!isWithinAllowedSocketRoot(parent)) {
   163	    return false;
   164	  }
   165	  try {
   166	    const stat = fs.lstatSync(socketPath);
   167	    if (!stat.isSocket()) {
   168	      return false;
   169	    }
   170	    if (typeof process.getuid === 'function' && stat.uid !== process.getuid()) {
   171	      return false;
   172	    }
   173	    return true;
   174	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   175	    // Same race on the socket node itself: if it is already gone the prior EADDRINUSE was
   176	    // transient, so treat the path as reclaimable instead of throwing and orphaning the bind.
   177	    if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'ENOENT') {
   178	      return true;
   179	    }
   180	    throw error;
   181	  }
   182	}
   183	
   184	function getIpcBridgeStats(): IpcBridgeStats {
   185	  return {
   186	    socket_path: activeSocketPath,
   187	    secondary_clients_count: activeSockets.size,
   188	    total_secondary_messages_in: totalSecondaryMessagesIn,
   189	    total_secondary_messages_out: totalSecondaryMessagesOut,
   190	  };
   191	}
   192	
   193	async function listenOnce(server: net.Server, socketPath: string): Promise<void> {
   194	  await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
   195	    const onError = (error: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   196	      server.off('listening', onListening);
   197	      reject(error);
   198	    };
   199	    const onListening = () => {
   200	      server.off('error', onError);
   201	      resolve();
   202	    };
   203	    server.once('error', onError);
   204	    server.once('listening', onListening);
   205	    if (socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   206	      const url = new URL(socketPath);
   207	      server.listen(Number.parseInt(url.port, 10), url.hostname);
   208	      return;
   209	    }
   210	    server.listen(socketPath);
   211	  });
   212	}
   213	
   214	// TCP fallback (used when a unix socket path exceeds the sun_path limit) cannot be reclaimed by
   215	// unlinking a node — a stale TCP port frees itself once the prior owner exits, so retry the bind a
   216	// few times before giving up. Returns true once a retry binds, false if the port stays in use.
   217	async function retryTcpListenAfterEaddrInUse(
   218	  server: net.Server,
   219	  socketPath: string,
   220	  log: (message: string) => void,
   221	): Promise<boolean> {
   222	  for (const delayMs of TCP_EADDRINUSE_RETRY_DELAYS_MS) {
   223	    log(`[ipc-bridge] tcp socket in use at ${socketPath}; retrying listen in ${delayMs}ms`);
   224	    await sleep(delayMs);
   225	    try {
   226	      await listenOnce(server, socketPath);
   227	      return true;
   228	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   229	      const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   230	      if (err.code !== 'EADDRINUSE') {
   231	        throw err;
   232	      }
   233	    }
   234	  }
   235	  return false;
   236	}
   237	
   238	// Tear down any server still registered in the module-global slots before a new bind reuses them.
   239	// startIpcSocketServer tracks its server/sockets in module globals, so a second start without a
   240	// dispose would orphan the prior listener (leaking the socket and its clients) and corrupt the
   241	// shared stats. Re-entrancy must close the prior server first.
   242	async function disposeActiveServer(): Promise<void> {
   243	  const priorServer = activeServer;
   244	  for (const socket of activeSockets) {
   245	    socket.destroy();
   246	  }
   247	  activeSockets.clear();
   248	  activeTransports.clear();
   249	  if (priorServer) {
   250	    await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   251	      priorServer.close(() => resolve());
   252	    });
   253	  }
   254	  activeServer = null;
   255	  activeSocketPath = null;
   256	}
   257	
   258	async function startIpcSocketServer(options: IpcSocketServerOptions): Promise<IpcSocketServerHandle> {
   259	  if (activeServer) {
   260	    await disposeActiveServer();
   261	  }
   262	  const socketPath = options.socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')
   263	    ? options.socketPath
   264	    : path.resolve(options.socketPath);
   265	  const log = options.log ?? ((message: string) => console.error(message));
   266	  const maxClients = options.maxClients ?? parseMaxClients();
   267	  const onActivity = options.onActivity ?? (() => undefined);
   268	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   269	    const socketDir = path.dirname(socketPath);
   270	    fs.mkdirSync(socketDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
   271	    // `mode: 0o700` only applies when mkdir CREATES the dir. A pre-existing socket dir
   272	    // (e.g. an attacker-planted dir on a shared host) is not protected by the mkdir above,
   273	    // so refuse to bind under a dir not owned by us or that is group/world-writable.
   274	    try {
   275	      const st = fs.statSync(socketDir);
   276	      const uid = typeof process.getuid === 'function' ? process.getuid() : null;
   277	      if (uid !== null && st.uid !== uid) {
   278	        throw new Error(`IPC socket dir ${socketDir} not owned by current user (uid ${st.uid} != ${uid})`);
   279	      }
   280	      if ((st.mode & 0o022) !== 0) {
   281	        throw new Error(`IPC socket dir ${socketDir} is group/world-writable (mode ${(st.mode & 0o777).toString(8)})`);
   282	      }
   283	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   284	      const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error ? (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code : undefined;
   285	      if (code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;
   286	    }
   287	    // Refuse to bind over a symlink planted at the socket path. The EADDRINUSE reclaim branch
   288	    // fences a stale node via canUnlinkExistingSocket (lstat); the fresh-bind path needs the same
   289	    // protection so a symlink whose target we would later chmod cannot be used to redirect the
   290	    // permission change onto an arbitrary file.
   291	    try {
   292	      const linkStat = fs.lstatSync(socketPath);
   293	      if (linkStat.isSymbolicLink()) {
   294	        throw new Error(`IPC socket path ${socketPath} is a symlink; refusing to bind`);
   295	      }
   296	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   297	      const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error ? (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code : undefined;
   298	      if (code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;
   299	    }
   300	  }
   301	
   302	  const server = net.createServer((socket) => {
   303	    if (activeSockets.size >= maxClients) {
   304	      log(`[ipc-bridge] refusing secondary connection: max clients ${maxClients} reached`);
   305	      socket.end();
   306	      socket.destroy();
   307	      return;
   308	    }
   309	
   310	    activeSockets.add(socket);
   311	    onActivity();
   312	    log('[ipc-bridge] secondary connected pid=?');
   313	
   314	    const originalWrite = socket.write.bind(socket);
   315	    socket.write = ((chunk: unknown, ...args: unknown[]) => {
   316	      onActivity();
   317	      totalSecondaryMessagesOut += countJsonRpcFrames(chunk);

   319	    }) as typeof socket.write;

   321	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {

   324	    });

   326	    const transport = new StdioServerTransport(socket, socket);
   327	    activeTransports.set(socket, transport);

   330	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   331	      log(`[ipc-bridge] secondary connect error: ${message}`);
   332	      socket.destroy();
   333	    });
   334	
   335	    socket.once('close', () => {
   336	      activeSockets.delete(socket);
   337	      activeTransports.delete(socket);
   338	      void transport.close();
   339	      log('[ipc-bridge] disconnect');
   340	    });

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Code Graph Recovery Procedures
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Typed implementations of SQLite recovery, partial-scan recovery, and bad-apply rollback.
     5	
     6	import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     7	import {
     8	  copyFileSync,
     9	  existsSync,

    11	  readdirSync,
    12	  renameSync,
    13	  rmSync,

    18	
    19	export type ScanFunction = (args: { incremental: boolean }) => Promise<unknown>;
    20	
    21	export interface RecoveryProcedureOptions {
    22	  dbDir?: string;
    23	  auditDir?: string;
    24	  now?: () => Date;
    25	  scan?: ScanFunction;
    26	  /**
    27	   * Known-good snapshot directories that must NOT be selected as a rollback
    28	   * restore target — above all the snapshot taken at the start of the
    29	   * current run. Without this exclusion a rollback restores the snapshot it
    30	   * just created from the very state being rolled back: a no-op restore of
    31	   * the suspect database.
    32	   */

    35	
    36	export interface PruneApplyArtifactsOptions {

    39	  now?: () => Date;

    42	  /** Age threshold for quarantine/recovery/bad-apply directories. */
    43	  maxAgeDays?: number;
    44	  /** Directories that must never be pruned (e.g. the current run's snapshot). */

    46	}
    47	
    48	export interface PruneApplyArtifactsResult {
    49	  prunedDirs: string[];
    50	  keptKnownGoodDirs: string[];
    51	  errors: string[];
    52	}
    53	
    54	export interface RecoveryProcedureResult {
    55	  procedureId: 'CG-RP-001' | 'CG-RP-002' | 'CG-RP-003';
    56	  status: 'ok' | 'failed';
    57	  quarantineDir?: string;
    58	  recoveryDir?: string;
    59	  knownGoodDir?: string;
