    66	
    67	const DB_TRIPLET = [
    68	  'code-graph.sqlite',
    69	  'code-graph.sqlite-wal',
    70	  'code-graph.sqlite-shm',
    71	] as const;
    72	
    73	function timestamp(now: () => Date): string {
    74	  return now().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-');
    75	}
    76	
    77	function ensureDir(path: string): string {
    78	  mkdirSync(path, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
    79	  return path;
    80	}
    81	
    82	function assertInside(parent: string, child: string): void {
    83	  const parentResolved = resolve(parent);
    84	  const childResolved = resolve(child);
    85	  const parentWithSep = parentResolved.endsWith(sep) ? parentResolved : `${parentResolved}${sep}`;
    86	  if (childResolved !== parentResolved && !childResolved.startsWith(parentWithSep)) {
    87	    throw new Error(`Path escapes recovery root: ${child}`);
    88	  }
    89	}
    90	
    91	function resolveDbDir(dbDir?: string): string {
    92	  const resolved = resolve(dbDir ?? DATABASE_DIR);
    93	  ensureDir(resolved);
    94	  return resolved;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function tripletPath(dbDir: string, fileName: typeof DB_TRIPLET[number]): string {
    98	  const path = join(dbDir, fileName);
    99	  if (basename(path) !== fileName) {
   100	    throw new Error(`Invalid DB triplet file name: ${fileName}`);
   101	  }
   102	  assertInside(dbDir, path);
   103	  return path;
   104	}
   105	
   106	function copyTriplet(dbDir: string, targetDir: string): number {
   107	  ensureDir(targetDir);
   108	  assertInside(resolve(targetDir, '..'), targetDir);
   109	  let copied = 0;
   110	  for (const fileName of DB_TRIPLET) {
   111	    const source = tripletPath(dbDir, fileName);
   112	    if (!existsSync(source)) {
   113	      continue;
   114	    }
   115	    copyFileSync(source, join(targetDir, fileName));
   116	    copied += 1;
   117	  }
   118	  return copied;
   119	}
   120	
   121	function moveTriplet(dbDir: string, targetDir: string): number {
   122	  ensureDir(targetDir);
   123	  let moved = 0;
   124	  for (const fileName of DB_TRIPLET) {
   125	    const source = tripletPath(dbDir, fileName);
   126	    if (!existsSync(source)) {
   127	      continue;
   128	    }
   129	    renameSync(source, join(targetDir, fileName));
   130	    moved += 1;
   131	  }
   132	  return moved;
   133	}
   134	
   135	function restoreTriplet(sourceDir: string, dbDir: string): number {
   136	  let restored = 0;
   137	  for (const fileName of DB_TRIPLET) {
   138	    const source = join(sourceDir, fileName);
   139	    if (!existsSync(source)) {
   140	      continue;
   141	    }
   142	    copyFileSync(source, tripletPath(dbDir, fileName));
   143	    restored += 1;
   144	  }
   145	  return restored;
   146	}
   147	
   148	function runIntegrityCheck(sqlitePath: string): string {
   149	  if (!existsSync(sqlitePath)) {
   150	    return 'missing';
   151	  }
   152	  const db = new Database(sqlitePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
   153	  try {
   154	    const rows = db.pragma('integrity_check') as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
   155	    return rows.map((row) => String(Object.values(row)[0] ?? '')).join('\n') || 'ok';
   156	  } finally {
   157	    db.close();
   158	  }
   159	}
   160	
   161	function listKnownGoodDirs(dbDir: string, auditDir: string): string[] {
   162	  const roots = [auditDir, dbDir];
   163	  const candidates: string[] = [];
   164	  for (const root of roots) {
   165	    if (!existsSync(root)) {
   166	      continue;
   167	    }
   168	    for (const entry of readdirSync(root, { withFileTypes: true })) {
   169	      if (entry.isDirectory() && entry.name.startsWith('known-good-')) {
   170	        candidates.push(join(root, entry.name));
   171	      }
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	  return candidates.sort();
   175	}
   176	
   177	function findLatestKnownGood(dbDir: string, auditDir: string, excludeDirs: string[] = []): string | null {
   178	  const excluded = new Set(excludeDirs.map((dir) => resolve(dir)));
   179	  return listKnownGoodDirs(dbDir, auditDir)
   180	    .filter((candidate) => !excluded.has(resolve(candidate)))
   181	    .at(-1) ?? null;
   182	}
   183	
   184	/**
   185	 * Resolves the directory a rollback WOULD restore, without mutating
   186	 * anything. Uses the same selection logic as the live rollback so a
   187	 * dry-run preview can never name a different target than the real run.
   188	 */
   189	export function previewRollbackTarget(options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {}): string | null {
   190	  const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
   191	  const auditDir = resolve(options.auditDir ?? join(dbDir, 'apply-audit'));
   192	  return findLatestKnownGood(dbDir, auditDir, options.excludeKnownGoodDirs ?? []);
   193	}
   194	
   195	export function snapshotKnownGoodTriplet(options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {}): string {
   196	  const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
   197	  const auditDir = ensureDir(resolve(options.auditDir ?? join(dbDir, 'apply-audit')));
   198	  assertInside(dbDir, auditDir);
   199	  const knownGoodDir = join(auditDir, `known-good-${timestamp(options.now ?? (() => new Date()))}`);
   200	  copyTriplet(dbDir, knownGoodDir);
   201	  return knownGoodDir;
   202	}
   203	
   204	export async function recoverSqliteCorruption(
   205	  options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {},
   206	): Promise<RecoveryProcedureResult> {
   207	  const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
   208	  const ts = timestamp(options.now ?? (() => new Date()));
   209	  const recoveryDir = join(dbDir, `recovery-${ts}`);
   210	  const quarantineDir = join(dbDir, `quarantine-${ts}`);
   211	  const errors: string[] = [];
   212	  let integrityCheck = 'missing';
   213	
   214	  try {
   215	    closeDb();
   216	    copyTriplet(dbDir, recoveryDir);
   217	    integrityCheck = runIntegrityCheck(join(recoveryDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'));
   218	    moveTriplet(dbDir, quarantineDir);
   219	    if (options.scan) {
   220	      await options.scan({ incremental: false });
   221	    }
   222	    return {
   223	      procedureId: 'CG-RP-001',
   224	      status: 'ok',
   225	      recoveryDir,
   226	      quarantineDir,
   227	      integrityCheck,
   228	      scanIncremental: false,
   229	      errors,
   230	    };
   231	  } catch (error) {
   232	    errors.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
   233	    return {
   234	      procedureId: 'CG-RP-001',
   235	      status: 'failed',
   236	      recoveryDir,
   237	      quarantineDir,
   238	      integrityCheck,
   239	      errors,
   240	    };
   241	  }
   242	}
   243	
   244	export async function recoverPartialScanFailure(
   245	  options: RecoveryProcedureOptions & {
   246	    staleFileCount?: number;
   247	    gitHeadDrift?: boolean;
   248	    schemaOrErrorSignal?: boolean;
   249	  } = {},
   250	): Promise<RecoveryProcedureResult> {
   251	  const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
   252	  const errors: string[] = [];
   253	  const sqlitePath = tripletPath(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
   254	  let integrityCheck = 'missing';
   255	  let stagedFiles: string[] = [];
   256	
   257	  try {
   258	    integrityCheck = runIntegrityCheck(sqlitePath);
   259	    if (existsSync(sqlitePath)) {
   260	      const db = new Database(sqlitePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
   261	      try {
   262	        const rows = db.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_mtime_ms = 0 ORDER BY file_path LIMIT 50').all() as Array<{ file_path: string }>;
   263	        stagedFiles = rows.map((row) => row.file_path);
   264	      } finally {
   265	        db.close();
   266	      }
   267	    }
   268	    const staleFileCount = options.staleFileCount ?? stagedFiles.length;
   269	    const incremental = staleFileCount <= 50 && options.gitHeadDrift !== true && options.schemaOrErrorSignal !== true;
   270	    if (options.scan) {
   271	      await options.scan({ incremental });
   272	    }
   273	    return {
   274	      procedureId: 'CG-RP-002',
   275	      status: 'ok',
   276	      integrityCheck,
   277	      stagedFiles,
   278	      scanIncremental: incremental,
   279	      errors,
   280	    };
   281	  } catch (error) {
   282	    errors.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
   283	    return {
   284	      procedureId: 'CG-RP-002',
   285	      status: 'failed',
   286	      integrityCheck,
   287	      stagedFiles,
   288	      errors,
   289	    };
   290	  }
   291	}
   292	
   293	export async function rollbackBadApply(
   294	  options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {},
   295	): Promise<RecoveryProcedureResult> {
   296	  const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
   297	  const auditDir = ensureDir(resolve(options.auditDir ?? join(dbDir, 'apply-audit')));
   298	  assertInside(dbDir, auditDir);
   299	  const ts = timestamp(options.now ?? (() => new Date()));
   300	  const quarantineDir = join(auditDir, `bad-apply-${ts}`);
   301	  const errors: string[] = [];
   302	  let knownGoodDir: string | null = null;
   303	  let restored = false;
   304	
   305	  try {
   306	    closeDb();
   307	    moveTriplet(dbDir, quarantineDir);
   308	    knownGoodDir = findLatestKnownGood(dbDir, auditDir, options.excludeKnownGoodDirs ?? []);
   309	    if (knownGoodDir) {
   310	      assertInside(dbDir, knownGoodDir);
   311	      restored = restoreTriplet(knownGoodDir, dbDir) > 0;
   312	    }
   313	    if (restored && existsSync(tripletPath(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'))) {
   314	      const db = new Database(tripletPath(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'));
   315	      try {
   316	        db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)');
   317	      } finally {
   318	        db.close();
   319	      }
   320	    }
   321	    if (options.scan) {
   322	      await options.scan({ incremental: false });
   323	    }
   324	    return {
   325	      procedureId: 'CG-RP-003',
   326	      status: 'ok',
   327	      quarantineDir,
   328	      ...(knownGoodDir ? { knownGoodDir } : {}),
   329	      restored,
   330	      scanIncremental: false,
   331	      errors,
   332	    };
   333	  } catch (error) {
   334	    errors.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
   335	    try {
   336	      if (existsSync(quarantineDir) && readdirSync(quarantineDir).length === 0) {
   337	        rmSync(quarantineDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
   338	      }
   339	    } catch {
   340	      // Cleanup failure is not more important than the recovery failure.
   341	    }
   342	    return {
   343	      procedureId: 'CG-RP-003',
   344	      status: 'failed',
   345	      quarantineDir,
   346	      ...(knownGoodDir ? { knownGoodDir } : {}),
   347	      restored,
   348	      errors,
   349	    };
   350	  }
   351	}
   352	
   353	export function relativizeRecoveryPath(path: string, root: string = process.cwd()): string {
   354	  const rel = relative(root, path);
   355	  return rel.startsWith('..') ? path : rel;
   356	}

    62	const OWNER_LEASE_FILE_NAME = '.code-graph-owner.json';

    65	// A mutation lock is only ever held for a few synchronous fs ops, so any lock with an
    66	// UNPARSEABLE pid (empty/partial) older than this is treated as a wedged orphan from a pre-fix
    67	// binary or a hard crash between openSync and writeFileSync, and is self-healed once.
    68	const WEDGED_MUTATION_LOCK_MAX_AGE_MS = 30_000;
    69	
    70	interface OwnerLeaseMutationLock {

    72	  readonly lockPath: string;

    74	
    75	function fsyncPath(path: string): void {
    76	  let fd: number | undefined;
    77	  try {
    78	    fd = openSync(path, 'r');
    79	    fsyncSync(fd);
    80	  } finally {
    81	    if (typeof fd === 'number') {
    82	      closeSync(fd);
    83	    }
    84	  }
    85	}

    87	function makeTempPath(targetPath: string): string {

    89	}
    90	
    91	function ownerLeasePath(canonicalDbDir: string): string {

    95	function ownerLeaseLockPath(canonicalDbDir: string): string {
    96	  return join(canonicalDbDir, OWNER_LEASE_LOCK_FILE_NAME);

    99	function readOwnerLeaseMutationLockPid(lockPath: string): number | null {

   102	    const pid = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);

   105	    return null;
   106	  }

   112	  try {
   113	    return now - statSync(lockPath).mtimeMs;
   114	  } catch {
   115	    return null;
   116	  }
   117	}

   119	function tryAcquireOwnerLeaseMutationLock(canonicalDbDir: string): OwnerLeaseMutationLock | null {
   120	  const lockPath = ownerLeaseLockPath(canonicalDbDir);
   121	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 2; attempt++) {
   122	    let fd: number | undefined;
   123	    try {
   124	      fd = openSync(lockPath, 'wx', 0o600);
   125	      writeFileSync(fd, `${process.pid}\n`, { encoding: 'utf8' });
   126	      fsyncSync(fd);
   127	      return { fd, lockPath };
   128	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   129	      if (typeof fd === 'number') {
   130	        // The openSync('wx') succeeded (we own the just-created lock), but the
   131	        // subsequent writeFileSync/fsyncSync threw (e.g. ENOSPC/EIO). Close the fd AND unlink the
   132	        // orphan lock before re-throwing — otherwise it lingers empty/partial with no parseable
   133	        // pid, every later EEXIST acquirer reads a null pid, the stale-reclaim guard (which
   134	        // requires lockPid !== null) never removes it, and tryAcquire returns null forever ->
   135	        // refreshOwnerLease() returns false forever -> the heartbeat self-shuts-down the server.
   136	        closeSync(fd);
   137	        try {
   138	          unlinkSync(lockPath);
   139	        } catch (cleanupError: unknown) {
   140	          const cleanupCode = cleanupError && typeof cleanupError === 'object' && 'code' in cleanupError

   142	            : undefined;
   143	          if (cleanupCode !== 'ENOENT') {
   144	            throw cleanupError;
   145	          }
   146	        }
   147	      }
   148	      const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error ? (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code : undefined;

   150	        throw error;
   151	      }
   152	
   153	      const lockPid = readOwnerLeaseMutationLockPid(lockPath);
   154	      if (attempt === 0 && lockPid !== null && getProcessLiveness(lockPid) === 'dead') {
   155	        try {
   156	          // Identity-checked stale-lock removal. Re-read immediately before unlink; a
   157	          // successor may have replaced the dead lock between classification and removal, and
   158	          // unlink-by-path would otherwise delete the successor's live lock. Only remove it if it
   159	          // still holds the same dead PID we classified.
   160	          if (readOwnerLeaseMutationLockPid(lockPath) === lockPid) {
   161	            unlinkSync(lockPath);
   162	          }
   163	          continue;
   164	        } catch (unlinkError: unknown) {
   165	          const unlinkCode = unlinkError && typeof unlinkError === 'object' && 'code' in unlinkError
   166	            ? (unlinkError as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code
   167	            : undefined;
   168	          if (unlinkCode !== 'ENOENT') {
   169	            throw unlinkError;
   170	          }
   171	        }
   172	      }
   173	
   174	      // Self-heal a wedged lock whose pid is UNPARSEABLE (empty/partial) — the live-pid
   175	      // check above can never clear it, so without this a pre-fix orphan (or one from a hard crash
   176	      // between openSync and writeFileSync) would wedge tryAcquire -> refresh forever. Gate it on a
   177	      // conservative mtime age (locks are held only for a few synchronous ops) and re-confirm the

   180	      if (attempt === 0 && lockPid === null) {
   181	        const now = Date.now();
   182	        const ageMs = ownerLeaseMutationLockAgeMs(lockPath, now);
   183	        if (ageMs !== null && ageMs > WEDGED_MUTATION_LOCK_MAX_AGE_MS) {
   184	          try {
   185	            if (
   186	              readOwnerLeaseMutationLockPid(lockPath) === null &&
   187	              ownerLeaseMutationLockAgeMs(lockPath, now) === ageMs
   188	            ) {
   189	              unlinkSync(lockPath);
   190	            }
   191	            continue;
   192	          } catch (unlinkError: unknown) {
   193	            const unlinkCode = unlinkError && typeof unlinkError === 'object' && 'code' in unlinkError
   194	              ? (unlinkError as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code
   195	              : undefined;
   196	            if (unlinkCode !== 'ENOENT') {
   197	              throw unlinkError;
   198	            }
   199	          }
   200	        }

   203	      return null;
   204	    }
   205	  }

   210	function releaseOwnerLeaseMutationLock(lock: OwnerLeaseMutationLock): void {

   213	  } finally {

   221	    }
   222	  }

   245	    typeof candidate.canonicalDbDir === 'string'
   246	  );
   247	}

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:8:  "angle": "startup-grace-window",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:10:    "The wedge lives in maybeBridgeLeaseHolder's no-socket branch (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:405-408): when the PID lease says 'held' but the socket FILE is absent, it only emits LEASE_HELD_BY…(no-bridge-socket) and returns {action:'report'}. It never reaches the respawn path, so a live-but-socketless PID is reclaimed by NOTHING. The deep-probe/respawn machinery (bridge:418-429 → launcher respawnAfterDeadSocket L668-717) only fires on an existing-but-dead socket, not a missing one.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:11:    "classifyOwnerLease's stale-heartbeat-reclaim (launcher.cjs:465-468) does NOT cover this wedge: it needs the owner lease FILE present and keys off lastHeartbeatIso > ttlMs*2 (120s). The heartbeat proves only the LAUNCHER process is alive (startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat L399-406 runs in the launcher), NOT that the spawned MCP child ever bound its socket. A wedged child behind a live launcher keeps heartbeating forever and never trips stale-heartbeat-reclaim. Heartbeat advancing ≠ socket readiness; do not use lastHeartbeatIso to gate the no-socket decision.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:12:    "The lease's single usable startup timestamp is startedAtIso (buildOwnerLease L410-421, written once at acquire). It is already flowed into the bridge via launcher.cjs:1077 (startedAt: holder.startedAtIso), so maybeBridgeLeaseHolder has the age signal in hand at the no-socket branch.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:13:    "A legitimately-starting daemon writes both lease files BEFORE spawning the child (writeLeaseFile L1182 precedes launchServer L1189), and the child creates the socket asynchronously after spawn. So 'lease present + no socket' is the expected transient state for every cold start; reclaiming it eagerly would kill healthy spawns. The 2-min stale-heartbeat threshold (ttlMs*2, L467) and BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 (L870) already establish 120s as the house ceiling for init lateness — reuse it as the max-init deadline.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:14:    "The incident's 'lease file gone + orphan WAL' is a distinct, worse failure mode: with no owner lease file, acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L474-515) sees nothing to reclaim, reclaimedOrphanPid stays null (L1066), and the second writer spawns against a DB the orphan still holds. A grace window cannot help when the source-of-truth file is missing — that needs a separate DB-level 'writer-present without live lease' probe (wal/journal-holder check), out of scope for this angle but must be flagged."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:18:      "change": "In the no-bridge-socket branch of maybeBridgeLeaseHolder, replace the unconditional report with a 3-state classifier keyed on owner-lease age (now − startedAtIso): (1) age <= STARTUP_GRACE_MS → {action:'report', reason:'still-starting'} (do NOT kill); (2) STARTUP_GRACE_MS < age <= MAX_INIT_MS → retry fs.existsSync(socketPath) on a short bounded poll (e.g. 3× @ 1s) and only report if still absent; (3) age > MAX_INIT_MS → return {action:'respawn', reason:'startup-deadline-exceeded'} so it flows into the existing respawnAfterDeadSocket reap+respawn path.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:19:      "where": ".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:maybeBridgeLeaseHolder (lines ~405-408, the `if (!fs.existsSync(socketPath))` block)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:20:      "why": "This is the exact point where the wedge currently dead-ends. Routing the over-deadline case to action:'respawn' reuses the proven reap (SIGTERM→RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS→SIGKILL, launcher.cjs:640-666) + lease re-acquire + relaunch instead of inventing a second kill path."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:23:      "change": "Add constants STARTUP_GRACE_MS=30000 and MAX_INIT_MS=120000, both env-overridable via SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_STARTUP_GRACE_MS / SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_MAX_INIT_MS (mirror the existing parsePositiveInteger pattern at bridge L21-25). Rationale: 30s grace > observed healthy socket-bind latency (child binds within seconds; 30s absorbs FTS/model-init jitter); 120s max-init aligns with ttlMs*2 (L467) and BOOTSTRAP_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS (L870) so the system has one init-latency ceiling.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:24:      "where": ".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs (top-level consts near L14-16) + parsed in resolveProbeTimeoutMs siblings",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:28:      "change": "Add childSpawnedAtIso to the owner lease, stamped in launchServer right after the child pid is known (refreshOwnerLeaseFile call at launcher.cjs:974-978), and prefer it over startedAtIso as the grace-window clock. startedAtIso is set at lease acquire (L415) which precedes bootstrap-lock wait + npm/tsc build (acquireBootstrapLock L921-953, buildIfNeeded L847-867) — using it would let a slow first-build consume the entire grace window before the child even exists.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:29:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:buildOwnerLease (L410-421 schema), launchServer (L974-978 stamp), readOwnerLeaseFile validation (L327-336 add field as optional-but-preferred)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:30:      "why": "The grace window must measure time since the CHILD was spawned (the thing that creates the socket), not since the launcher took the lease. Without childSpawnedAtIso, MAX_INIT_MS can false-positive on a cold-machine first build."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:33:      "change": "Make the respawn decision in the no-socket branch pass the ownerPid explicitly (it is already in leaseResult.ownerPid, surfaced by leaseHeldFromFile L582-610 and ownerSocketPath L371-379) so respawnAfterDeadSocket's reapOwnerBeforeRespawn (L640-666) targets the correct pid — the recorded child/owner, not a recomputed guess.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:34:      "where": ".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:maybeBridgeLeaseHolder return at the new deadline-exceeded branch → consumed by bridgeOrReportLeaseHolder (launcher.cjs:719-754) → respawnAfterDeadSocket (launcher.cjs:668-717, uses options.respawnChildPid)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:35:      "why": "respawnAfterDeadSocket already requires options.respawnChildPid (L674-679) and aborts as 'missing-child-pid' if absent; the no-socket branch must supply it from the lease or the reclaim silently no-ops."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:38:      "change": "Before force-reclaim in the deadline-exceeded branch, re-run classifyOwnerLease on the live lease (launcher.cjs:455-472) as a belt-and-suspenders cross-check: if it already returned stale-heartbeat-reclaim or ppid-1-orphan, the reclaim is corroborated and should proceed unconditionally; if it returns live-owner (heartbeat fresh) despite the missing socket, that is the strongest possible wedge signature (launcher alive, child socketless) — reclaim and log it loudly as 'launcher-alive-socketless-child'.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:39:      "where": "Plumb the current owner lease into the bridge call (launcher.cjs:1075 already has holder), or do the classify in bridgeOrReportLeaseHolder (launcher.cjs:728-744) before dispatching respawn",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:40:      "why": "Avoids a single timestamp being the only thing between a healthy daemon and a SIGKILL; the dual signal (age > MAX_INIT_MS AND classifyOwnerLease non-live-or-socketless) is the safe gate."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:45:    "Polling fs.existsSync in the grace-to-deadline middle zone (recommendation 1, state 2) adds latency to MCP reconnect for a socketless owner; bound it to <=3s total and fall through to report if still absent so the runtime sees LEASE_HELD_BY promptly.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:46:    "tcp:// socket endpoints bypass the existence check (bridge L405 short-circuits on startsWith('tcp://')), so the grace-window logic only applies to UDS file-backed sockets; a tcp owner that never binds is still a no-bridge-socket dead-end and needs the same age-based treatment via a connect probe instead of existsSync.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:47:    "If the owner lease file itself was deleted (the incident's 'lease file gone'), startedAtIso/childSpawnedAtIso are unrecoverable and the grace window cannot fire — acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L474-515) will simply take a fresh lease and spawn a second writer against the orphan-held DB. This angle does NOT fix that; it requires a separate DB-holder liveness probe (journal/WAL owner check) and must be tracked as its own hardening item.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-004.md:48:    "Reaping a launcher whose child is mid-write could leave the 17MB WAL orphan again unless wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) runs after reap and before respawn; respawnAfterDeadSocket (L668-717) currently does no DB checkpoint — add an explicit checkpoint step after reapOwnerBeforeRespawn succeeds."

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:19:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/recovery-procedures.ts[90m [offset=290, limit=50][0m
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:21:{"angle":"wal-startup-robustness","insights":["The launcher never touches SQLite directly — it only manages leases, sockets, and process lifecycle. DB open happens in the spawned child via initDb (code-graph-db.ts:1027), which sets journal_mode=WAL (L1038) but sets NO wal_autocheckpoint and runs NO wal_checkpoint on open. SQLite's default wal_autocheckpoint=1000 pages (~4MB) only fires on transaction commit inside a connection; a writer that wedged/crashed mid-stream leaves a WAL that grows unbounded and never auto-checkpoints — exactly the 17MB orphan seen.","The launcher already holds the unambiguous single-writer guarantee at startup: acquireBootstrapLock (L921) + acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L474) + the orphan-reap gate (launcherMain L1174) together prove no other process can be writing the DB when launchServer (L955) spawns the child. This is the ONLY safe place to run wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) without racing another writer, and the launcher currently wastes it.","The migration block (L1118-1164) copies code-graph.sqlite-wal verbatim (L1128, L1145 COPYFILE_EXCL). A crash-orphaned 17MB WAL is migrated forward as-is, so the problem survives relocation — checkpointing before/instead-of copying the WAL would also fix the migrate path.","recovery-procedures.ts:316 already runs wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) after a restore, proving the team's accepted idiom — it just only fires on the rollback-bad-apply path, never on normal startup.","The incident's manual recovery (kill orphans, wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE), clean socket dir) is precisely what the launcher should automate inside its existing lock+lease critical section — the lease CAS (acquireOwnerLeaseFile L474-515) and stale-heartbeat reclaim (classifyOwnerLease L467-469) already detect the wedge condition that produced the orphan; they just don't clean up the WAL the orphan left."],"recommendations":[{"change":"Add a startup WAL hygiene step after lockHeld is acquired and before launchServer: stat code-graph.sqlite-wal; if it exists and exceeds a threshold (e.g. 8MB) OR if the DB was opened by a now-reaped orphan (reclaimedOrphanPid set), open the DB read-only-ish, run PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE), then close before spawning the child. This collapses the 17MB-orphan class entirely.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain ~L1180 (between the reclaimedOrphanPid reap gate and writeLeaseFile/launchServer); new helper e.g. checkpointOversizedWal(dbDir, {force})","why":"At L1180 the bootstrap lock is held and the prior owner is provably reaped/dead, so this is the one provably single-writer moment. TRUNCATE here folds the WAL into the main db before the child's initDb re-opens it, so the child never pays startup cost against a giant WAL and never inherits an orphaned WAL."},{"change":"Set wal_autocheckpoint in initDb alongside journal_mode=WAL, e.g. db.pragma('wal_autocheckpoint = 500') (~2MB) or a tunable env (SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_WAL_AUTOCHECKPOINT_PAGES). This caps how large a WAL can grow between forced checkpoints during normal operation.","where":"system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:initDb ~L1038 (immediately after the journal_mode=WAL pragma)","why":"The default 1000 pages only triggers on commit in the connection doing the writing; a long-running daemon doing incremental scans with many small transactions can blow well past it before autocheckpoint fires, and a crash mid-batch leaves the oversized WAL. A lower, explicit bound bounds the worst-case orphan size at crash time."},{"change":"In the migration block, before copying code-graph.sqlite-wal (L1128/1145), open the source DB and run wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) so only the main .sqlite is copied (or copy then checkpoint the target). At minimum skip copying an oversized -wal and checkpoint the target after copy.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain migration block ~L1139-1147","why":"As written, a 17MB orphan WAL at the former shared location is cloned verbatim into the skill-local target, propagating the stall across the migration. Checkpointing at the source (formerLocationOwnerLive already proved no live writer at L1123) yields a clean target."},{"change":"Reuse the recovery-procedures.ts:316 idiom (open Database, PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE), close) for the launcher-side checkpoint helper so the two code paths share the same defensive close-in-finally shape.","where":"new helper referenced by launcherMain + recovery-procedures.ts:316","why":"Consolidates the one accepted checkpoint pattern; recovery-procedures already proves this is the team's sanctioned technique, just scope-limited to rollback-bad-apply."},{"change":"Bound the startup checkpoint against a pathological WAL: if the -wal exceeds a hard cap (e.g. 256MB), log and run PASSIVE checkpoint or skip TRUNCATE rather than blocking startup on a multi-second frame walk; surface the size in the ready log (L1167).","where":"new checkpointOversizedWal helper","why":"TRUNCATE walks every committed frame; on a genuinely huge WAL from a runaway writer this could add seconds to every cold start. A size-graded policy (small→TRUNCATE, huge→PASSIVE+warn) keeps startup latency bounded while still clearing the common 17MB case."}],"risks":["Checkpointing committed WAL frames is safe by construction — TRUNCATE only removes frames already applied to the main db file; it cannot lose committed data. The real risk is running it against a DB another process still holds open: mitigated by doing it exclusively inside the bootstrap-lock + owner-lease critical section (L1102/L1174), which the code already treats as the single-writer proof.","If the -shm file is stale/missing relative to the -wal (e.g. a hard crash dropped it), wal_checkpoint can return BUSY/RECOVER or no-op; the helper must read the PRAGMA result (busy:0/1, log:N, checkpointed:N) and not assume success — wrap in try/catch and log, never block launch on checkpoint failure.","Opening the DB from the launcher (Node better-sqlite3) requires the native module to be loadable in the launcher process, which today only shells out to the child; verify better-sqlite3 resolves from the launcher's require path, or shell out to a tiny `sqlite3` CLI / a short-lived node -e to avoid coupling the launcher to the child's build artifacts (artifactsReady at L839 may be false on first run — checkpoint must not run before buildIfNeeded at L1166 if it depends on the built module).","wal_autocheckpoint=500 increases checkpoint frequency, which adds small fsync stalls on the writer thread mid-scan; on a fast NVMe this is negligible but on slow disks it could show as scan latency jitter — make it tunable and benchmark a full code_graph_scan before/after.","The migration-source checkpoint (recommendation 3) opens a DB at the legacy shared path; if a live legacy owner appears between the formerLocationOwnerLive probe (L1123) and the open, you'd open a DB under a live writer. Re-probe formerLocationOwnerLive immediately before opening, or rely on busy_timeout + treating SQLITE_BUSY as 'skip checkpoint, copy as-is'."]}
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:10:    "ROOT: liveness is decided by process.kill(pid,0) + a heartbeat that the LAUNCHER (P1) writes, with ZERO socket-health correlation. leaseHeldFromFile L602-603 and classifyOwnerLease L455-472 both treat a hung daemon that is alive in the process table but never bound/lost its IPC socket as fully healthy. A hung P1 that keeps heartbeating (setInterval L399) while its child daemon C1 is wedged is classified live-owner FOREVER — ppid-1-orphan (L461) only fires when P1 is DEAD; the 2×ttlMs=120s stale-heartbeat window (L467) only trips when the heartbeat stops. So the precise un-reclaimable state is: P1 alive+heartbeating, C1 alive but not serving. None of the three reclaim signals (stale-pid / ppid-1-orphan / stale-heartbeat-reclaim) can fire.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:11:    "THE WEDGE POINT is launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs maybeBridgeLeaseHolder L405-408: when the holder PID is reported held:true by the launcher (L603) but the socket FILE is absent, fs.existsSync(socketPath) is false → it returns {action:'report', reason:'no-bridge-socket'} and writes LEASE_HELD_BY:<pid> (no-bridge-socket). It NEVER reaches the deep probe at L418-425, so it NEVER returns action:'respawn' (which only comes from probe.status!=='alive' at L426-429). The entire reap+respawn machine (respawnAfterDeadSocket L668, reapOwnerBeforeRespawn L640) is gated BEHIND action:'respawn' — a missing-socket holder short-circuits to report BEFORE the probe that would have authorized a reap.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:12:    "Consequence in launcherMain: bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld (L719-754) receives decision.action==='report', so it skips the respawn branch (L728), emits a heldByOther metric (L745), returns, and launcherMain returns at L1082 (owner-lease-held path) or L1089 (pid-lease-held path). The runtime is handed LEASE_HELD_BY:<pid> with no usable transport → its MCP client connects to a non-existent/refused socket → JSON-RPC -32000.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:13:    "Lease-FILE-gone sub-path: if .mk-code-index-launcher.json is absent, leaseHeldFromFile L584 returns held:false immediately, isLeaseHeld (L612-623) falls through, launcherMain skips bridging (L1086) and spawns a successor. BUT this only executes if the OWNER lease (.code-graph-owner.json) was ALSO reclaimable. If the owner lease was wiped too, reclaimedOrphanPid stays null (L1048) so the reap guard at L1174-1180 has nothing to kill → new child C2 opens the same SQLite DB alongside the still-live orphan C1 → writer collision, the 17MB WAL grows unbounded, C2 blocks/errors → -32000 again. The reap guard depends entirely on the prior owner lease having survived to name C1; a double-wipe produces a silent collision.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:14:    "Even in the recovery-favorable case (P1 dead), there is a 120s blind window: classifyOwnerLease needs Date.now()-heartbeatMs > ttlMs*2 (L467) before it returns stale-heartbeat-reclaim. For the full 120s after P1 dies while C1 is wedged, every secondary reconnect gets live-owner → no-bridge-socket → -32000. The heartbeat is the only freshness signal and it has no socket correlation, so 'fresh heartbeat' does not imply 'serving IPC'.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:15:    "ownerSocketPath() (L371-379) and buildOwnerLease socketPath (L419) record the computed path at WRITE time, but nothing ever re-validates that the recorded path is bound. The socketPath field is surfaced to the bridge (L587) but only as a connect TARGET, never as a health signal feeding back into held/staleReclaimable.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:20:      "change": "Treat a held-lease with a missing socket FILE as respawn-eligible, not report-only. Change the L405-408 branch to return {action:'respawn', reason:'no-bridge-socket', socketPath} instead of {action:'report',...} when leaseResult.ownerPid is alive. This routes the existing respawn/reap pipeline (respawnAfterDeadSocket → reapOwnerBeforeRespawn) at a holder whose socket is gone. Keep a single guard: if the holder PID is itself dead (staleReclaimable), keep returning report/stale so the normal reclaim path in launcherMain handles it.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:21:      "where": "launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:maybeBridgeLeaseHolder/L405-408",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:22:      "why": "This is the single decision that turned a recoverable dead-socket into a permanent -32000. The probe that authorizes reap is currently placed after an early-return that fires exactly when reap is needed."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:25:      "change": "Add a bounded socket-existence (or connect-ok) check into classifyOwnerLease before returning 'live-owner'. If lease.socketPath is set and fs.existsSync is false (and not tcp://), downgrade 'live-owner' to a new 'live-pid-dead-socket' classification that acquireOwnerLeaseFile treats as reclaimable (reclaim + record orphanPid for reap). Gate it behind the existing uid/legacy guards so foreign-owned paths are not probed.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:26:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease/L455-472 (and acquireOwnerLeaseFile L478-484 to treat the new class as reclaimable)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:27:      "why": "The owner-lease gate is the first decision; if it returns live-owner for a hung P1+missing-socket C1, no secondary ever reaches the bridge's probe. Socket-health must feed the SAME decision that grants the single-writer lease."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:30:      "change": "Add the same socket-health correlation to leaseHeldFromFile: after process.kill(pid,0) succeeds (L602), if lease.socketPath is present and not tcp:// and !fs.existsSync, return {held:false, ownerPid, staleReclaimable:true} with a 'dead-socket' reason instead of held:true. This makes the L1086/L1091 branches in launcherMain treat a socket-less holder as reclaimable and emit the staleReclaimed metric + reap path.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:31:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:leaseHeldFromFile/L601-609",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:32:      "why": "held:true/staleReclaimable:false (L603) is the exact misclassification the incident hinged on; it must reflect IPC servability, not just process-table presence."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:35:      "change": "Add an orphan-detection fallback for the double-wipe case (both lease files gone, live holder still holding the DB). Before writeLeaseFile()/launchServer() in launcherMain (L1182-1189), when isLeaseHeld returned held:false AND no reclaimedOrphanPid was recorded, probe for a live process holding code-graph.sqlite (lsof/fuser or a non-blocking open-with-BUSY check on the -wal). If a holder is found, treat it as reclaimedOrphanPid and run reapOwnerBeforeRespawn before spawning.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:36:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain/L1085-1097 and L1174-1180",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:37:      "why": "The current reap guard is purely lease-driven; with no lease file naming C1, the launcher spawns C2 straight into a SQLite writer collision (the 17MB orphaned WAL). The lease is advisory metadata; the DB lock is ground truth."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:40:      "change": "Move the serving-heartbeat ownership from the launcher (P1) to the daemon (C1), or add a daemon-written 'lastServedIso' field updated from inside the MCP server's event loop (e.g. after each request handled). Have classifyOwnerLease require lastServedIso freshness, not just P1's lastHeartbeatIso. Keep P1's heartbeat as a secondary launcher-alive signal.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:41:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat/L394-408 + buildOwnerLease/L410-421; daemon side in mcp_server/dist",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:45:      "change": "Reduce the stale-heartbeat blind window (L467 ttlMs*2 = 120s) for the dead-socket sub-signal: once socket-existence is part of the classification (recs above), a missing socket should bypass the heartbeat TTL entirely and reclaim immediately, since a missing socket is not a transient-busy condition the TTL was designed to absorb.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:46:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease/L465-469",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:47:      "why": "The 120s TTL protects a busy daemon mid-FTS-merge from false reaping; that rationale does not apply to a socket that is simply absent. Conflating the two prolongs every dead-socket outage by the full TTL."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:51:    "Reclassifying no-bridge-socket as respawn can false-reap a daemon that is mid-(re)start — socket transiently absent between unlink and rebind. Mitigate with the existing probe-retry discipline (resolveLeaseProbeAttempts, L52-54) applied even on the missing-socket branch: require N consecutive existence/probe failures across a short window before reaping.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:52:    "Adding fs.existsSync/socket probes to the hot lease path adds latency to every launcher start; bound it (existence check is sub-ms, but a connect probe must keep the <7000ms grace ceiling) and skip for tcp:// and foreign-uid legacy paths.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:53:    "Downgrading live-owner to reclaimable on a missing socket changes concurrency semantics: two secondaries could both see reclaimable and both unlink+O_EXCL — the existing CAS (acquireOwnerLeaseFile L491-505) already serializes this, but the reap+respawn inside respawnAfterDeadSocket must continue to hold the bootstrap lock so only one reaper kills C1.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:54:    "lsof/fuser orphan detection (rec 4) is platform-dependent (darwin vs linux) and may itself need elevated permissions under sandbox; a pure-SQLite approach (attempt open with busy_timeout=0) is more portable but can itself block on a wedged writer.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:55:    "A serving-heartbeat written by C1 (rec 5) couples the daemon binary to the lease format; ensure back-compat read of leases lacking lastServedIso (treat as P1-heartbeat-only, the current behavior) so a rolling upgrade does not classify every existing owner as stale.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:56:    "If respawn-on-missing-socket is enabled while SPECKIT_BRIDGE_RESPAWN_DISABLED=1 (L669), the respawn is still inert — the env kill-switch must be documented as the only way to suppress the new recovery, otherwise operators expecting the old report-only behavior will see unexpected reaps."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:16:You are GPT-5.5 at xhigh reasoning doing an ADVERSARIAL verification pass over a hardening design produced by a different model (GLM-5.2). Be skeptical: your job is to CONFIRM what is right, REFUTE what is wrong, and find GAPS the prior research missed — not to agree. Read the synthesized design at .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md and the actual source .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs and .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs (and the child initDb under system-code-graph/mcp_server if useful). Ground every claim in the REAL code (cite function/line). Incident recap: the code-index daemon wedged (PID alive, IPC socket never created, lease file gone, 17MB orphaned WAL) and /mcp reconnect returned -32000.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:19:Try to REFUTE each P0 recommendation (socket-health probe reuse, the live-but-dead-socket reclaimable state, no-bridge-socket->respawn, the startup grace window, startup WAL hygiene, diagnostics). Specifically stress: (a) does reusing probeDaemon({deepProbe:true}) as a health check have side effects — does opening an extra JSON-RPC initialize connection consume/disturb the real client, race the bridge, or get counted as a client? (b) is the launcher safe to open/checkpoint the sqlite WAL itself given the launcher normally never touches sqlite — could that create a second writer or lock contention with the child? (c) is the grace-window math sound given the bootstrap-lock + first npm/tsc build happen BEFORE the child spawns? Confirm or refute each with code evidence.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:39:**Subject:** Prevent recurrence of the Code Graph daemon wedge — a PID-alive-but-socket-dead daemon the launcher does not reclaim, causing MCP reconnect `-32000`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:41:**Target file:** `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` (+ `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` `initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:45:Liveness is **bidimensional** — `processLiveness` proves only `process.kill(pid,0)` (PID exists), and the heartbeat is written by the **launcher**, not gated on the daemon's ability to serve. There is **zero socket-health correlation**. So a wedged daemon (PID alive, 0% CPU, socket never (re)created) classifies as `live-owner` / `held:true`. The inert branch is `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder`'s no-socket path (bridge `L405-408`): when the lease says "held" but the socket FILE is missing, it `report`s instead of respawning. Two compounding axes in the incident: (a) the lease file had also vanished from an empty socket dir, so the heartbeat-reclaim (which needs a lease file with an aging `lastHeartbeatIso`) never had anything to age out; (b) a 17 MB orphaned WAL from the unclean crash.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:51:1. **Reuse the existing probe.** `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:probeDaemon({deepProbe:true})` (~L150-236) already connects the UDS + sends a JSON-RPC `initialize`. Wrap it as `probeExistingService(socketPath)` returning `{status, kind}` where `kind ∈ {enoent, econnrefused, timeout, json-rpc-reply}`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:52:2. **Socket-health gate in classification.** Add a `live-but-dead-socket` state to `classifyOwnerLease` and mirror it in `leaseHeldFromFile` (after the `process.kill(pid,0)` success at `~L602`): if `socketPath` is missing/dead (fast-path `!existsSync`, else N-consecutive deep-probe failures), return `staleReclaimable:true` instead of `held:true`. Extend the reclaimable allowlist (`~L479-484`) — the existing `unlink + O_EXCL` CAS handles it atomically, no CAS change.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:53:3. **No-bridge-socket → respawn, not report.** Change bridge `L405-408` to route a socketless-but-live owner into the existing `respawnAfterDeadSocket → reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` pipeline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:54:4. **Startup grace window** (so a *starting* daemon isn't killed): `STARTUP_GRACE_MS=30000`, `MAX_INIT_MS=120000` (env-overridable), keyed on a new `childSpawnedAtIso` stamped right after the child PID is known — NOT `startedAtIso` (which precedes the bootstrap-lock wait + first build). `age ≤ grace` → still-starting (report); `grace < age ≤ max` → bounded socket re-poll; `age > max` → respawn (`startup-deadline-exceeded`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:55:5. **Socket-gated heartbeat.** Refresh `lastHeartbeatIso` only when the socket is actually serving; on probe failure, stop heartbeating so a dead-socket daemon ages out into `stale-heartbeat-reclaim` within 2 min. Ideally move heartbeat ownership into the daemon child (refresh only after its IPC listener echoes).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:56:6. **Crash-surviving PID registry + self-heal on acquire.** Write `.code-graph-daemon-pid.json` (`{daemonPid, launcherPid, socketPath, startedAt, canonicalDbDir}`) right after the child PID is known; unlink only on clean child exit. On acquire, discover the orphan via `reclaimedOrphanPid → that registry → lsof on the WAL`, and reap (SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL) **only** when `alive AND socket provably dead (N consecutive failures)**, then checkpoint, then spawn.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:57:7. **WAL startup hygiene.** Before spawn: if `code-graph.sqlite-wal` exceeds a threshold (~8 MB) or an orphan was just reaped, run `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` (no other writer holds it). Set `wal_autocheckpoint=500` (~2 MB, tunable) in `initDb`. Checkpoint before the migration copy so a giant `-wal` is never propagated.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:58:8. **Race + permission safety.** Apply the uid check to the PRIMARY lease (not just `legacyPath`) and to the socket owner — never unlink/bridge/kill a foreign-owned lease. Before any SIGKILL, verify PID identity (cmdline basename + process start-time vs `startedAtIso`) to avoid killing a PID-reuse victim; on mismatch log `pid-reuse-suspected` and abort. Re-stat the lease between classify and unlink (abort if changed). Gate self-heal behind a one-shot `.self-heal-attempted` marker so a failed heal never loops into a kill storm.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:59:9. **One-line diagnostics.** Emit `LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC: reason=<token> ownerPid=<n> socketPath=<p> walHeldBy=<pid|none>` on every failure exit. Tokens: `dead-socket-reclaimed`, `startup-timeout`, `foreign-owner`, `wal-locked-by-orphan`, `bridge-socket-refused`, `pid-reuse-suspected`, `stale-heartbeat-reclaimed`. Replaces manual lsof/ps forensics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:63:- **Wedge → reclaim:** spawn a fake owner that writes the lease + holds a live PID but never opens the socket; past `MAX_INIT_MS`, assert the launcher reclaims (reaps + respawns) and emits `dead-socket-reclaimed`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:65:- **Foreign-owner → not killed:** lease/socket owned by a different uid → assert no signal, `foreign-owner` diagnostic.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:66:- **PID-reuse → not killed:** live PID whose cmdline/start-time mismatch the lease → `pid-reuse-suspected`, abort.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:67:- **Oversized WAL → checkpointed:** seed an 8MB+ `-wal` with no holder → assert `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` runs pre-spawn and WAL shrinks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:68:- **Healthy owner → bridged:** live PID + serving socket → assert bridge, no reclaim.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:72:Implement components **1-3 + 4 + 7** as the P0 core (they directly close the incident class), then **6, 8, 9** as the durability/safety layer, then **5** (heartbeat) as defense-in-depth. The minimal P0 (socket-health gate + reclaim-on-dead-socket past a grace window + startup WAL hygiene + diagnostics) collapses both failure axes from the incident.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:79:- Launcher under study: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, and the child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` (`initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:80:- source: live incident forensics, 2026-06-29 — orphaned PIDs 37269/37358 at 0% CPU, empty `/tmp/mk-code-index/`, a 17 MB `code-graph.sqlite-wal` with no holder, DB integrity ok; manual recovery via `kill` + `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` + socket-dir clean.
