.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5176:.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:889:// staleMs ago wedges every requireLock respawn for the full wait deadline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5177:.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1046:  // against the same DB (mirrors the dead-socket respawn reap and the sibling
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5178:.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1080:          respawnChildPid: ownerLeaseResult.holder.ownerPid,
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5179:.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1172:    // winning launcher reaps + respawns; a still-live orphan would otherwise keep
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5180:.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tests/code-index-cli-owner-respawn.vitest.ts:11:describe('code-index CLI owner lease and respawn locking', () => {
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5181:.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tests/code-index-cli-owner-respawn.vitest.ts:30:    const harness = createCodeIndexHarness('dead-socket-respawn');
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5189:     4	// initDb must set busy_timeout = 5000ms so concurrent writers
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5260:    "GLM rec socket-health correlation is correct: classifyOwnerLease only checks process liveness, ppid-orphan, and heartbeat before returning live-owner; it never checks socket serviceability (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:455). leaseHeldFromFile likewise returns held:true immediately after process.kill(pid,0) succeeds (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:582).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5261:    "GLM rec reusing the deep probe is mostly correct: probeDaemon({deepProbe:true}) opens the socket, sends JSON-RPC initialize, and only reports alive after a JSON-RPC reply with the probe id (.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:150, .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202, .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:221). The code-index CLI already uses this before opening its real client (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/code-index-cli.ts:1118).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5262:    "GLM rec no-bridge-socket->respawn targets a real hole: maybeBridgeLeaseHolder currently treats a missing Unix socket as report/no-bridge-socket, not respawn (.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:405).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5263:    "GLM rec not to key startup grace on startedAt is correct: the owner lease is acquired before bootstrap lock and build (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1060, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1102, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1166), while the child PID is only known after spawn in launchServer (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:967, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:973).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5264:    "GLM rec WAL hygiene is directionally correct: initDb enables WAL and busy_timeout but does not set wal_autocheckpoint or checkpoint on startup (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:1027). The launcher migration path copies code-graph.sqlite-wal forward if present (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1125).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5265:    "GLM rec diagnostics is correct as a need: current launcher output is fragmented LEASE_HELD_BY/log strings and no LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC or walHeldBy equivalent exists (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:625, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1074)."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5270:      "problem": "It does not disturb the primary stdio client, but it is not side-effect-free. The IPC server counts the probe as a secondary connection, calls onActivity, and can refuse it when max secondary clients is reached. A refused probe is indistinguishable from a dead daemon to the caller, so a saturated but healthy bridge can be false-classified dead unless the health path treats max-client/refused separately.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5274:      "target": "GLM rec: live-but-dead-socket is reclaimable",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5275:      "problem": "Correct only after a startup-state gate exists. Today a legitimate first launcher owns the lease before the bootstrap lock and first npm/tsc build complete, so a second launcher can see a live owner with no socket while startup is still valid. A raw no-socket=>reclaim rule would kill a real startup.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5276:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1060"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5279:      "target": "GLM rec: no-bridge-socket should route into respawnAfterDeadSocket",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5280:      "problem": "As a generic bridge change, this is incomplete. respawnAfterDeadSocket for code-index refuses to act without options.respawnChildPid; the PID-lease path calls bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld without that option, so a missing socket there would still degrade to dead-socket-no-child-pid unless the PID lease records childPid or a registry supplies it.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5281:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:674"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5285:      "problem": "Refuted for the 'lease file gone' incident variant. reclaimedOrphanPid is populated only from a reclaimed owner lease, and the orphan reap runs only when that value exists. If both lease artifacts are gone, socket-health classification has no PID to classify or reap.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5286:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1060"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5289:      "target": "GLM rec: launcher can checkpoint WAL at startup because no other writer holds it",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5290:      "problem": "That premise is not guaranteed by the current launcher. The launcher normally never opens SQLite; the child does in initDb. The bootstrap lock serializes launchers, not a still-live or orphaned child. Checkpointing is safe only after exclusive daemon ownership is proven or the old owner is reaped/absent; otherwise it can contend with the live child writer and produce SQLITE_BUSY or a startup stall.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5294:      "target": "GLM rec: childSpawnedAt grace fully solves startup math",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5295:      "problem": "childSpawnedAt is necessary but not sufficient. There is a pre-child phase where the lease owner is the launcher and no childSpawnedAt can exist yet; that phase includes bootstrap-lock wait and first build. The design needs an explicit pre-spawn/build state and deadline, not only childSpawnedAt-based daemon startup grace.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5296:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1166"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5300:      "problem": "Only if diagnostics actually collect the missing facts. Current code has no lsof/wal holder probe and no unified diagnostic line; adding reason tokens without walHeldBy/source-of-pid fields would still leave the exact incident requiring manual ps/lsof/WAL inspection.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5301:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1191"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5305:    "The bridge file path in the prompt/design is stale: code-index loads .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs via loadBridgeModule, not .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:164).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5306:    "No deterministic test currently covers live PID + owner lease + missing socket file + beyond startup deadline. Existing code-index respawn tests kill the owner daemon, which exercises stale/dead ownership, not the PID-alive/socket-absent wedge (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tests/code-index-cli-owner-respawn.vitest.ts:29).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5308:    "The child starts refreshing the owner lease after server.connect but before startIpcSocketServer completes, so heartbeat freshness can still precede socket availability (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/index.ts:136, .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/index.ts:149).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5309:    "The owner lease TypeScript shape does not include socketPath, while the launcher writes and expects it. Runtime preserves extra fields today, but the contract is implicit and fragile (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/owner-lease.ts:23, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:419)."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5313:      "change": "Add explicit owner lease fields for launcherPid, daemonPid, launcherStartedAtIso, childSpawnedAtIso, startupPhase, and socketPath; classify socketless owners as pre-spawn, starting-child, live-serving, or dead-socket instead of overloading live-owner.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5314:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:buildOwnerLease/classifyOwnerLease/launchServer",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5318:      "change": "Make missing-socket respawn policy owned by the launcher, not only the shared bridge helper: maybeBridgeLeaseHolder can return no-bridge-socket, and code-index should decide respawn only when it has a verified daemonPid and the startup deadline has expired.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5319:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5320:      "why": "The shared bridge cannot know whether a missing socket means pre-child build, missing childPid, stale PID lease, or a reclaimable daemon."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5323:      "change": "Record childPid in the PID lease or add the crash-surviving registry to P0, not later. On missing leases, use the registry plus lsof/fuser on code-graph.sqlite-wal/sqlite, with PID identity checks, before spawning a new writer.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5324:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:writeLeaseFile/launchServer/launcherMain",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5325:      "why": "Socket-health checks cannot recover the incident variant where the lease file is gone."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5328:      "change": "Gate WAL checkpoint behind exclusive ownership: after owner lease acquisition, after any orphan reap, and before launchServer. Use a bounded busy_timeout and treat SQLITE_BUSY as a diagnostic, not an unhandled launcher crash. Also set PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint=500 in initDb.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5329:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain and .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:initDb",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5330:      "why": "This gives WAL cleanup without creating an unsafe second writer or wedging startup on a live child."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5333:      "change": "Add one JSON-line diagnostic containing reason, ownerPid, launcherPid, daemonPid, socketPath, startupPhase, probeReason, walBytes, walHeldBy, and action.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5334:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:failure/lease-held/respawn branches",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5335:      "why": "The current logs explain symptoms but not the state needed to distinguish dead socket, pre-spawn build, orphan WAL, PID reuse, and foreign owner."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5347:    "GLM rec socket-health correlation is correct: classifyOwnerLease only checks process liveness, ppid-orphan, and heartbeat before returning live-owner; it never checks socket serviceability (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:455). leaseHeldFromFile likewise returns held:true immediately after process.kill(pid,0) succeeds (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:582).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5348:    "GLM rec reusing the deep probe is mostly correct: probeDaemon({deepProbe:true}) opens the socket, sends JSON-RPC initialize, and only reports alive after a JSON-RPC reply with the probe id (.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:150, .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:202, .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:221). The code-index CLI already uses this before opening its real client (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/code-index-cli.ts:1118).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5349:    "GLM rec no-bridge-socket->respawn targets a real hole: maybeBridgeLeaseHolder currently treats a missing Unix socket as report/no-bridge-socket, not respawn (.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:405).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5350:    "GLM rec not to key startup grace on startedAt is correct: the owner lease is acquired before bootstrap lock and build (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1060, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1102, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1166), while the child PID is only known after spawn in launchServer (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:967, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:973).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5351:    "GLM rec WAL hygiene is directionally correct: initDb enables WAL and busy_timeout but does not set wal_autocheckpoint or checkpoint on startup (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:1027). The launcher migration path copies code-graph.sqlite-wal forward if present (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1125).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5352:    "GLM rec diagnostics is correct as a need: current launcher output is fragmented LEASE_HELD_BY/log strings and no LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC or walHeldBy equivalent exists (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:625, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1074)."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5357:      "problem": "It does not disturb the primary stdio client, but it is not side-effect-free. The IPC server counts the probe as a secondary connection, calls onActivity, and can refuse it when max secondary clients is reached. A refused probe is indistinguishable from a dead daemon to the caller, so a saturated but healthy bridge can be false-classified dead unless the health path treats max-client/refused separately.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5361:      "target": "GLM rec: live-but-dead-socket is reclaimable",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5362:      "problem": "Correct only after a startup-state gate exists. Today a legitimate first launcher owns the lease before the bootstrap lock and first npm/tsc build complete, so a second launcher can see a live owner with no socket while startup is still valid. A raw no-socket=>reclaim rule would kill a real startup.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5363:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1060"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5366:      "target": "GLM rec: no-bridge-socket should route into respawnAfterDeadSocket",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5367:      "problem": "As a generic bridge change, this is incomplete. respawnAfterDeadSocket for code-index refuses to act without options.respawnChildPid; the PID-lease path calls bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld without that option, so a missing socket there would still degrade to dead-socket-no-child-pid unless the PID lease records childPid or a registry supplies it.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5368:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:674"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5372:      "problem": "Refuted for the 'lease file gone' incident variant. reclaimedOrphanPid is populated only from a reclaimed owner lease, and the orphan reap runs only when that value exists. If both lease artifacts are gone, socket-health classification has no PID to classify or reap.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5373:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1060"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5376:      "target": "GLM rec: launcher can checkpoint WAL at startup because no other writer holds it",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5377:      "problem": "That premise is not guaranteed by the current launcher. The launcher normally never opens SQLite; the child does in initDb. The bootstrap lock serializes launchers, not a still-live or orphaned child. Checkpointing is safe only after exclusive daemon ownership is proven or the old owner is reaped/absent; otherwise it can contend with the live child writer and produce SQLITE_BUSY or a startup stall.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5381:      "target": "GLM rec: childSpawnedAt grace fully solves startup math",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5382:      "problem": "childSpawnedAt is necessary but not sufficient. There is a pre-child phase where the lease owner is the launcher and no childSpawnedAt can exist yet; that phase includes bootstrap-lock wait and first build. The design needs an explicit pre-spawn/build state and deadline, not only childSpawnedAt-based daemon startup grace.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5383:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1166"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5387:      "problem": "Only if diagnostics actually collect the missing facts. Current code has no lsof/wal holder probe and no unified diagnostic line; adding reason tokens without walHeldBy/source-of-pid fields would still leave the exact incident requiring manual ps/lsof/WAL inspection.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5388:      "evidence": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1191"
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5392:    "The bridge file path in the prompt/design is stale: code-index loads .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs via loadBridgeModule, not .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs (.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:164).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5393:    "No deterministic test currently covers live PID + owner lease + missing socket file + beyond startup deadline. Existing code-index respawn tests kill the owner daemon, which exercises stale/dead ownership, not the PID-alive/socket-absent wedge (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tests/code-index-cli-owner-respawn.vitest.ts:29).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5395:    "The child starts refreshing the owner lease after server.connect but before startIpcSocketServer completes, so heartbeat freshness can still precede socket availability (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/index.ts:136, .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/index.ts:149).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5396:    "The owner lease TypeScript shape does not include socketPath, while the launcher writes and expects it. Runtime preserves extra fields today, but the contract is implicit and fragile (.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/owner-lease.ts:23, .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:419)."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5400:      "change": "Add explicit owner lease fields for launcherPid, daemonPid, launcherStartedAtIso, childSpawnedAtIso, startupPhase, and socketPath; classify socketless owners as pre-spawn, starting-child, live-serving, or dead-socket instead of overloading live-owner.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5401:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:buildOwnerLease/classifyOwnerLease/launchServer",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5405:      "change": "Make missing-socket respawn policy owned by the launcher, not only the shared bridge helper: maybeBridgeLeaseHolder can return no-bridge-socket, and code-index should decide respawn only when it has a verified daemonPid and the startup deadline has expired.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5406:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5407:      "why": "The shared bridge cannot know whether a missing socket means pre-child build, missing childPid, stale PID lease, or a reclaimable daemon."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5410:      "change": "Record childPid in the PID lease or add the crash-surviving registry to P0, not later. On missing leases, use the registry plus lsof/fuser on code-graph.sqlite-wal/sqlite, with PID identity checks, before spawning a new writer.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5411:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:writeLeaseFile/launchServer/launcherMain",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5412:      "why": "Socket-health checks cannot recover the incident variant where the lease file is gone."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5415:      "change": "Gate WAL checkpoint behind exclusive ownership: after owner lease acquisition, after any orphan reap, and before launchServer. Use a bounded busy_timeout and treat SQLITE_BUSY as a diagnostic, not an unhandled launcher crash. Also set PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint=500 in initDb.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5416:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain and .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:initDb",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5417:      "why": "This gives WAL cleanup without creating an unsafe second writer or wedging startup on a live child."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5420:      "change": "Add one JSON-line diagnostic containing reason, ownerPid, launcherPid, daemonPid, socketPath, startupPhase, probeReason, walBytes, walHeldBy, and action.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5421:      "where": ".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:failure/lease-held/respawn branches",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-011.md:5422:      "why": "The current logs explain symptoms but not the state needed to distinguish dead socket, pre-spawn build, orphan WAL, PID reuse, and foreign owner."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-006.md:5:{"angle":"heartbeat-vs-socket","insights":["The 2-min reclaim (classifyOwnerLease L465-469) fires only when a lease FILE exists with lastHeartbeatIso older than ttlMs*2 (120s). It never ran here because the lease file was already gone — refreshOwnerLeaseFile/CAS and stale-heartbeat both depend on a present, owned file; with no file, acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L474-515) sees existing=null and writes a fresh lease immediately. No aging, no reclaim event.","The heartbeat is refreshed by the LAUNCHER process (startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat setInterval L399-406), and refreshOwnerLeaseFile (L517-532) gates ONLY on file ownership (lease.ownerPid === ownerPid). It never probes the IPC socket. So two distinct wedge modes both bypass reclaim: (a) launcher alive but daemon wedged → heartbeat stays fresh → classifyOwnerLease returns 'live-owner' (Option 1); (b) launcher exited cleanly → process.on('exit', clearAllLeaseFiles) L1053 deletes the lease → nothing to age out (Option 2, the actual incident).","The orphan-reap safety net (launcherMain L1174-1180) keys entirely off ownerLeaseResult.reclaimed.ownerPid. reclaimed is set only when acquireOwnerLeaseFile overwrote a STALE existing lease. A clean-exited launcher leaves existing=null → reclaimed stays null → reclaimedOrphanPid never populated → the reparented-to-init daemon is invisible and keeps the SQLite DB/WAL locked. That is the 17MB WAL with no holder.","classifyOwnerLease has a ppid-1-orphan branch (L460-463) that WOULD catch a reparented child, but it is unreachable here: it only runs against an existing lease, and the lease was already deleted by the launcher's exit handler. The orphan detector is gated behind the very artifact that vanished.","ownerSocketPath (L371-379) records the socket path in the lease, and maybeBridgeLeaseHolder/respawnAfterDeadSocket (L668-717) CAN detect a dead socket and respawn — but only when a SECONDARY launcher finds a HELD lease. With the lease gone, bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld is never called; the dead-socket probe never executes. Liveness and socket-serving are on completely separate code paths.","processLiveness (L423-433) is a kill(pid,0) check — it answers 'is the kernel struct alive', not 'is the daemon serving'. A 0%-CPU wedged process passes alive forever."],"recommendations":[{"change":"Make the heartbeat refresh socket-gated: in the setInterval callback (L399-406) and refreshOwnerLeaseFile, probe the recorded socket (stat + a non-blocking connect/echo) before writing lastHeartbeatIso. On probe failure, STOP heartbeating (call clearOwnerLeaseHeartbeat) and let the lease age out, so a dead-socket daemon becomes stale-heartbeat-reclaim within 2 min.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat L394-408 and refreshOwnerLeaseFile L517-532","why":"Liveness then means 'can serve IPC', not 'PID exists'. This closes Option 1 (launcher-alive/socket-dead wedge) directly. The existing classifyOwnerLease stale branch becomes effective for socket-dead owners."},{"change":"Move heartbeat ownership from the launcher process to the daemon child: have the spawned MCP server (system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js) own a self-ping loop that refreshes lastHeartbeatIso only after its IPC listener accepts/echoes a request. The launcher should stop heartbeating once launchServer hands off (or refresh only as a degenerate fallback).","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launchServer L955-1008 (handoff) + system-code-graph/mcp_server owner-lease module","why":"If the launcher dies cleanly the lease stops aging correctly (the daemon is the thing that can actually serve). Survives launcher crash/SIGKILL where exit handlers don't run but the daemon keeps serving — and crucially, a wedged daemon stops refreshing, aging itself out even when its PID is alive."},{"change":"Add a socket-served predicate to classifyOwnerLease alongside processLiveness: when socketPath is present and stat says the socket file is absent, return 'dead-socket' (treat as reclaimable) BEFORE the heartbeat age check. Mirror this in leaseHeldFromFile (L582-610) so isLeaseHeld reports staleReclaimable on missing-socket-but-live-pid.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease L455-472 and leaseHeldFromFile L582-610","why":"Defense in depth: even if a heartbeat stays fresh, a missing socket should classify the owner as reclaimable so acquireOwnerLeaseFile reclaims and the existing respawn/dead-socket path engages. Closes the gap where 'live-owner' is returned for a non-serving owner."},{"change":"Make the orphan-reap path independent of the lease file: before writing a fresh lease in acquireOwnerLeaseFile when existing is null, scan for a live daemon via the recorded socket path absence OR a known startup-PID file written by the daemon itself (not cleared on launcher exit). Reap any process holding the SQLite DB before spawning a successor.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:acquireOwnerLeaseFile L474-515 and launcherMain orphan-reap L1174-1180","why":"Closes Option 2: a clean-exited launcher deletes the lease but leaves a reparented daemon holding the DB. A PID/socket artifact owned by the DAEMON (not cleared by the launcher's exit handler) gives the reclaim path a target when the owner lease is gone."},{"change":"Run a socket liveness probe (connect + send a zero-length/ping frame, expect a defined response or at least accept) inside refreshOwnerLeaseFile before stamping the heartbeat. Treat ECONNREFUSED/ENOENT on the socket path as a failed refresh (return false) so the lease ages out instead of being kept fresh.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:refreshOwnerLeaseFile L517-532","why":"Aligns the existing 2-min TTL with real serving ability without adding new state machines. A wedged-but-alive daemon fails the probe → heartbeat stalls → classifyOwnerLease crosses the 2*ttlMs threshold and reclaims."}],"risks":["A socket probe inside the heartbeat interval adds I/O to the hot path every ttlMs/2 (30s); keep it non-blocking with a short timeout (e.g. 250ms) and treat timeout as probe-failure to avoid a slow socket masquerading as healthy.","Moving heartbeat to the daemon means a daemon that can't reach its own lease file (permissions, disk full) stops refreshing and gets reclaimed even while serving correctly over a live socket — the probe must be the source of truth, and a lease-write failure should NOT be the trigger; decouple 'serving' from 'writable'.","The existing bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld/respawnAfterDeadSocket logic and the session-proxy replay machinery assume a held lease. Tightening classifyOwnerLease to return dead-socket earlier will route more cases through respawn; verify respawnAfterDeadSocket's reap grace (RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS=7000) is enough for a wedged process to die on SIGTERM before SIGKILL, or it may force-kill a daemon mid-checkpoint and re-introduce WAL orphans.","The ppid-1-orphan branch (L460-463) and the new socket checks overlap; a reparented wedged daemon could match multiple classifications. Define precedence explicitly (dead-pid > dead-socket > ppid-orphan > stale-heartbeat > live) to avoid ambiguous reclaim decisions racing two launchers.","A new daemon-owned PID/socket artifact that survives launcher exit must be cleaned by the daemon's OWN exit handler, not the launcher's — otherwise you trade 'lease cleared too eagerly' for 'PID file leaks across reboots and falsely reports a dead PID as owner.'"]}
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:5:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:10:    "The probe you want ALREADY EXISTS: launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:probeDaemon({deepProbe:true}) (L150-236) connects to the UDS, sends JSON-RPC initialize, and only returns 'alive' on a matching result/error frame. A wedged daemon (event loop blocked, 0% CPU) accepts the connection but never replies → timeout → 'dead'. connect-ok-only is explicitly NOT trusted for the reap/bridge decision (see the comment L155-157). This is the exact semantics the incident needs; the gap is WHERE it is invoked.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:11:    "probeDaemon is only called today inside maybeBridgeLeaseHolder (L365-442), which runs AFTER a lease file is confirmed held. In the incident the lease file was GONE, so neither acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L474) nor isLeaseHeld (L612) entered the bridge path; launcherMain fell straight through to writeLeaseFile()+launchServer() (L1182-1189) and spawned a SECOND writer against a DB still occupied by the wedged first daemon → orphaned 17MB WAL.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:12:    "processLiveness (L423-433) is process.kill(pid,0) — it cannot distinguish 'serving' from 'wedged-but-alive'. Every classification in the file (classifyOwnerLease L455, leaseHeldFromFile L582, pidLiveAt L565, removeStaleBootstrapLock L890) is PID-only. The 2-min stale-heartbeat-reclaim (L467, ttlMs*2) is the only wedge detector, and it ONLY fires if the owner lease file PERSISTS; a deleted/corrupt lease file short-circuits it entirely (readOwnerLeaseFile returns null → acquireOwnerLeaseFile treats it as fresh acquire).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:13:    "Error-code semantics from probeDaemon are already diagnostic: connect ENOENT = socket file absent (no server ever bound, or cleaned up); ECONNREFUSED = stale socket file left behind by a dead server (server gone, fs entry remains — must unlink before bind or the new server hits EADDRINUSE); timeout = wedged server holding the socket AND the DB (the dangerous case); ECONNREFUSED-with-no-PID = safe to reap-and-spawn. The incident was timeout-or-ENOENT-with-orphaned-WAL: PID alive, no socket, lease gone.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:14:    "The unfixable-without-extra-discovery problem: when the lease file is gone, you have no PID to reap. probeDaemon returning 'timeout' tells you SOMETHING is listening on that path, but you need the PID to kill it. The lease-leasePath/writeLeaseFile (L756) writes socketPath into the PID lease, and buildOwnerLease (L410) writes it into the owner lease — but if both are deleted, the socket path must be recomputed via getIpcSocketPath + you must enumerate the holder (lsof/fuser on the socket inode, or scan for node processes running dist/index.js with the matching dbDir).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:20:      "where": ".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:probeDaemon (L150) — enrich the finish() calls with a kind field; export a thin classify wrapper.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:21:      "why": "Callers (launcherMain, classifyOwnerLease) need to branch on WHY the probe failed, not just dead/alive. ENOENT vs ECONNREFUSED vs timeout demand different recovery actions (spawn vs unlink-socket-then-spawn vs reap-PID-then-spawn)."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:24:      "change": "Insert a socket probe into launcherMain's no-lease-held path. After the strictSingleWriter block determines no owner/PID lease exists (around L1097-1100, just before acquireBootstrapLock at L1102), compute socketPath=getIpcSocketPath('mk-code-index',{dbDir}) and run probeLeaseHolderWithRetries with deepProbe:true. If kind==='alive' → a daemon IS serving but lost its lease files; bridge to it (call bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld with a synthesized leaseResult) instead of spawning a second writer. If kind==='econnrefused' → unlink the stale socket file before launchServer so the new bind succeeds. If kind==='timeout' → a wedged daemon holds the DB; MUST discover and reap its PID (see next rec) before spawning, else double-writer corruption. If kind==='enoent' → clean slate, proceed to spawn.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:25:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain (L1039), insert between the strictSingleWriter decision block exit (L1100) and acquireBootstrapLock (L1102).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:26:      "why": "This is the exact incident path: lease file gone → no reclaim → blind spawn into an occupied DB. A socket probe here is the only thing that catches a wedged daemon whose lease files were deleted."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:29:      "change": "Add discoverSocketHolderPid(socketPath) that resolves the PID owning the listening socket when no lease file records it. On darwin/linux use `lsof -t -c node <socketPath>` (or `fuser <socketPath>` on linux) filtered to processes whose argv contains the code-graph dist/index.js and whose cwd/env dbDir matches resolvedDbDir(). Fall back to null when unavailable. Feed the discovered PID into reapOwnerBeforeRespawn (L640) which already handles SIGTERM-grace-then-SIGKILL. If discovery returns null and probe is timeout, HARD BLOCK the spawn (log + exit 0 like the reclaimedOrphanPid EPERM path at L1177) rather than risk a double writer — require manual recovery.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:30:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs: new helper near readParentPid (L435); invoke from the new launcherMain probe branch; route output to reapOwnerBeforeRespawn (L640).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:31:      "why": "A wedged daemon with no lease file is unreapable without PID discovery. Spawning alongside it corrupts SQLite (the orphaned 17MB WAL in the incident). When you cannot prove the holder is dead, blocking is safer than racing two writers."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:34:      "change": "Strengthen classifyOwnerLease so a PID-alive owner is not classified 'live-owner' until the socket deep-probe confirms it is SERVING. Currently processLiveness=='alive' (L456) gates straight to the heartbeat check; a wedged owner with a fresh heartbeat returns 'live-owner' and blocks acquireOwnerLeaseFile, deferring detection to the next secondary's bridge-probe. Add: if liveness==='alive', run probeLeaseHolderWithRetries on lease.socketPath (or recomputed path); timeout/ECONNREFUSED → return 'wedged-owner' (a new classification) so acquireOwnerLeaseFile reclaims immediately instead of waiting for the 2-min heartbeat TTL.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:35:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease (L455-472), add the socket probe after the ppid-1-orphan check (L463) and before the heartbeat check (L465); make classifyOwnerLease async OR run the probe synchronously-via-spawnSync-lsof+short-socket-read if async is not viable at this callsite.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:36:      "why": "Cuts wedge-detection latency from ttlMs*2 (~2min) to one probe round (~5s). The bridge path already does this, but only for secondaries; the reclaim path trusts PID liveness alone."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:39:      "change": "After a timeout-classified probe, before respawning, run wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on the DB (or document that launchServer's child must do it on cold start when it detects no concurrent writer). The incident's 17MB orphaned WAL indicates the wedged daemon never checkpointed. A pre-spawn checkpoint (gated on exclusive ownership after successful reap) prevents a respawned daemon inheriting a bloated WAL that inflates every read.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:40:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:respawnAfterDeadSocket (L668), after clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner (L698) and before buildIfNeeded (L708); or in the new launcherMain probe branch after a confirmed reap.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:      "why": "The WAL is a symptom; reclaiming the lease without checkpointing leaves the new daemon replaying a large WAL under load."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:45:    "Adding a deep probe to every launcherMain cold path adds up to resolveLeaseProbeAttempts()*timeoutMs (~10s worst case for a wedged daemon) before spawn. Mitigate: short-circuit on ENOENT (socket file absent → no probe, proceed immediately); only deep-probe when fs.existsSync(socketPath) is true. The healthy case stays O(connect+initialize-reply) ≈ tens of ms.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:46:    "classifyOwnerLease is currently synchronous; making it async ripples into acquireOwnerLeaseFile and launcherMain (already async, low cost) but also into emitLeaseMetric/staleReclaimed paths. If async is undesirable, the socket probe there must be a blocking spawnSync('node', probe-script) — slower and uglier. Prefer making acquireOwnerLeaseFile async and leaving classifyOwnerLease as a pure-PID pre-filter, with the socket probe as a second async gate inside acquireOwnerLeaseFile after the PID-alive branch.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:47:    "lsof/fuser PID discovery is non-portable and may be absent in sandboxes; if discovery returns null on a timeout-probe, the launcher must HARD BLOCK (not spawn) — which converts a self-healing daemon into a manual-recovery-required state for that edge. Document the recovery command (kill the node holding the socket, wal_checkpoint TRUNCATE, rm the socket) so operators aren't guessing.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:48:    "TCP socket mode (SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=tcp://) leaves no filesystem artifact, so the ENOENT-vs-ECONNREFUSED distinction collapses to ECONNREFUSED/timeout; the stale-socket-unlink step (recommendation 2) is a no-op there and the bind-collision risk is lower (OS reuses ports), but PID discovery via lsof-on-a-file is impossible — must lsof on host:port, which may match unrelated processes.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:49:    "Race window between probe-alive and spawn: the daemon could die after the probe returns alive but before the bridge attaches. The existing session-proxy (bridgeStdioThroughSessionProxy, L207) already handles reattach-on-reconnect, so a client bridged to a just-died daemon will be replayed on respawn — but only if the respawn path fires. Ensure the bridge's onError (L437) triggers the same respawn-decision as a fresh launcherMain probe, not just a diagnostic line.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:50:    "wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) from the launcher requires a SQLite handle and must run AFTER the wedged PID is provably dead (reapOwnerBeforeRespawn success) and BEFORE the new child opens the DB — otherwise it either fails (DB locked) or races the child's open. Safest: have the child run PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) as its first DB statement on cold start when it detects it is the sole writer (no live lease), keeping the launcher out of SQLite entirely."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:16:You are GPT-5.5 at xhigh reasoning doing an ADVERSARIAL verification pass over a hardening design produced by a different model (GLM-5.2). Be skeptical: your job is to CONFIRM what is right, REFUTE what is wrong, and find GAPS the prior research missed — not to agree. Read the synthesized design at .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md and the actual source .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs and .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs (and the child initDb under system-code-graph/mcp_server if useful). Ground every claim in the REAL code (cite function/line). Incident recap: the code-index daemon wedged (PID alive, IPC socket never created, lease file gone, 17MB orphaned WAL) and /mcp reconnect returned -32000.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:19:Verify GLM's specific code citations against the REAL files. Confirm or correct: probeDaemon({deepProbe:true}) exists in .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs around L150-236 and actually sends a JSON-RPC initialize over the UDS; the inert no-bridge-socket 'report' branch exists around L405-408; leaseHeldFromFile returns held:true after process.kill(pid,0) succeeds (~L602) with no socket check; classifyOwnerLease's state set (stale-pid / stale-heartbeat-reclaim / unknown-eperm) and the reclaim CAS (unlink + O_EXCL) are as described. Flag ANY hallucinated line number, non-existent function, or mischaracterized behavior — list each with the corrected reality.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:132:| Surface style/process conventions | `sk-code` detected surface | Surface guidance overrides baseline generic style/process advice |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:257:/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs | sed -n '1,760p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:259:nl: .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs: No such file or directory
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:266:     3	**Subject:** Prevent recurrence of the Code Graph daemon wedge — a PID-alive-but-socket-dead daemon the launcher does not reclaim, causing MCP reconnect `-32000`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:268:     5	**Target file:** `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` (+ `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` `initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:272:     9	Liveness is **bidimensional** — `processLiveness` proves only `process.kill(pid,0)` (PID exists), and the heartbeat is written by the **launcher**, not gated on the daemon's ability to serve. There is **zero socket-health correlation**. So a wedged daemon (PID alive, 0% CPU, socket never (re)created) classifies as `live-owner` / `held:true`. The inert branch is `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder`'s no-socket path (bridge `L405-408`): when the lease says "held" but the socket FILE is missing, it `report`s instead of respawning. Two compounding axes in the incident: (a) the lease file had also vanished from an empty socket dir, so the heartbeat-reclaim (which needs a lease file with an aging `lastHeartbeatIso`) never had anything to age out; (b) a 17 MB orphaned WAL from the unclean crash.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:278:    15	1. **Reuse the existing probe.** `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:probeDaemon({deepProbe:true})` (~L150-236) already connects the UDS + sends a JSON-RPC `initialize`. Wrap it as `probeExistingService(socketPath)` returning `{status, kind}` where `kind ∈ {enoent, econnrefused, timeout, json-rpc-reply}`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:279:    16	2. **Socket-health gate in classification.** Add a `live-but-dead-socket` state to `classifyOwnerLease` and mirror it in `leaseHeldFromFile` (after the `process.kill(pid,0)` success at `~L602`): if `socketPath` is missing/dead (fast-path `!existsSync`, else N-consecutive deep-probe failures), return `staleReclaimable:true` instead of `held:true`. Extend the reclaimable allowlist (`~L479-484`) — the existing `unlink + O_EXCL` CAS handles it atomically, no CAS change.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:280:    17	3. **No-bridge-socket → respawn, not report.** Change bridge `L405-408` to route a socketless-but-live owner into the existing `respawnAfterDeadSocket → reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` pipeline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:281:    18	4. **Startup grace window** (so a *starting* daemon isn't killed): `STARTUP_GRACE_MS=30000`, `MAX_INIT_MS=120000` (env-overridable), keyed on a new `childSpawnedAtIso` stamped right after the child PID is known — NOT `startedAtIso` (which precedes the bootstrap-lock wait + first build). `age ≤ grace` → still-starting (report); `grace < age ≤ max` → bounded socket re-poll; `age > max` → respawn (`startup-deadline-exceeded`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:282:    19	5. **Socket-gated heartbeat.** Refresh `lastHeartbeatIso` only when the socket is actually serving; on probe failure, stop heartbeating so a dead-socket daemon ages out into `stale-heartbeat-reclaim` within 2 min. Ideally move heartbeat ownership into the daemon child (refresh only after its IPC listener echoes).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:283:    20	6. **Crash-surviving PID registry + self-heal on acquire.** Write `.code-graph-daemon-pid.json` (`{daemonPid, launcherPid, socketPath, startedAt, canonicalDbDir}`) right after the child PID is known; unlink only on clean child exit. On acquire, discover the orphan via `reclaimedOrphanPid → that registry → lsof on the WAL`, and reap (SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL) **only** when `alive AND socket provably dead (N consecutive failures)**, then checkpoint, then spawn.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:284:    21	7. **WAL startup hygiene.** Before spawn: if `code-graph.sqlite-wal` exceeds a threshold (~8 MB) or an orphan was just reaped, run `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` (no other writer holds it). Set `wal_autocheckpoint=500` (~2 MB, tunable) in `initDb`. Checkpoint before the migration copy so a giant `-wal` is never propagated.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:285:    22	8. **Race + permission safety.** Apply the uid check to the PRIMARY lease (not just `legacyPath`) and to the socket owner — never unlink/bridge/kill a foreign-owned lease. Before any SIGKILL, verify PID identity (cmdline basename + process start-time vs `startedAtIso`) to avoid killing a PID-reuse victim; on mismatch log `pid-reuse-suspected` and abort. Re-stat the lease between classify and unlink (abort if changed). Gate self-heal behind a one-shot `.self-heal-attempted` marker so a failed heal never loops into a kill storm.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:286:    23	9. **One-line diagnostics.** Emit `LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC: reason=<token> ownerPid=<n> socketPath=<p> walHeldBy=<pid|none>` on every failure exit. Tokens: `dead-socket-reclaimed`, `startup-timeout`, `foreign-owner`, `wal-locked-by-orphan`, `bridge-socket-refused`, `pid-reuse-suspected`, `stale-heartbeat-reclaimed`. Replaces manual lsof/ps forensics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:290:    27	- **Wedge → reclaim:** spawn a fake owner that writes the lease + holds a live PID but never opens the socket; past `MAX_INIT_MS`, assert the launcher reclaims (reaps + respawns) and emits `dead-socket-reclaimed`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:292:    29	- **Foreign-owner → not killed:** lease/socket owned by a different uid → assert no signal, `foreign-owner` diagnostic.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:293:    30	- **PID-reuse → not killed:** live PID whose cmdline/start-time mismatch the lease → `pid-reuse-suspected`, abort.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:294:    31	- **Oversized WAL → checkpointed:** seed an 8MB+ `-wal` with no holder → assert `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` runs pre-spawn and WAL shrinks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:295:    32	- **Healthy owner → bridged:** live PID + serving socket → assert bridge, no reclaim.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:299:    36	Implement components **1-3 + 4 + 7** as the P0 core (they directly close the incident class), then **6, 8, 9** as the durability/safety layer, then **5** (heartbeat) as defense-in-depth. The minimal P0 (socket-health gate + reclaim-on-dead-socket past a grace window + startup WAL hygiene + diagnostics) collapses both failure axes from the incident.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:306:    43	- Launcher under study: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, and the child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` (`initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:307:    44	- source: live incident forensics, 2026-06-29 — orphaned PIDs 37269/37358 at 0% CPU, empty `/tmp/mk-code-index/`, a 17 MB `code-graph.sqlite-wal` with no holder, DB integrity ok; manual recovery via `kill` + `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` + socket-dir clean.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:3396:- P0 for breaking changes to public APIs or shared interfaces with no migration path.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:3437:{"angle":"code-claim-verification","confirms":["GLM rec 1 is correct in behavior but path-corrected: the real `probeDaemon(socketPath, options)` is in `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:150-236`; it builds a JSON-RPC `initialize` request at lines 159-168, writes it only for `deepProbe` at lines 197-203, and treats a matching JSON-RPC result/error reply as alive at lines 213-222.","GLM's no-bridge-socket inert branch is real in the live bridge module: `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder` computes `socketPath`, then if a non-TCP socket file is absent it writes `LEASE_HELD_BY ... (no-bridge-socket)` and returns `{action:'report', reason:'no-bridge-socket'}` at `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:404-408`.","GLM's `leaseHeldFromFile` claim is correct: it extracts a recorded `socketPath`, but after `process.kill(lease.pid, 0)` succeeds it returns `held:true` with no socket existence or serving check at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:582-604`.","GLM's core liveness critique is correct: `classifyOwnerLease` checks PID liveness, EPERM, PPID orphaning, and heartbeat age, then returns `live-owner`; it does not inspect `socketPath` or probe the daemon at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:455-472`.","GLM's socket-gated-heartbeat recommendation is justified: after spawn, the launcher parent rewrites the owner lease to the child PID and starts a parent-owned heartbeat at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:973-982`; that heartbeat calls `refreshOwnerLeaseFile` without any socket probe at lines 394-407, and the refresh always stamps `lastHeartbeatIso` at lines 517-525.","GLM's statement that the reclaim path uses unlink plus exclusive create is correct: stale owner leases are unlinked at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:491-494`, and `writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive` uses `fs.openSync(currentLeasePath, 'wx', 0o600)` at lines 351-358."],"refutes":[{"target":"GLM path citation `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`","problem":"The named top-level file is not the live source. The launcher requires `./lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`; GLM's line numbers match the lib file, not `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`.","evidence":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:164-167"},{"target":"`probeDaemon` returning `{status, kind}` / normalized `enoent|econnrefused|timeout|json-rpc-reply`","problem":"Current `probeDaemon` returns `{status, reason}`. It has `json-rpc-reply` and `timeout`, but socket errors are raw `error.message` strings, not a normalized `kind` enum. A wrapper is still needed if callers require stable failure categories.","evidence":".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:179-184"},{"target":"GLM's abbreviated `classifyOwnerLease` state set: `stale-pid / stale-heartbeat-reclaim / unknown-eperm`","problem":"Incomplete. The real classifier also returns `ppid-1-orphan` and `live-owner`. Also, reclaim is implemented as 'anything except live-owner or unknown-eperm', not an explicit reclaimable allowlist.","evidence":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:455-483"},{"target":"`unlink + O_EXCL` is an atomic CAS needing no CAS change","problem":"Overstated. `O_EXCL` ensures only one creator wins after unlink, but the code does not re-stat or compare the stale lease between classification and unlink. A successor lease written after the read can still be unlinked by path.","evidence":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:476-494"},{"target":"Existing dead-socket respawn pipeline fully covers held PID leases","problem":"Not for the PID lease path. If `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder` returns `action:'respawn'` for a held `.mk-code-index-launcher.json` lease, `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld` is called without `respawnChildPid`; `respawnAfterDeadSocket` then reports `missing-child-pid` instead of reaping/spawning.","evidence":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:668-679"}],"gaps":["The incident case 'lease file gone but orphaned daemon PID alive' is still under-covered by current code. `reclaimedOrphanPid` is populated only when `acquireOwnerLeaseFile` actually reclaims an existing owner lease; if the owner lease vanished, there is no registry/lsof fallback to find the WAL holder before spawning. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1048-1067`.","There is no startup grace/deadline in the bridge decision. Missing socket reports forever at `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:405-408`; existing-but-unresponsive sockets go to respawn after retry probes at lines 417-428, without checking spawn age.","Primary lease and owner lease safety are weaker than GLM's safety target: uid checking exists only for legacy PID lease paths, not primary PID lease, owner lease, or socket owner. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:591-598`.","PID identity is not verified before signals. `reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` checks liveness, then sends SIGTERM/SIGKILL to the numeric PID without cmdline/start-time validation, so PID reuse remains a real safety gap. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:640-665`.","WAL hygiene is not implemented in `initDb`: it sets `busy_timeout`, `journal_mode = WAL`, and `foreign_keys`, but no `wal_autocheckpoint`; checkpointing exists only in rollback recovery, not launcher startup or migration. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:1033-1040` and `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/recovery-procedures.ts:313-319`."],"new_recommendations":[{"change":"Add a real `probeExistingService(socketPath)` wrapper that first distinguishes absent socket, connect failure, timeout, and JSON-RPC reply, returning stable machine tokens.","where":".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:150-236","why":"`probeDaemon` has the right deep probe mechanics, but its current `reason` strings are not stable enough for reclaim policy or diagnostics."},{"change":"Add socket health and startup-age policy to both owner-lease and PID-lease classification, with a grace window before any reap and a hard deadline after which dead sockets become reclaimable.","where":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:455-604","why":"The current classifiers prove PID/heartbeat only; that is the exact wedge class from the incident."},{"change":"Change the no-socket branch from unconditional `report` to a policy decision: report during startup grace, otherwise respawn/reclaim when owner identity and uid are safe.","where":".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:404-408 and .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:719-743","why":"A missing socket with a live owner is currently inert, so reconnect can keep surfacing failure instead of self-healing."},{"change":"Harden owner lease reclaim by re-reading/statting the lease before unlink, comparing owner PID/heartbeat/inode or using a mutation lock/rename-to-claim pattern.","where":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:474-514","why":"Current unlink-by-path plus `O_EXCL` serializes creators after deletion but does not protect a fresh successor lease from being deleted."},{"change":"Record child PID identity and add PID-reuse checks before SIGTERM/SIGKILL; include uid checks for primary lease, owner lease, and socket path.","where":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:640-665","why":"The design's safety layer is necessary; current code can signal a reused PID and only legacy PID leases get uid filtering."},{"change":"Add startup WAL hygiene: checkpoint/truncate oversized WAL when no writer is alive, set `wal_autocheckpoint=500` in `initDb`, and do not migrate/copy a giant WAL without checkpointing first.","where":".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:1102-1159 and .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/code-graph-db.ts:1027-1040","why":"The incident included a 17MB orphaned WAL, and current startup/migration code can preserve that failure residue."}],"verdict":"sound-with-fixes"}
