.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:9:    "The uid check in leaseHeldFromFile (L591-599) is gated on `legacyPath` only, so the PRIMARY lease (.code-graph-owner.json / .mk-code-index-launcher.json in skill-local dbDir) and its recorded socketPath are trusted without any ownership verification. On a shared/multi-user repo volume, a foreign user's primary lease is treated as a live owner, we bridge to a foreign-owned socket, and acquireOwnerLeaseFile will unlink a foreign lease file (L493) we happen to have permission to delete.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:10:    "acquireOwnerLeaseFile's CAS is unlink-then-O_EXCL (L491-505). The win-once property holds (loser hits EEXIST at L498), but the unlink at L493 is DESTRUCTIVE and runs on a lease that was only classified stale a few syscalls earlier — there is no re-stat of mtime/inode between classifyOwnerLease (L479) and unlink (L493), so an owner whose heartbeat just refreshed (startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat fires every ttlMs/2 = 30s) can have its now-live lease ripped out from under it.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:11:    "refreshOwnerLeaseFile (L517-532) writes via writeOwnerLeaseFile, which is tmp+rename and NON-exclusive (L344-349). Its post-write re-read (L528-529) only detects 'another owner wrote between my read and write' — it does NOT detect 'I just clobbered the winner's lease with my own pid'. After a concurrent reclaim, the dying owner's heartbeat rename overwrites the winner's O_EXCL lease, re-read sees its OWN pid, and returns true (success). This is a plausible mechanism for the incident's 'lease file gone / pointing at dead owner while a second daemon spawns' signature.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:12:    "processLiveness (L423-433) maps EPERM → 'unknown-eperm', and classifyOwnerLease (L458) + acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L480) treat that as untouchable-live. This is safe against foreign-process kills but is NON-self-healing: a wedged owner in a sandbox that returns EPERM on kill(0) can never be reclaimed, so the wedge persists exactly as observed (0% CPU, socket never recreated). There is no socket-liveness tiebreaker to escape this.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:13:    "PID reuse is unguarded before SIGTERM/SIGKILL. reapOwnerBeforeRespawn (L640-666) and the reclaimedOrphanPid reap (L1175) signal the recorded pid with only processLiveness as the gate. buildOwnerLease records executablePath and startedAtIso (L410-421), but nothing re-verifies them against the live process before kill. The PID lease (.mk-code-index-launcher.json via writeLeaseFile L756-761) is even weaker: it stores only pid + startedAt, no executable/token, so a reused PID is indistinguishable from the original holder.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:14:    "classifyOwnerLease's 'ppid-1-orphan' branch (L461-463) conflates legitimate reparent-to-init (the orphan we WANT to reap after the launcher dies) with PID reuse in a container where every process has ppid=1. Combined with the missing cmdline/start-time check, a reused PID with ppid=1 in a container is classified reclaimable and then SIGKILLed as an unrelated process.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:15:    "The stale-heartbeat-reclaim threshold (L467, ttlMs*2 = 120s) is the incident's most likely trigger: a long synchronous SQLite op, a GC pause, or a stalled event loop > 2 min flips a healthy-but-slow owner into reclaimable. The heartbeat interval (ttlMs/2 = 30s) offers no margin against a multi-second event-loop stall, and there is no 'grace extension' on a partial stall."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:19:      "change": "Lift the uid check out of the `if (legacyPath)` guard so it applies to the PRIMARY lease too; also stat the recorded socketPath's owner uid before bridging. Refuse to unlink or bridge any lease whose file uid != process.getuid().",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:20:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:leaseHeldFromFile L591-599 (move uid check before the legacyPath branch) and acquireOwnerLeaseFile L491-497 (guard the unlink) and bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld/ownerSocketPath consumer L719-727",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:21:      "why": "Removes the spoofed-IPC-socket and foreign-lease-unlink exposure on shared volumes; the legacy-only gate was a relic of the relocation, not a security boundary."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:24:      "change": "Before any SIGTERM/SIGKILL in reapOwnerBeforeRespawn, verify PID identity: read /proc/PID/cmdline (Linux) or `ps -o command=` (macOS) and assert it contains lease.executablePath's basename; additionally compare the process start time against lease.startedAtIso (ps -o lstart= or /proc/PID/stat boottime-relative). On mismatch, treat as PID-reuse, do NOT signal, log 'pid-reuse-suspected' and exit the reclaim path.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:25:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:reapOwnerBeforeRespawn L640-666 (insert identity check after processLiveness, before L651 SIGTERM); reuse the helper at the reclaimedOrphanPid reap site L1174-1180",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:26:      "why": "Closes the PID-reuse → SIGKILL-unrelated-process hole; both the PID lease (pid-only) and the owner lease currently trust pid alone."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:29:      "change": "Make the stale-lease reclaim non-destructive: replace unlink-then-O_EXCL with a generation-pointer scheme (write new lease to .code-graph-owner.json.gen.<nonce>, then atomically link/rename it in only if the current lease still matches the stale snapshot's mtime+ownerPid). At minimum, re-stat the lease (mtime + size + ownerPid) between classifyOwnerLease and unlink; if changed, re-classify and abort the reclaim.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:30:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:acquireOwnerLeaseFile L491-505",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:34:      "change": "Make refreshOwnerLeaseFile fail-fast on ANY ownership change by checking the pre-write lease's identity (read-ownerPid + mtime) immediately before the rename and re-reading after, returning false if the file it would overwrite is not provably still its own previous content. Equivalently, write through writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive semantics (O_EXCL on a fresh file is wrong for refresh, so use read-mine → write-temp → rename → re-read-mine, aborting if the pre-write snapshot no longer matches).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:35:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:refreshOwnerLeaseFile L517-532 and writeOwnerLeaseFile L344-349",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:36:      "why": "The current re-read at L528 cannot detect self-clobber of a reclaimed lease; this is the most likely path by which the winner's lease silently migrates back to the dying owner."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:39:      "change": "Add a socket-liveness tiebreaker to the unknown-eperm path: when classifyOwnerLease returns 'unknown-eperm' AND the recorded socketPath refuses connection (ECONNREFUSED) AND heartbeat is stale, reclassify as reclaimable. reapOwnerBeforeRespawn already runs under a confirmed-dead-socket gate in respawnAfterDeadSocket; surface the same gate into acquireOwnerLeaseFile for the cold-start reclaim.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:40:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease L455-472 (accept optional socketProbe result) and acquireOwnerLeaseFile L478-484",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:41:      "why": "Restores self-healing for sandboxed/EPERM owners; the incident's wedge would have recovered instead of requiring manual PID kill + wal_checkpoint."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:44:      "change": "Embed a per-launcher random nonce in the lease and have the daemon echo it on its IPC socket; reclaim/reap paths probe the socket for the nonce before signaling. This is the strongest PID-reuse defense and subsumes the cmdline check for the daemon-controlled socket.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:45:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:buildOwnerLease L410-421 (add nonce) + reapOwnerBeforeRespawn L640 (probe via bridge module) + launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs getIpcSocketPath consumer",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:49:      "change": "Stop treating ppid==1 as sufficient for 'ppid-1-orphan': require the cmdline/start-time identity check (rec. 2) AND a stale heartbeat before reclaiming a ppid-1 process; in containers where ppid==1 is the norm, rely on heartbeat+socket staleness alone.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:50:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease L460-463",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:54:      "change": "On reclaim of a stale owner, before spawning the successor, probe for and reap the orphan's CHILD daemon (the recorded ownerLeasePid post-refreshOwnerLeaseFile in launchServer L974-983) — not just the launcher pid. The current reclaimedOrphanPid path reaps lease.ownerPid which may be the launcher; the actual DB writer is the child recorded in the refreshed lease.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:55:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain L1058-1067 (reclaimedOrphanPid selection) and reapOwnerBeforeRespawn L640",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:56:      "why": "The incident's 17MB orphaned WAL was held by the CHILD daemon, not the launcher; reaping only the launcher pid leaves the DB writer alive, producing the two-writer -32000."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:59:      "change": "After any successful reap and before launchServer, run an explicit wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on the DB (or defer to the daemon's startup but gate on WAL size) so a long-lived orphan WAL doesn't leave the successor opening a 17MB WAL under a possibly-stale shm.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:60:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain after reapOwnerBeforeRespawn success L1174-1180, before writeLeaseFile L1182",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:66:    "The uid-check-primary-lease change can break a legitimate same-uid-but-different-runtime scenario (e.g., a launcher spawned by a different agent runtime under the same user). The check should reject only DIFFERENT uids, not same-uid, and should never block the owner's own refresh path.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:67:    "The non-destructive CAS (generation pointer) adds filesystem-op count per reclaim; on a slow NFS-style volume this could push cold-start latency. Keep the fast-path (no existing lease) on the original O_EXCL and only pay the generation dance on the reclaim branch.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:68:    "cmdline-based PID identity is bypassable by a malicious same-uid process that execs a matching argv; the nonce-attestation recommendation is the real boundary, but it requires the daemon to implement a nonce-echo RPC, which is a daemon-side change, not launcher-only.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:69:    "wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) from the launcher requires opening the SQLite DB from a second process before the daemon owns it — if the orphan isn't fully dead, this is a second writer and can itself corrupt. The reap must be confirmed-dead (waitForPidExit) before any checkpoint; consider deferring checkpoint to the daemon's first startup hook instead.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-008.md:70:    "Lifting uid checks to the primary lease may surface false positives on macOS where getuid() under sudo/seatbelt differs from the file's recorded uid; the guard should fall open (treat as own) when process.getuid is unavailable (Windows) or when the stat fails with EACCES rather than ENOENT."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-009.md:5:{"angle":"mcp-client-resilience","insights":["`processLiveness` (L423-433) only proves the PID exists — a wedged daemon (alive, 0% CPU, socket never created) returns 'alive', so `classifyOwnerLease` (L455-472) treats it as `live-owner` and the bridge short-circuits to held-by-other, never triggering reap.","The incident's lease-gone state bypasses ALL self-heal: with no owner lease, `acquireOwnerLeaseFile` (L474) builds a fresh lease (no `existing` to reclaim), `isLeaseHeld` (L612) sees no PID lease, so the launcher spawns a SECOND daemon — but the orphan still holds the SQLite WAL writer lock → silent WAL contention, no structured reason.","`respawnAfterDeadSocket` (L668-717) is gated on `respawnChildPid` being a recorded integer (L674-679); when the owner lease vanished, it bails at L676 with `dead-socket-no-child-pid` and self-heal does NOT fire. This is the exact hole the incident fell through.","The 2-min `stale-heartbeat-reclaim` window (L467, ttlMs*2=120s) only triggers when the OWNER LEASE file exists AND its heartbeat is stale. A daemon that wedged before/while writing its lease (or whose lease was deleted) is invisible to this path.","`-32000` is a generic JSON-RPC error surfaced to the runtime; the launcher's rich stderr diagnostics (`ownerLeaseReclaimed`, `dead-socket-no-child-pid`, `confirmed-dead socket`) are free-form prose, not machine-parseable, so the next incident still needs manual lsof/ps.","WAL holder ≠ lease holder: `leaseHeldFromFile` (L582) and `formerLocationOwnerLive` (L577) check PID/owner leases but never probe whether a live unleased process holds the `-wal` file. The orphaned 17MB WAL had no lease holder yet kept the writer lock."],"recommendations":[{"change":"Emit a single structured diagnostic line `LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC: reason=<token> ownerPid=<n> socketPath=<p> walHeldBy=<pid|none>` on EVERY non-zero/failure exit path. Tokens: dead-socket-reclaimed, dead-socket-no-child-pid, respawn-superseded, respawn-lock-held, owner-liveness-unknown-eperm, startup-timeout, foreign-owner, wal-locked-by-orphan, stale-heartbeat-reclaimed, bridge-socket-refused, bridge-module-missing. This gives the runtime one grep-able line per incident instead of prose forensics.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain L1039-1199 (catch/finally + every early `return`/`process.exit` in bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld L719, respawnAfterDeadSocket L668, acquireOwnerLeaseFile L474)","why":"The runtime currently swallows stderr into a generic -32000. A stable `reason=` token lets the MCP client surface 'wal-locked-by-orphan' or 'dead-socket-reclaimed' directly to the operator, eliminating manual ps/lsof."},{"change":"Add a pre-spawn WAL-holder probe in `launcherMain` right before `launchServer()` (L1189): if `code-graph.sqlite-wal` exists and is large (>1MB) AND no live lease claims ownership, run `lsof -t <wal>` (macOS) / scan `/proc/*/fd` (Linux) for the holding PID; if that PID is alive, unleased, and unreachable on `ownerSocketPath()`, reap it once via the existing `reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` (L640) before spawning. Gate behind a one-shot `.self-heal-attempted` marker file written atomically with the launcher pid+timestamp so a failed heal never loops into a kill storm.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain ~L1182-1189 (after writeLeaseFile, before launchServer); reuse reapOwnerBeforeRespawn L640-666","why":"Closes the exact incident: lease gone + WAL held by orphan. The current code only reaps PIDs it can attribute via a lease; an orphan that lost its lease but kept the WAL writer lock is invisible and produces a silent second-writer wedge."},{"change":"Augment `classifyOwnerLease` (L455) so a live ownerPid whose recorded `socketPath` fails a non-blocking connect (ECONNREFUSED/ENOENT) AND whose heartbeat is past ttlMs is classified as `dead-socket` (reclaimable) rather than waiting the full ttlMs*2 `stale-heartbeat-reclaim`. Add an `isSocketReachable(socketPath)` helper (connect+close, 50ms timeout) and call it after the ppid check at L463, before the heartbeat TTL check at L465.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease L455-472; new helper near ownerSocketPath L371","why":"A live-but-wedged daemon (PID alive, socket gone) is the incident signature. PID liveness alone is insufficient; socket unreachability + stale heartbeat is a deterministic reclaim signal that fires in seconds, not 2 minutes."},{"change":"In `respawnAfterDeadSocket`, replace the hard bail at L676-679 with a fallback: when `respawnChildPid` is missing, re-read the owner lease; if still missing, probe the WAL holder PID (from recommendation #2) and use THAT as the reap target. Only fall through to the current `dead-socket-no-child-pid` report if the WAL probe also finds nothing.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:respawnAfterDeadSocket L674-679","why":"Today a dead socket with no recorded child pid is inert — the exact condition that forced manual recovery. Falling back to WAL-holder attribution makes self-heal fire even when the lease vanished."},{"change":"Have `bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld` (L719) return the structured `reason` token upstream so `launcherMain`'s early `return` at L1082 emits a `LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC` line to stdout (not just stderr) right before exit. Stdout is what the MCP transport reads for handshake diagnostics; stderr is often lost on -32000.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld L719-754 return path; launcherMain L1075-1082","why":"The runtime sees stdout protocol lines; the diagnostic must ride the same channel the runtime actually inspects post-reconnect, or -32000 stays opaque."}],"risks":["WAL-holder probing via lsof/proc is platform-dependent and racy — a process can release the lock between probe and reap; mitigate with the one-shot marker + a max-reap-count of 1 per launcher invocation.","Socket-reachability checks add a 50ms connect per classify; on hot paths (every secondary connect) this compounds — gate behind 'heartbeat near/over ttl' so it only runs for suspicious owners, not healthy ones.","Reaping a WAL holder that is ACTUALLY healthy but slow to write its lease (race during legitimate startup) could kill a valid daemon — the `.self-heal-attempted` marker plus requiring BOTH stale heartbeat AND unreachable socket minimizes false positives, but a startup-timeout guard (e.g. skip heal if owner startedAt < 10s ago) is needed.","Stdout diagnostic lines could collide with MCP JSON-RPC framing if emitted mid-protocol — must only emit on the failure/exit path, never while stdio is being bridged through the session proxy (L207-217).","Foreign-owner detection (uid check at L591) doesn't cover a same-user orphan from a crashed previous session; the WAL probe must cross-check the holding PID's start time against the recorded startedAtIso to avoid reaping a legitimate successor."]}
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:3:**Subject:** Prevent recurrence of the Code Graph daemon wedge — a PID-alive-but-socket-dead daemon the launcher does not reclaim, causing MCP reconnect `-32000`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:5:**Target file:** `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` (+ `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` `initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:9:Liveness is **bidimensional** — `processLiveness` proves only `process.kill(pid,0)` (PID exists), and the heartbeat is written by the **launcher**, not gated on the daemon's ability to serve. There is **zero socket-health correlation**. So a wedged daemon (PID alive, 0% CPU, socket never (re)created) classifies as `live-owner` / `held:true`. The inert branch is `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder`'s no-socket path (bridge `L405-408`): when the lease says "held" but the socket FILE is missing, it `report`s instead of respawning. Two compounding axes in the incident: (a) the lease file had also vanished from an empty socket dir, so the heartbeat-reclaim (which needs a lease file with an aging `lastHeartbeatIso`) never had anything to age out; (b) a 17 MB orphaned WAL from the unclean crash.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:15:1. **Reuse the existing probe.** `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:probeDaemon({deepProbe:true})` (~L150-236) already connects the UDS + sends a JSON-RPC `initialize`. Wrap it as `probeExistingService(socketPath)` returning `{status, kind}` where `kind ∈ {enoent, econnrefused, timeout, json-rpc-reply}`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:16:2. **Socket-health gate in classification.** Add a `live-but-dead-socket` state to `classifyOwnerLease` and mirror it in `leaseHeldFromFile` (after the `process.kill(pid,0)` success at `~L602`): if `socketPath` is missing/dead (fast-path `!existsSync`, else N-consecutive deep-probe failures), return `staleReclaimable:true` instead of `held:true`. Extend the reclaimable allowlist (`~L479-484`) — the existing `unlink + O_EXCL` CAS handles it atomically, no CAS change.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:17:3. **No-bridge-socket → respawn, not report.** Change bridge `L405-408` to route a socketless-but-live owner into the existing `respawnAfterDeadSocket → reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` pipeline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:18:4. **Startup grace window** (so a *starting* daemon isn't killed): `STARTUP_GRACE_MS=30000`, `MAX_INIT_MS=120000` (env-overridable), keyed on a new `childSpawnedAtIso` stamped right after the child PID is known — NOT `startedAtIso` (which precedes the bootstrap-lock wait + first build). `age ≤ grace` → still-starting (report); `grace < age ≤ max` → bounded socket re-poll; `age > max` → respawn (`startup-deadline-exceeded`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:19:5. **Socket-gated heartbeat.** Refresh `lastHeartbeatIso` only when the socket is actually serving; on probe failure, stop heartbeating so a dead-socket daemon ages out into `stale-heartbeat-reclaim` within 2 min. Ideally move heartbeat ownership into the daemon child (refresh only after its IPC listener echoes).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:20:6. **Crash-surviving PID registry + self-heal on acquire.** Write `.code-graph-daemon-pid.json` (`{daemonPid, launcherPid, socketPath, startedAt, canonicalDbDir}`) right after the child PID is known; unlink only on clean child exit. On acquire, discover the orphan via `reclaimedOrphanPid → that registry → lsof on the WAL`, and reap (SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL) **only** when `alive AND socket provably dead (N consecutive failures)**, then checkpoint, then spawn.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:21:7. **WAL startup hygiene.** Before spawn: if `code-graph.sqlite-wal` exceeds a threshold (~8 MB) or an orphan was just reaped, run `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` (no other writer holds it). Set `wal_autocheckpoint=500` (~2 MB, tunable) in `initDb`. Checkpoint before the migration copy so a giant `-wal` is never propagated.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:22:8. **Race + permission safety.** Apply the uid check to the PRIMARY lease (not just `legacyPath`) and to the socket owner — never unlink/bridge/kill a foreign-owned lease. Before any SIGKILL, verify PID identity (cmdline basename + process start-time vs `startedAtIso`) to avoid killing a PID-reuse victim; on mismatch log `pid-reuse-suspected` and abort. Re-stat the lease between classify and unlink (abort if changed). Gate self-heal behind a one-shot `.self-heal-attempted` marker so a failed heal never loops into a kill storm.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:23:9. **One-line diagnostics.** Emit `LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC: reason=<token> ownerPid=<n> socketPath=<p> walHeldBy=<pid|none>` on every failure exit. Tokens: `dead-socket-reclaimed`, `startup-timeout`, `foreign-owner`, `wal-locked-by-orphan`, `bridge-socket-refused`, `pid-reuse-suspected`, `stale-heartbeat-reclaimed`. Replaces manual lsof/ps forensics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:27:- **Wedge → reclaim:** spawn a fake owner that writes the lease + holds a live PID but never opens the socket; past `MAX_INIT_MS`, assert the launcher reclaims (reaps + respawns) and emits `dead-socket-reclaimed`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:29:- **Foreign-owner → not killed:** lease/socket owned by a different uid → assert no signal, `foreign-owner` diagnostic.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:30:- **PID-reuse → not killed:** live PID whose cmdline/start-time mismatch the lease → `pid-reuse-suspected`, abort.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:31:- **Oversized WAL → checkpointed:** seed an 8MB+ `-wal` with no holder → assert `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` runs pre-spawn and WAL shrinks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:32:- **Healthy owner → bridged:** live PID + serving socket → assert bridge, no reclaim.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:36:Implement components **1-3 + 4 + 7** as the P0 core (they directly close the incident class), then **6, 8, 9** as the durability/safety layer, then **5** (heartbeat) as defense-in-depth. The minimal P0 (socket-health gate + reclaim-on-dead-socket past a grace window + startup WAL hygiene + diagnostics) collapses both failure axes from the incident.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:43:- Launcher under study: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, and the child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` (`initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md:44:- source: live incident forensics, 2026-06-29 — orphaned PIDs 37269/37358 at 0% CPU, empty `/tmp/mk-code-index/`, a 17 MB `code-graph.sqlite-wal` with no holder, DB integrity ok; manual recovery via `kill` + `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` + socket-dir clean.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:5:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:6:{"angle":"self-heal-on-acquire","insights":["The orphan escaped because `acquireOwnerLeaseFile` (L474-515) only sets `reclaimed` when a STALE lease EXISTS to unlink+reclaim; in this incident the lease file was already GONE, so `existing` is null, `reclaimed` is undefined, and the L1061-1067 guard never records `reclaimedOrphanPid` → the L1174-1180 reap block is skipped → the wedged daemon survives and `launchServer` opens a SECOND writer against the same DB+WAL.","`reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` (L640-666) already implements the correct kill ladder (SIGTERM → RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS 7s → SIGKILL), ESRCH-safe, and refuses on `unknown-eperm`; it is reusable as-is — the gap is that no pid reaches it when the lease is missing.","The orphan pid can also diverge from the lease's `ownerPid` when `refreshOwnerLeaseFile` to the child pid FAILS (L974-981: 'launcher pid remains the recorded owner'); reaping `ownerPid` then SIGKILLs the launcher while the reparented daemon child (ppid→1) survives holding the WAL. So the owner lease is NOT a reliable orphan handle.","`acquireBootstrapLock` (L921) with `requireLock:true` is ALREADY the correct critical section — mkdir-CAS plus `removeStaleBootstrapLock` (L890) dead-owner reclaim via pid stamp. The self-heal must run INSIDE this lock exactly as `respawnAfterDeadSocket` does (L683); no new lock is needed and the CAS stays single-writer.","`wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` CANNOT succeed while the orphan holds the WAL file lock, so it MUST run AFTER `waitForPidExit` confirms dead (L631-638) and BEFORE `launchServer` opens the child DB — otherwise the successor inherits the 17MB WAL or collides on the file lock.","The stale `daemon-ipc.sock` (SOCKET_FILE_NAME, bridge L13) must be unlinked before spawn or the new child hits EADDRINUSE; `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder` only checks existence (bridge L405) and never unlinks on the respawn path."],"recommendations":[{"change":"Add a pre-spawn self-heal gate `reapOrphanHolderIfAny({dbDir,socketPath})` invoked in `launcherMain` right after `acquireBootstrapLock({requireLock:true})` succeeds, REPLACING the narrow L1174-1180 block (which only fires on a reclaimed stale lease). Discover the orphan pid via three descending sources: (a) reclaimedOrphanPid when present, (b) a new crash-surviving daemon-pid registry (see next rec), (c) `lsof`/`fuser -t` on `code-graph.sqlite-wal`. Reap ONLY when `processLiveness(pid)==='alive'` AND the recorded socket is provably dead (reuse `probeLeaseHolderWithRetries` with the N-consecutive-failure gate from bridge L417, NOT a single miss). Hand the pid to the existing `reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` for the kill ladder.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain ~L1102-1189","why":"Closes the 'lease gone, orphan alive' hole: today the winner spawns a second writer because it has no pid to reap. The gate runs inside the held bootstrap lock so only the single CAS winner reaps; a losing racer's mkdir fails and it bridges/reports as today."},{"change":"Write a crash-surviving `.code-graph-daemon-pid.json` in `launchServer` immediately after `childProcess.pid` is known (after L973), recording {daemonPid, launcherPid, socketPath, startedAt, canonicalDbDir}. Do NOT clear it in `clearAllLeaseFiles`; unlink it ONLY in the child 'exit' handler (L985) on a clean exit. This file is the durable orphan handle that survives SIGKILL (no handlers run) and owner-lease loss.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launchServer L973-983 and child exit handler L985-1001","why":"The owner lease is unreliable after a failed child-pid refresh (L979) or once a secondary reclaims+clears it; a separate registry that survives unclean death is the only way the self-heal can recover a pid when both leases are absent — exactly this incident."},{"change":"After `reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` reports the orphan dead, run `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`: best-effort `spawnSync('sqlite3', [dbPath, 'PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);'])` from the launcher; if the `sqlite3` binary is absent, defer to a one-shot `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` executed in the daemon's boot path (it already owns better-sqlite3). Place AFTER `waitForPidExit` confirms dead and BEFORE `writeLeaseFile`/`launchServer`.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:new gate between L1180 and L1182; plus daemon boot in mcp_server","why":"A 17MB orphaned WAL left in place slows the successor's open and risks WAL-recovery races; checkpointing after the lock is released (dead holder) and before the successor opens collapses it to the main db. Idempotent: TRUNCATE is a no-op once already checkpointed."},{"change":"Clean the stale socket artifact before spawn: `fs.rmSync(path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME), {force:true})` (and any `.sock.lock`), then `fs.mkdirSync(socketDir, {recursive:true, mode:0o700})`. Use the lease-recorded socketPath when available, else `getIpcSocketPath('mk-code-index', {dbDir})` (bridge L85). Place AFTER checkpoint, BEFORE launchServer.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:new gate, after checkpoint step","why":"A leftover .sock file makes the new child's bind fail EADDRINUSE; force:true unlink + recursive mkdir is idempotent and safe to repeat across retries."},{"change":"Enforce the strict ordering, all under the held bootstrap lock (requireLock:true): (1) discover orphan pid [registry→lsof], (2) verify pid alive AND socket dead via N-consecutive deep-probe failures, (3) SIGTERM→7s→SIGKILL via `reapOwnerBeforeRespawn`, (4) `waitForPidExit` re-confirms dead, (5) `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`, (6) unlink stale socket + guarded lease clears (`clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner` L552 / clearLeaseFile ownership re-check L766), (7) `writeLeaseFile`, (8) `launchServer`. Mirror this same sequence in `respawnAfterDeadSocket` (L668-717) so the secondary-driven respawn path also checkpoints + cleans the socket instead of only reaping.","where":"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:launcherMain winner path + respawnAfterDeadSocket L668-717","why":"Reap-before-checkpoint is mandatory (WAL lock held by live process); checkpoint-before-spawn prevents the successor inheriting the WAL; cleaning the socket before spawn prevents EADDRINUSE. Keeping both respawn paths symmetric stops the secondary path from leaving the same WAL/socket residue."}],"risks":["`lsof`/`fuser` may be absent or path-different across macOS/Linux; never block on it — bound with a timeout and fall back to the daemon-pid registry. Treat lsof as a third-line signal, not the primary handle.","PID reuse: the OS may recycle the recorded pid for an unrelated process. Mitigate by recording `startedAt`/process start-time and cross-checking `ps -o lstart` (or /proc/$pid/stat boot-time field) before killing; refuse the kill on mismatch and let the new daemon's SQLite recover the WAL instead.","A busy daemon mid large scan/FTS-merge can transiently miss a deep probe; the socket-dead decision MUST reuse `resolveLeaseProbeAttempts` (bridge L52, default 2 consecutive failures) — a single miss must NOT trigger a reap.","Launcher-side `wal_checkpoint` opens a second sqlite connection; if the reap race left the orphan not fully dead, checkpoint collides. Guard: only checkpoint after `processLiveness(pid)==='dead'` is re-confirmed post-SIGKILL (waitForPidExit returned true), and prefer the daemon-boot checkpoint as the reliable fallback.","The new `.code-graph-daemon-pid.json` is another state file to migrate/clean; add it to the legacy-path probe set and the migration `dbFiles` COPYFILE_EXCL list (L1125) only if it should follow the DB, else keep it strictly skill-local and let each DB dir own its own."]}
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:4:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:5:[0m→ [0mRead .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:8:  "angle": "reclaim-on-dead-socket",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:10:    "The liveness model is bidimensional (PID-alive + heartbeat-fresh) but must be tridimensional: PID-alive AND socket-serving AND heartbeat-fresh. Socket health is currently gated ONLY in the secondary bridge path (maybeBridgeLeaseHolder L418-429 via probeLeaseHolderWithRetries deepProbe), NOT in the primary owner-acquire path (classifyOwnerLease L455-472, leaseHeldFromFile L582-610). That asymmetry is the root defect: a primary launcher that starts fresh never probes the prior owner's socket — it trusts process.kill(pid,0) at L426/L602 and declares 'held'/'live-owner' even when the UDS was never recreated.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:11:    "The incident is WORSE than the dead-socket-with-lease case: the lease FILE was gone, so classifyOwnerLease and leaseHeldFromFile have nothing to read (existing===null at L478, lease===null at L583). There is no ownerPid to probe. The current reclaim predicate is purely lease-file-driven; a live orphan holding the SQLite writer lock but missing its lease file is structurally invisible to acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L474-515) and isLeaseHeld (L612-623). Socket-gating classifyOwnerLease alone will NOT catch this — it needs an orphan-discovery channel independent of the lease file.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:12:    "classifyOwnerLease returns 'live-owner' on a bare PID-0 signal success (L423-432 processLiveness only does kill -0). A wedged daemon (event loop blocked, 0% CPU, socket absent) passes this check trivially. The 2-min stale-heartbeat-reclaim at L467-469 is the ONLY backstop, and it fires 2 min late — during which the WAL grew to 17MB and /mcp reconnect hit -32000.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:13:    "The dead-socket respawn machinery already exists and is correct: maybeBridgeLeaseHolder L426-428 returns {action:'respawn'}, respawnAfterDeadSocket L668 reaps (reapOwnerBeforeRespawn L640 SIGTERM→SIGKILL), rebuilds the lease via writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive (CAS), and relaunches. The problem is purely that this path is gated behind isLeaseHeld returning held:true with a live ownerPid — which the primary acquire path short-circuits before reaching.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:14:    "The CAS at acquireOwnerLeaseFile L491-505 (unlink stale → O_EXCL create) is already reclaim-safe and race-correct; it does NOT need restructuring. It only needs its precondition (the classification at L479-484) to treat 'live-but-dead-socket' as reclaimable rather than as 'live-owner'.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:15:    "leaseHeldFromFile L601-609 has the same defect: process.kill(lease.pid,0) success → held:true with no socket verification. A secondary launcher reading the PID lease (not the owner lease) will declare held and bridge to a socket that doesn't exist — then maybeBridgeLeaseHolder L405-408 catches the missing socket and returns 'no-bridge-socket' (report, not respawn). So the PID-lease path never self-heals; it only reports."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:19:      "change": "Add a socket-health gate to classifyOwnerLease before returning 'live-owner'. Introduce states: 'dead' (ESRCH, current stale-pid), 'live-and-serving' (PID alive + socketPath exists + deepProbe alive → keep as 'live-owner' semantics), 'live-but-dead-socket' (PID alive but socket missing OR deepProbe fails N times → RECLAIMABLE), 'unknown-eperm' (unchanged, conservative hold). When PID is alive, call a bounded probeLeaseHolderWithRetries(lease.socketPath, {deepProbe:true}) and branch: alive→live-and-serving, dead→live-but-dead-socket. Skip the probe (fast-path dead-socket) when lease.socketPath is null/empty OR !fs.existsSync(socketPath) AND no tcp://.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:20:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease:455-472 (insert probe between L456 liveness check and L471 return 'live-owner'; reuse probeLeaseHolderWithRetries from launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:340)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:21:      "why": "This is the single highest-leverage fix: it makes the PRIMARY owner-acquire path socket-aware, closing the asymmetry where only the secondary bridge path probes. A wedged PID with a missing socket becomes reclaimable immediately instead of waiting the 2-min heartbeat TTL."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:24:      "change": "Make acquireOwnerLeaseFile treat 'live-but-dead-socket' as reclaimable, same as 'stale-pid'/'stale-heartbeat-reclaim'/'ppid-1-orphan'. Only 'live-and-serving' and 'unknown-eperm' return acquired:false. The existing unlink+O_EXCL CAS (L491-505) already handles the reclaim atomically — no CAS change needed, just extend the classification allowlist at L479-484.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:25:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:acquireOwnerLeaseFile:479-484",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:26:      "why": "The CAS is correct; only the gate predicate is too permissive. After the socket gate, a 'respawn' here also needs the reap: acquireOwnerLeaseFile currently does NOT reap the orphan PID it reclaimed (unlike launcherMain L1174-1180 which reaps reclaimedOrphanPid). Wire the reclaimed live-but-dead-socket ownerPid into reclaimedOrphanPid so reapOwnerBeforeRespawn kills it before launchServer opens a second SQLite writer."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:29:      "change": "Mirror the socket gate into leaseHeldFromFile: after process.kill(lease.pid,0) succeeds at L602, before returning held:true, verify lease.socketPath exists and (optionally, for the high-stakes case) deep-probe. If socket missing/dead, return {held:false, staleReclaimable:true, reason:'live-but-dead-socket'} so isLeaseHeld→launcherMain L1091-1097 reclaims and the bridgeOrReport path is bypassed. This prevents the PID-lease path from declaring 'held' and then reporting no-bridge-socket (the L405-408 inert branch).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:30:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:leaseHeldFromFile:601-609",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:31:      "why": "Currently a dead socket on the PID-lease path only ever reports, never respawns. Surfacing staleReclaimable routes it through the existing staleReclaimed metric + fresh-acquire at L1091-1097, unifying both lease kinds under one reclaim semantics."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:34:      "change": "Add lease-less orphan discovery for the incident's hardest case (lease file GONE, live PID still holds DB). On the fresh-acquire path (existing===null at L478), before acquiring, scan for a live orphan: parse the readiness file (.code-graph-readiness.json — note it's already referenced in the migration file list at L1129) and/or probe the SQLite DB's WAL holder. Simplest durable option: have the child daemon write its own pid into .code-graph-daemon.pid on socket bind, distinct from the launcher-managed lease; acquireOwnerLeaseFile checks this file when existing===null and, if the recorded pid is live-but-dead-socket, routes it through reapOwnerBeforeRespawn before writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive. This decouples orphan detection from the launcher's own lease lifecycle.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:35:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:acquireOwnerLeaseFile:478-486 (insert orphan probe when existing===null) + system-code-graph MCP server socket-bind site (write .code-graph-daemon.pid)",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:36:      "why": "Socket-gating classifyOwnerLease only helps when the lease file survives. The incident had no lease file at all, so the current code spawns a second daemon against an orphan-held DB. A pid stamp written by the DAEMON at bind time (not the launcher) survives launcher crashes and gives acquireOwnerLeaseFile a probe target."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:39:      "change": "Tighten the heartbeat-TTL backstop from 2x→1.5x at L467 (Date.now()-heartbeatMs > ttlMs*2) and ensure refreshOwnerLeaseFile (L517, called from startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat L399-406 at ttlMs/2) is the only freshness source. The 2x multiplier at L467 means classifyOwnerLease tolerates up to 120s of missed heartbeats before reclaim — that window is exactly when the WAL ballooned. Pair this with the socket gate so the socket check is primary and the heartbeat is a coarse fallback, not the sole signal.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:40:      "where": "mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:classifyOwnerLease:465-469 + startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat:394-408",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:41:      "why": "The socket probe is a precise, sub-second dead-socket signal; the heartbeat is a coarse liveness proxy. Making the socket gate authoritative and demoting heartbeat to a fallback closes the multi-minute wedge window without risking false-reclaims on a briefly-busy daemon (the N-retry deep probe already handles transient load)."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:45:    "Deep-probing inside classifyOwnerLease adds latency (worst case N×timeoutMs, default 2×5s=10s) to EVERY owner-acquire on startup. Mitigate with the fast-path: if !fs.existsSync(lease.socketPath) AND non-tcp, return live-but-dead-socket immediately without probing (a missing socket file is already proof). Reserve the deep probe for the socket-file-exists-but-unresponsive case. The current DEFAULT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 is for the secondary bridge; the primary path should use a shorter timeout (e.g. 2000ms) since a local UDS deep-probe round-trips in <10ms when healthy.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:46:    "False-reclaim of a genuinely busy daemon mid-FTS-merge: the existing probeLeaseHolderWithRetries retry/backoff (L340-363, default 1 retry + 250ms backoff) was tuned for the secondary path. Reusing it on the primary path means a daemon stalled >~12s on a long FTS write could be reaped while still making progress. Mitigation: increase SPECKIT_LEASE_PROBE_RETRIES default to 2 for the primary-acquire probe and/or gate the reap behind the heartbeat (only reap-on-dead-socket if heartbeat is ALSO stale, so a freshly-heartbeating busy daemon is not reaped for a transient probe miss).",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:47:    "Adding a .code-graph-daemon.pid written by the child at bind time creates a new trust/TOCTOU surface: a stale pid file from a crashed daemon could point at an UNRELATED reused PID (PID recycling). Must validate executablePath/ppid like readOwnerLeaseFile L327-336 does, not just liveness. Also must be cleaned on graceful shutdown or it becomes a new wedge source itself.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:48:    "Race between the socket-gated reclaim and startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat: if a new owner is mid-heartbeat-refresh when a secondary reclaims on dead-socket, refreshOwnerLeaseFile L528-529 re-reads and returns false (good), but the reclaimed owner's child may still be draining a request. reapOwnerBeforeRespawn's 7s SIGTERM grace (RESPAWN_REAP_GRACE_MS L121) must exceed the daemon's in-flight request drain window or a second writer opens against the not-yet-released WAL.",
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-003.md:49:    "The socket gate assumes lease.socketPath is populated; buildOwnerLease L419 writes ownerSocketPath() which can return null (ownerSocketPath L371-379 swallows errors). A lease with socketPath:null would make the fast-path treat every live owner as dead-socket. Must treat null socketPath on an otherwise-live lease as 'unknown' (conservative hold / fall back to connect-liveness), not as dead — otherwise a transient getIpcSocketPath failure causes a reclaim storm."
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:16:You are GPT-5.5 at xhigh reasoning doing an ADVERSARIAL verification pass over a hardening design produced by a different model (GLM-5.2). Be skeptical: your job is to CONFIRM what is right, REFUTE what is wrong, and find GAPS the prior research missed — not to agree. Read the synthesized design at .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/research.md and the actual source .opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs and .opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs (and the child initDb under system-code-graph/mcp_server if useful). Ground every claim in the REAL code (cite function/line). Incident recap: the code-index daemon wedged (PID alive, IPC socket never created, lease file gone, 17MB orphaned WAL) and /mcp reconnect returned -32000.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:19:Find failure modes GLM's 10 angles did NOT cover. Probe at least: the TCP-socket path (tcp:// vs UDS — does the socket-existence fast-path break for tcp?); cross-platform PID/socket discovery (lsof/fuser/ps/proc differ on macOS vs linux — does the design assume /proc?); the cold first-build (npm/tsc via buildIfNeeded) consuming the grace window before the child exists; two MCP clients reconnecting concurrently; a partially-written or corrupt lease/pid-registry file; the migration-copy path propagating an oversized -wal; signal handling if the child ignores SIGTERM. Report each as a gap with a concrete mitigation.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:32:Reading the hardening design and the launcher code first. This is read-only, so I’m treating it as a review pass: evidence from source lines, then JSON only in the final.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:578:One requested file path doesn’t exist exactly as written: `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs` is missing. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:592:const { spawn, spawnSync } = require('child_process');
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:593:const { createSessionProxy, createClassifyFrame } = require('./lib/launcher-session-proxy.cjs');
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:601:// .env, both are gitignored. Existing process.env wins over file values (do not override).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:621:      process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] env value for ${key} contains control chars; skipping\n`);
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:625:      process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] env ${key} from ${path.basename(filePath)} is not allowlisted; skipping\n`);
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:628:    if (!(key in process.env)) {

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:642:// Category -> INDEX_* env key. Module-level so the launcher and its tests share one source.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:666:      if (n > 0) process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] loaded ${n} env(s) from ${fname}\n`);
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:677:  const maintainerCategories = resolveMaintainerModeCategories(process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE);
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:679:    process.env[MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV[cat]] = 'true';

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:805:  lockDir = path.join(dbDir, '.mk-code-index-launcher.lockdir');
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:848:     3	**Subject:** Prevent recurrence of the Code Graph daemon wedge — a PID-alive-but-socket-dead daemon the launcher does not reclaim, causing MCP reconnect `-32000`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:850:     5	**Target file:** `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` (+ `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` `initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:854:     9	Liveness is **bidimensional** — `processLiveness` proves only `process.kill(pid,0)` (PID exists), and the heartbeat is written by the **launcher**, not gated on the daemon's ability to serve. There is **zero socket-health correlation**. So a wedged daemon (PID alive, 0% CPU, socket never (re)created) classifies as `live-owner` / `held:true`. The inert branch is `maybeBridgeLeaseHolder`'s no-socket path (bridge `L405-408`): when the lease says "held" but the socket FILE is missing, it `report`s instead of respawning. Two compounding axes in the incident: (a) the lease file had also vanished from an empty socket dir, so the heartbeat-reclaim (which needs a lease file with an aging `lastHeartbeatIso`) never had anything to age out; (b) a 17 MB orphaned WAL from the unclean crash.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:860:    15	1. **Reuse the existing probe.** `launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:probeDaemon({deepProbe:true})` (~L150-236) already connects the UDS + sends a JSON-RPC `initialize`. Wrap it as `probeExistingService(socketPath)` returning `{status, kind}` where `kind ∈ {enoent, econnrefused, timeout, json-rpc-reply}`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:861:    16	2. **Socket-health gate in classification.** Add a `live-but-dead-socket` state to `classifyOwnerLease` and mirror it in `leaseHeldFromFile` (after the `process.kill(pid,0)` success at `~L602`): if `socketPath` is missing/dead (fast-path `!existsSync`, else N-consecutive deep-probe failures), return `staleReclaimable:true` instead of `held:true`. Extend the reclaimable allowlist (`~L479-484`) — the existing `unlink + O_EXCL` CAS handles it atomically, no CAS change.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:862:    17	3. **No-bridge-socket → respawn, not report.** Change bridge `L405-408` to route a socketless-but-live owner into the existing `respawnAfterDeadSocket → reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` pipeline.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:863:    18	4. **Startup grace window** (so a *starting* daemon isn't killed): `STARTUP_GRACE_MS=30000`, `MAX_INIT_MS=120000` (env-overridable), keyed on a new `childSpawnedAtIso` stamped right after the child PID is known — NOT `startedAtIso` (which precedes the bootstrap-lock wait + first build). `age ≤ grace` → still-starting (report); `grace < age ≤ max` → bounded socket re-poll; `age > max` → respawn (`startup-deadline-exceeded`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:864:    19	5. **Socket-gated heartbeat.** Refresh `lastHeartbeatIso` only when the socket is actually serving; on probe failure, stop heartbeating so a dead-socket daemon ages out into `stale-heartbeat-reclaim` within 2 min. Ideally move heartbeat ownership into the daemon child (refresh only after its IPC listener echoes).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:865:    20	6. **Crash-surviving PID registry + self-heal on acquire.** Write `.code-graph-daemon-pid.json` (`{daemonPid, launcherPid, socketPath, startedAt, canonicalDbDir}`) right after the child PID is known; unlink only on clean child exit. On acquire, discover the orphan via `reclaimedOrphanPid → that registry → lsof on the WAL`, and reap (SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL) **only** when `alive AND socket provably dead (N consecutive failures)**, then checkpoint, then spawn.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:866:    21	7. **WAL startup hygiene.** Before spawn: if `code-graph.sqlite-wal` exceeds a threshold (~8 MB) or an orphan was just reaped, run `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` (no other writer holds it). Set `wal_autocheckpoint=500` (~2 MB, tunable) in `initDb`. Checkpoint before the migration copy so a giant `-wal` is never propagated.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:867:    22	8. **Race + permission safety.** Apply the uid check to the PRIMARY lease (not just `legacyPath`) and to the socket owner — never unlink/bridge/kill a foreign-owned lease. Before any SIGKILL, verify PID identity (cmdline basename + process start-time vs `startedAtIso`) to avoid killing a PID-reuse victim; on mismatch log `pid-reuse-suspected` and abort. Re-stat the lease between classify and unlink (abort if changed). Gate self-heal behind a one-shot `.self-heal-attempted` marker so a failed heal never loops into a kill storm.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:868:    23	9. **One-line diagnostics.** Emit `LAUNCHER_DIAGNOSTIC: reason=<token> ownerPid=<n> socketPath=<p> walHeldBy=<pid|none>` on every failure exit. Tokens: `dead-socket-reclaimed`, `startup-timeout`, `foreign-owner`, `wal-locked-by-orphan`, `bridge-socket-refused`, `pid-reuse-suspected`, `stale-heartbeat-reclaimed`. Replaces manual lsof/ps forensics.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:872:    27	- **Wedge → reclaim:** spawn a fake owner that writes the lease + holds a live PID but never opens the socket; past `MAX_INIT_MS`, assert the launcher reclaims (reaps + respawns) and emits `dead-socket-reclaimed`.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:874:    29	- **Foreign-owner → not killed:** lease/socket owned by a different uid → assert no signal, `foreign-owner` diagnostic.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:875:    30	- **PID-reuse → not killed:** live PID whose cmdline/start-time mismatch the lease → `pid-reuse-suspected`, abort.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:876:    31	- **Oversized WAL → checkpointed:** seed an 8MB+ `-wal` with no holder → assert `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` runs pre-spawn and WAL shrinks.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:877:    32	- **Healthy owner → bridged:** live PID + serving socket → assert bridge, no reclaim.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:881:    36	Implement components **1-3 + 4 + 7** as the P0 core (they directly close the incident class), then **6, 8, 9** as the durability/safety layer, then **5** (heartbeat) as defense-in-depth. The minimal P0 (socket-health gate + reclaim-on-dead-socket past a grace window + startup WAL hygiene + diagnostics) collapses both failure axes from the incident.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:888:    43	- Launcher under study: `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`, and the child `system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` (`initDb`).
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:889:    44	- source: live incident forensics, 2026-06-29 — orphaned PIDs 37269/37358 at 0% CPU, empty `/tmp/mk-code-index/`, a 17 MB `code-graph.sqlite-wal` with no holder, DB integrity ok; manual recovery via `kill` + `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` + socket-dir clean.

Modes: neighborhood (1-hop calls+imports), outline (file symbols), impact (reverse callers). When readiness requires a full scan, returns an explicit blocked payload with requiredAction `code_graph_scan`, readiness metadata, and lastPersistedAt instead of degraded graph answers. Successful responses include metadata.partialOutput for deadline/budget truncation details (reasons, omittedSections, omittedAnchors, truncatedText). Token Budget: 1200.',
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/028-memory-search-intelligence/002-code-graph/009-daemon-reclaim-hardening/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:4011:.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:160:      confirm: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Required for hard-stale recovery; for prune-excludes when any candidate pattern classifies medium-tier; and for every destructive operation (recover-sqlite-corruption, rollback-bad-apply) regardless of staleness. Low-tier prune-excludes additionally requires lowTierOptIn=true.' },
